En: Behind this building -- the other one. Once filled with engineers and designers of Feld Electric, now collapsed and dead, but for some rats... You feel drawn, for some reason, to the faded mural again.
Zh: 在这栋大厦之后——是另一栋大厦。曾经里面都是菲尔德电气的工程师和设计师，现在只剩断壁颓垣、白骨累累，不过对于某些老鼠来说……不知道为什么，你被一幅褪色的壁画吸引住了。

En: I'm dumb.
Zh: 我无话可说。

En: Fine. Okay.
Zh: 好吧。很好。

En: The scattering of bullet holes looks like one giant smiling mouth. Smiling its deadly smile.
Zh: 散布的弹孔看起来就像一张巨大的微笑的嘴。展露出致命的微笑。

En: The scattering of bullet holes looks like one giant smiling mouth. Smiling its deadly smile. Laughing at you and the world and the living.
Zh: 散布的弹孔看起来就像一张巨大的微笑的嘴。展露出致命的微笑。嘲笑着你，这个世界，还有活着的人们。

En: What *is* this...
Zh: 这*是*什么……

En: Maybe you should change it. They were praying... screaming...
Zh: 也许你应该改变它。他们在祈祷……尖叫……

En: Remember what Trant Heidelstam said about Feld.
Zh: 记起特兰特·海德斯塔姆口中菲尔德的故事。

En: Or maybe...
Zh: 或者也许……

En: You mentioned Coalition forces. Could it have been them against the wall?
Zh: “你刚说联盟军队。墙边的可能是他们吗？”

En: A scattering of bullet holes is spread across the cracked wall, reaching from one corner to the other.
Zh: 破裂的墙上布满了弹孔，从一个角落一直延伸到另一个角落。

En: Why this many bullet holes?
Zh: 为什么有这么多弹孔？

En: Bullet holes on the plaza.
Zh: 广场上的弹孔。

En: Bullet holes in the back yard.
Zh: 后院的弹孔。

En: Know about the revolution.
Zh: 了解大革命的事。

En: Know about Feld Electrical.
Zh: 了解菲尔德电气的事。

En: Walk away. [Leave.]
Zh: 走开。[离开。]

En: A row of ghostly shades stand facing the wall. There are many of them, a dozen at least, their heads lowered and eyes blindfolded. It's quiet. No sound, no movement.
Zh: 一排幽影面对墙站立着。有很多，至少十几个，他们的头部低垂，眼睛也被蒙上了。周围很安静。没有声音，没有动作。

En: Unable to piece together the *big picture* just now. There's a hole in the hypothesis.
Zh: 现在还没办法把*完整的画面*拼凑起来。假说里还存在一个漏洞。

En: Who were they?
Zh: 他们是谁？

En: Comrades. The forsaken, the wretched, who tried to rise against the horrors of the world.
Zh: 同伴们。那些被遗弃的可怜人，他们想要挺身而出，反抗世界的恐怖。

En: Foreign dissidents, unwashed criminals, and hoi polloi. Such is your belief, officer.
Zh: 外国异见分子，下层罪犯，还有普通民众。这就是你的信念，警官。

En: Leeches, fleas, and thieves. All the freeloader scum living off the hard work of others. Such is your belief.
Zh: 水蛭，跳蚤，还有小偷。这些吃白食的人渣靠着他人的辛勤劳动生活。这就是你的信念。

En: Collateral damage on the path toward normalcy and progress. Such is your belief, officer.
Zh: 通往常态和发展道路上的连带伤害。这就是你的信念，警官。

En: Ordinary people -- familiar, each and every one of them.
Zh: 普通民众——熟悉的，每一个人。

En: Hmm, correct. The lieutenant examines the wall closely. "The density of the bullet holes is unusual, even in a general *average bullet hole frequency in Martinaise* sense. Grim affairs."
Zh: “嗯，没错。”警督仔细检查着墙壁。“这些弹孔的密度很不寻常，就算以马丁内斯的*弹孔平均出现频率*来说，都有些夸张了。”

En: The density and spread of these bullet holes is surprising -- this takes *a lot* of bullets. Fully-automatic rifle fire.
Zh: 这些弹孔的密度和覆盖范围都很惊人——需要*大量*子弹才能达到这种效果。一定是全自动步枪的杰作。

En: Oh my god, look at all these bullet holes! I haven't seen any other bullet holes in the walls before, somehow.
Zh: “我的天，看看这些弹孔！不知道为什么，我之前从没在墙上见过其他弹孔。”

En: Look, Kim. Even more bullet holes. Something's definitely gone down here.
Zh: “看，金。还有更多弹孔。这里肯定发生过什么。”

En: Even more bullet holes. Something has definitely happened here.
Zh: 还有更多弹孔。这里肯定发生过什么。

En: Ten metres away -- other shades are lined up in an orderly manner, automatic rifles primed. A gust of wind blows by -- the coats of the firing squad flap slowly in the breeze. A single person stands on the side.
Zh: 10米外——其他阴影井井有条地排成一行，自动步枪蓄势待发。一阵狂风吹过——行刑队的衣角在风中慢慢飞舞。有一个身影站在边上。

En: A long time has passed since the moment of this fusillading. Rain and brine have since washed all the blood away. Not a trace remains.
Zh: 自从那次齐射以来，已经过去了很长时间。血液早已被大雨和海水冲洗干净。没有留下一丝痕迹。

En: Kim, who was who in this execution?
Zh: “金，谁参与了这次行刑？”

En: Who was who in this execution? I need to understand.
Zh: 谁参与了这次行刑？我需要搞清楚。

En: At first the lieutenant doesn't say a word... he just stares at the wall. "I don't know," he says finally. "I don't know who died here, lined up beside that horrible wall. It could have been any of the parties involved in the Revolution."
Zh: 一开始，警督一言不发……只是默默地凝视墙壁。“我不知道，”他终于开了口。“我不知道谁死在这里，在那堵可怕的墙边一字排开。跟大革命有关的任何党派都有可能。”

En: What about people from the Coalition? The so-called moralists?
Zh: “联盟的人呢？那些所谓的道德家呢？”

En: Yeah... it's very unlikely the Coalition forces were the ones who died here. They were always the *last* ones against the wall.
Zh: “是啊……死在这里的不太可能是联盟的人。他们总是*最后*靠在墙边的人。”

En: Seven men in combat uniforms and dark coats, holding automatic rifles aimed at the people. Soldiers from some side... but from which one?
Zh: 7个穿着战斗制服和黑大衣的男人，手握自动步枪，枪口对准普通民众。是某一方的士兵……但到底是哪一方的？

En: The abundance of bullet holes leads to two options: either an inordinate amount of executions were performed here, or they did not use a *conscience round* -- where only one soldier has the loaded rifle. Looks like this was a mass execution with everyone fully armed.
Zh: 这么多弹孔只会有两种可能：要么是在这里执行过大量死刑，要么就是他们没用*道德子弹*——只有一个士兵拿着上膛的步枪。看来这是一场大规模的处决，每个人都是全副武装。

En: I'm just a representation of your mental faculties... piecing together any available information. You should probably ask the lieutenant.
Zh: 我只是你心智能力的表现……把所有可用的信息拼凑在一起。也许你应该问问警督。

En: To be honest, if a Coalition member was anyone in this situation -- it was the commandant. The superior giving the orders.
Zh: “说真的，如果说有联盟成员处于这种形势下——那就是司令官。只有上级才能下达命令。”

En: Look at the line of soldiers.
Zh: 看着列队的士兵。

En: Look at the people against the wall.
Zh: 看着墙边的人。

En: A host of men, probably in everyday clothes -- ragged from the conflict and covered in dust. They were not sitting (a common practice for executions in some nations), as demonstrated by the height level of the bullet holes.
Zh: 一大群人，穿的可能是便服——衣服因为冲突残破不堪，上面盖满尘土。通过弹孔的高度来看，他们不是坐着的（某些国家，这是死刑的惯例做法）。

En: The commandant -- the one who gives the order. Machine gun fire crackling through the air, the lights of the muzzle flashes dancing on his face...
Zh: 司令官——就是那位下达指令的人。机关枪的爆裂声在空中回荡，枪口的火光在他的脸上舞动……

En: Good bye. [Leave.]
Zh: 再见。[离开。]

En: A cold sea wind blows away the figures.
Zh: 一阵冰冷的海风吹散了幻象。

En: Kim, who was who in this execution?
Zh: “金，谁参与了这次行刑？”

En: A row of ghostly shades facing a crumbling wall, with another seven shades standing ten metres from them. A single person stands on the sideline.
Zh: 一排幽影面朝着一堵破裂的墙壁，还有7个阴影站在十米开外的地方。此外，还有一个人站在一边，仿佛置身局外。

En: Another likely scenario. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。“另一种可能的设想。”

En: Meaning, this is *a lot* of bullet holes. He brushes the wall with his hand. "Looks like fully-automatic rifle fire. Something you don't see these days..."
Zh: “意思是说，这里的弹孔*太多了*。”他用手扫过墙壁。“看起来是自动步枪。最近很难见到了……”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Say nothing, just nod.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是点头。

En: The manufacturing and sale of automatic rifles was curtailed after the Revolution. The destructive power of such tools proved to be... too much. We do need to retain some humanity in this world.
Zh: “大革命之后，自动步枪的生产和销售就被缩减了。这种武器的毁灭性力量……有点太过了。这个世界还是需要保持一点人性的。”

En: The lieutenant also nods. It is quite a scene -- the two of you standing next to the broken wall of an abandoned building... nodding. Nodding along.
Zh: 警督也点点头。这个场面挺有意思的——你们俩站在一栋废弃建筑的破墙边……点头。只是点头。

En: Two officers of the law against the world. Nodding in unison. It is your source of power. The bond of camaraderie between you... is palpable...
Zh: 两名执法人员对抗整个世界。一致地点头。这是你的力量源泉。你能够清晰地感觉到……彼此之间友谊的纽带……

En: Nod even more.
Zh: 更用力地点头。

En: Shake your head.
Zh: 摇头。

En: The lieutenant is nodding so hard it looks like his head is about to snap off his neck.
Zh: 警督用力点着头，感觉他的脑袋像是要从脖子上折断一样。

En: He nods in agreement -- it really is a very serious scene, worth shaking your head over.
Zh: 他赞同地点点头——这是一个很严肃的场面，值得你摇头。

En: The lieutenant gives a terse nod. "Nothing for us to do here. Let's move along."
Zh: 警督微微点点头。“我们没什么可以做的。还是走吧。”

En: Alright, the lieutenant coughs abruptly. "I'm glad we stopped when we did... my neck was starting to hurt. If there's nothing more, we should get going."
Zh: “好吧，”警督突然咳嗽了一下。“很高兴我们停下来了……我的脖子都有点痛了。如果没什么事的话，我们该走了。”

En: Follow your brother into death itself.
Zh: 跟着你的兄弟一起走向死亡。

En: Phew! All that nodding was rough on the old neck. Close to feeling that twinge...
Zh: 呼！对于您老的脖子来说，点头什么的实在是太难了。差一点就要感觉到那种刺痛……

En: Don't stop nodding.
Zh: 继续点头。

En: Stop nodding.
Zh: 别点头了。

En: The lieutenant matches your nodding pace. He's a true professional at this.
Zh: 警督跟随着你的点头频率。在这方面，他真的是专业人士。

En: The nodding's reaching critical mass! You can't take this much longer, captain!
Zh: 点头已经到达临界点！你无法再承受更多了！

En: Keep nodding, goddamn it! Stay the course!
Zh: 该死的，继续点头啊！坚持到底！

En: A small bead of sweat runs down the side of the lieutenant's face as he maintains his nodding...
Zh: 警督继续不断点头，一粒汗珠从他的侧脸滑落……

En: Abort! Abort! No... shit, it's too late!
Zh: 终止！终止！不……该死，太迟了！

En: Your neck couldn't withstand all this effort... it's too much!
Zh: 你的脖子承受不了这么大压力……太过了！

En: Almost snapped your neck... but I fucking got this. No pain... no pain...
Zh: 脖子差点就要断了……但我他妈的绝对可以。不疼……一点都不疼……

En: It's too much! You can feel your vertebrae starting to crack, your muscles groaning...
Zh: 够了！你感到自己的脊椎开始断裂，肌肉开始呻吟……

En: Just one more nod.
Zh: 再来一次。

En: As the lieutenant takes out a handkerchief and softly taps the sweat off his temple... a faint crack echoes through the coast...
Zh: 警督掏出一块手帕，轻轻擦掉太阳穴上流下的汗水……一阵微弱的咔擦声在海岸边回响……

En: Whew, got off lucky there... that could have resulted in a world of hurt.
Zh: 呼，真走运……差点就陷入受伤的世界了。

En: Whew, got off really lucky... that could have resulted in a world of hurt.
Zh: 呼，真走运……差点就陷入受伤的世界了。

En: Busted the neck, did you? He cranes his neck left and right, stretching. "Glad I stopped when I did... my neck was really starting to hurt. Don't worry, it'll mend... now, we should get going."
Zh: “脖子累惨了，是吗？”他左右拧动脖子，拉伸着。“还好停得够及时……我的脖子都开始抽痛了。别担心，会恢复的……现在，我们真的该走了。”

En: OH SHIT FUCK HELL!
Zh: “卧槽，该死！”

En: He would follow you into death itself... and you would do the same for him.
Zh: 他会跟着你去死……你也会为他做同样的事。

En: Alright, the lieutenant coughs abruptly. "It's a good moment to stop. My neck was starting to hurt. If there's nothing more, we should get going."
Zh: “好吧，”警督突然咳嗽了一下。“这是个停下来的好时机。我的脖子都有点痛了。如果没什么事的话，我们该走了。”

En: They stand, facing the wall... It's impossible to discern any details about their personality or background.
Zh: 他们面对墙，站立着……没办法辨认出他们的个人或是背景信息。

En: What if it was the Feld personnel when their assets were being seized by the revolutionaries?
Zh: “如果是菲尔德的员工呢，也许是他们的资产在大革命时期被查封的时候？”

En: Perhaps the ones executed here were the loyalist-conservatives -- killed by the communists at the start of the civil war. Or it could have been the communists, put to death during the last stretch of the conflict by the Coalition forces.
Zh: “也许被枪决的是保皇派——内战开始的时候，他们就被康米主义者杀了。或者是康米主义者，在跟联盟军队冲突的最后阶段被处死。”

En: The morning sun rises beyond the horizon, radiating the first light of the day. The order was carried out at dawn.
Zh: 朝阳从地平线上升起，发射出清晨的第一缕光线。指令是在拂晓执行的。

En: The sun blazes high up in the sky... baking the planks, the sand, your skin. The order was carried out in the afternoon.
Zh: 太阳高高地挂在天空上……烘烤着木板，沙子，还有你的皮肤。指令是在午后执行的。

En: The sun falls slowly toward the horizon... the last light of the day. The order was carried out in the evening.
Zh: 太阳慢慢朝地平线坠落……最后一丝光线消失在天边。指令是在傍晚执行的。

En: It's dark, very dark... faint lights flicker at the edge of your vision. It's rare for the order to be carried out at night...
Zh: 很黑，非常黑……微弱的光线在你视线的边缘摇曳。指令在夜间执行可不常见……

En: Droplets of rain fall on the wooden planks, the surrounding sand dunes. The clouds block out any rays of light...
Zh: 雨滴落在木板和周围的沙丘上。云层挡住了所有光线……

En: Snowflakes fall on the wooden planks and the surrounding sand dunes, covering the land with the white of innocence...
Zh: 雪花飘落在木板和周围的沙丘上，纯净的白色铺满整个大地……

En: Look at the person standing on the side.
Zh: 看着站在一边的人。

En: Men of duty. Dark duty.
Zh: 坚守使命。黑暗的使命。

En: Who were they?
Zh: 他们是谁？

En: Overpriced mercenaries putting down some rabid dogs. Someone's gotta do it, right?
Zh: 昂贵的雇佣兵杀了些疯狗。总得有人做吧，不是吗？

En: In all truth, violence offers no answer... It's only the last available resort when all mediation fails. And here, all did fail.
Zh: 事实上，暴力无法提供答案……它只是一切调停失败后的最终手段。而且在这里，一切都失败了。

En: Martyrs of the Nation, the ardent knights. The few who had the guts to do this.
Zh: 国家的殉道者，热诚的骑士们。有胆量这样做的少数人。

En: Murderers. Twisted by orders. Young boys forced into killing.
Zh: 杀人犯。被命令扭曲。被迫杀人的年轻小伙子。

En: You, your friends, your family. Everyone you had ever known. This was *you*.
Zh: 你，你的朋友，你的家人。每一个你认识的人。这就是*你*。

En: Soldiers... people in a bad spot they probably did not choose, ordered to commit acts they wanted no part of.
Zh: 士兵……身处困境的人，他们可能没有选择，只能遵守不想执行的指令。

En: It could even have been the employees of the Feld R&D Center down the coast, as their building was taken over by the revolutionaries.
Zh: “甚至可能是海岸那边菲尔德研究中心的雇员，毕竟他们的大厦被革命分子占领了。”

En: At any rate, he gestures toward Gary as though he were presenting a work of art, "*this* is our third, lucky racist."
Zh: “无论如何，”他朝着盖理比了个手势，就好像在展示一件艺术品，“*这位*就是我们的第三个，幸运的种族分子。”

En: At any rate, he gestures toward Gary as though he were presenting a work of art, "*this* is that racist."
Zh: “无论如何，”他朝着盖理比了个手势，就好像在展示一件艺术品，“*这位*就是那个种族分子。”

En: Do you know anything about the man hanged behind the Whirling-in-Rags?
Zh: “关于褴褛飞旋后面吊着的那个人，你知道些什么吗？”

En: Yellow Man? Feels significant somehow, him saying that. You should ask him about it, later...
Zh: 黄皮人？不知为什么，从他嘴里说出来感觉有些意味深长。你之后应该找个时间问问他……

En: Yellow Man? Feels significant somehow, him saying that. You should ask him about it, later...
Zh: 黄皮人？不知为什么，从他嘴里说出来感觉有些意味深长。你之后应该找个时间问问他……

En: He's trying not to look afraid, because that would be incriminating. Yet he *is*.
Zh: 他努力压抑着害怕的表情，因为那样就是有罪的信号。然而他*确实*有罪。

En: Nah, Gary. I just want information.
Zh: “不，盖理。我只想打听点消息。”

En: I am. (Rip out a fine slip.) "For 250 reál -- the maximum."
Zh: “我会的。”（撕下一张罚单）“250雷亚尔——最高罚款。”

En: I am... (Rip out a fine slip.) "For 100 reál."
Zh: “我会的……”（撕下一张罚单）“罚款100雷亚尔。”

En: I am... (Rip out a fine slip.) "For 20 reál."
Zh: “我会的……”（撕下一张罚单）“罚款20雷亚尔。”

En: I would -- but I don't know *how*.
Zh: “我会的——但是我不知道该*怎么做*。”

En: I would -- but I don't know *how.* How do I fine someone, Kim?
Zh: “我会的——但是我不知道该*怎么做*。我该怎么罚款啊，金？”

En: What did you do, Gary?
Zh: “你做了什么，盖理？”

En: WHAT DID YOU DO, GARY?!
Zh: “你做了什么，盖理？！”

En: NOTHING! Nothing... just answering some questions. Helping out the law...
Zh: “没什么！什么也没有……只是回答几个问题。帮助执法人员……”

En: Nothing! Nothing... just answering some questions. Helping out the law...
Zh: “没什么！什么也没有……只是回答几个问题。帮助执法人员……”

En: You wouldn't know anything about the victim's missing armour would you?
Zh: “说到那个被害人丢失的盔甲，你不会刚好知道些什么吧？”

En: Armour? No. He changes his mind. "I mean -- yes, of course. I know he was wearing armour. But I don't know anything *about* it..."
Zh: “盔甲？不知道。”他改变了主意。“我是说——是的，当然知道。我知道他穿着盔甲。但是*关于*它的事，我就不知道了……”

En: Oh, so *that's* what the RCM in Martinaise is about? Great. He nods in sincere approval. "Great to hear someone's finally taking care of that."
Zh: “哦，所以*这就是*RCM来马丁内斯的原因？太棒了。”他真诚地点头表示赞同。“总算有人来处理那件事，真是太好了。”

En: I told you everything I know, sir. I'm *truly* sorry for the mug, but I have nothing to do with that. He shifts uncomfortably in his clothes.
Zh: “我知道的一切都已经告诉你了，先生。关于马克杯的事，我*真的*很抱歉，但是那件事绝对和我无关。”他穿着衣服，不安地扭动着。

En: I told you everything -- I just took the cuirass that's all. I promise. I don't know anything else.
Zh: “我全都告诉你了——我只是把胸甲拿走了，就这些。我保证。其他的我就不知道了。”

En: I can buy into that. A flaming rhino.
Zh: “我可以接受这个说法。一头燃烧的犀牛。”

En: Revachol used to be flaming rhino once, a long time ago... he says and then pauses, thoughtfully.
Zh: “很久以前，瑞瓦肖也是一头燃烧的犀牛……”他说着，然后若有所思地停了下来。

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它吧。

En: Okay, I work as a special courier. He smiles. "You know, urgent deliveries, overnight deliveries, deliveries to out-of-the-way locations."
Zh: “好吧，我是一名特殊速递员。”他笑了笑。“你知道的，紧急配送、夜间配送、偏远地区配送。”

En: How about you deliver *me* a topping pie?
Zh: “那给*我*送个馅饼怎么样？”

En: Okay, let's change it.
Zh: “好吧，我们换个话题。”

En: He looks around, half-ashamed, half-relieved.
Zh: 他四处张望，有些羞愧，有些宽慰。

En: Do you remember how when we met Measurehead and I said the next racist will be the *really* good one?
Zh: “你还记得我们见到测颅先生的时候，我说下一个种族分子*肯定*更纯粹吗？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: I think so. Sorry, as you know, I've been having problems with my memory...
Zh: “我想是吧。抱歉，你也知道的，我的记忆出了点问题……”

En: Yes! Our *lucky* racist.
Zh: “是啊！我们的*幸运*种族分子。”

En: Well... He gestures toward Gary as though he were presenting a work of art. "*This* is that racist."
Zh: “呃……”他朝着盖理比了个手势，就像是在展示一件艺术品。“*这*就是那个种族分子。”

En: Will you grant us three wishes, Gary?
Zh: “你能送给我三个愿望吗，盖理？”

En: Are you, Gary? Are you a racist?
Zh: “你是吗，盖理？你是种族分子吗？”

En: I don't know. I liked the previous racists better.
Zh: “我不知道。我更喜欢之前那个种族分子。”

En: He is nothing compared to Measurehead.
Zh: “他跟测颅先生完全没法比。”

En: Hey, man. He blushes. "All I meant was there are not many Seolites around here. I'm just stating a fact."
Zh: “喂，老兄。”他的脸红了。“我的意思是说西奥人在这里很少见。我只是在陈述事实。”

En: I don't know who that is... He blushes. "But all I meant was there are not many Seolites around here."
Zh: “我不知道那是谁……”他的脸红了。“不过我的意思是说西奥人在这里可不多见。”

En: Return without the lieutenant -- for this? Your balance organ thinks it's a waste of time.
Zh: 撇下警督，自己回去——就因为这个？你的平衡器官觉得这是在浪费时间。

En: Your *gut feeling* tells you it'd be interesting.
Zh: 你的*直觉*告诉你，一定会很有趣。

En: I was saying -- even as Seol remains closed off from the world, Seolite agents have been slowly advancing through the echelons of power on the other isolae.
Zh: “我是说——就算西奥一直与世隔绝，西奥间谍还是慢慢爬上了其它地区的权利阶梯。”

En: So, keep an eye on that partner of yours... He lets the silence linger, then concludes: "Our talk here is concluded. It's not *safe* to discuss this anymore."
Zh: “所以，一定要留心你的搭档……”片刻寂静之后，他总结道：“我们这次对话该结束了。继续讨论下去会*不安全*的。”

En: Nothing. Just wanted to ask if your door's been unlocked lately. Now I have.
Zh: “没什么。只是想问问最近你家大门有没有被打开过。现在我知道了。”

En: Is this about... the Union? Did the big man open the door?
Zh: “这是跟……工会有关吗？那个大人物把门打开了吗？”

En: Relax. Evrart doesn't like the article you wanted to write. Don't write it and it'll will be fine.
Zh: “放松点。艾弗拉特不喜欢你想写的文章。只要你不写，那就没事了。”

En: Honestly, I feel bad about it. The article you wanted to write -- don't let this dissuade you from writing it.
Zh: “老实说，我感觉不太好。你想写的那篇文章——不要因此而放弃写作。”

En: Yeah, I don't know what it was, but he doesn't like you.
Zh: “是啊，我不知道是怎么回事，但他不喜欢你。”

En: Try not to shit yourself Gary. It's just an open door.
Zh: “别吓出屎了，盖理。只是开扇门而已。”

En: (Nod.) "Don't worry, I didn't go in."
Zh: （点头）“别担心，我没进去。”

En: The article about the businesses? How does he know... He shakes his head. "No way in *hell* I'm writing it now. Tell him I'm silent as the grave."
Zh: “关于生意的文章吗？他怎么知道……”他摇摇头。“现在我*绝对*不可能写了。告诉他，我会想坟地一样安静。”

En: I won't cause any more trouble, he whispers. "I'll be Mr. Cooperation. The Union doesn't have to worry about me -- they're honest Vacholieres. Honest men."
Zh: “我再也不会惹麻烦了，”他低语到。“我会当个乖乖先生，好好合作。工会不用担心我——他们是诚实的瓦科莱。诚实的人。”

En: The weather vane has turned, the lieutenant remarks with a smirk. "He cannot be un-turned."
Zh: “风向标已经变了，”警督笑着评论到。“他一定不会为之不动。”

En: He clearly liked his squirming. He may even have changed his mind about the whole door-opening operation.
Zh: 他显然很喜欢这样扭动。他甚至会完全改变对整个开门行动的看法。

En: (Nod grimly). "Our discussion on this topic is over, Gary."
Zh: （冷漠地点点头）“这个话题的讨论已经结束了，盖理。”

En: Really, I don't even know what it was about, I just opened the door.
Zh: “说真的，我自己都不知道是怎么回事，我只是开门而已。”

En: Whatever it is, tell him I'm silent as the grave. The man thinks. "I was probably talking too loud in the Whirling the other night, about some theories..."
Zh: “不管是什么，请转告他，我会跟坟地一样安静。”男人想了想。“那晚我在飞旋旅社一定是说话太大声，我的那些理论……”

En: I won't do it again. He looks around nervously. "If there's anything I can do to assist you -- or the Union -- just ask, okay? I'll try to help if I can."
Zh: 他紧张地环顾四周。“我再也不会这样做了。如果你有什么需要我帮忙的——或者是工会——没问题，我都可以。我会尽我所能的帮你。”

En: I was probably talking too loud in the Whirling the other night, about some theories... Stupid... Shouldn't have run my mouth loud like that.
Zh: “那晚我在飞旋旅社一定是说话太大声，我的那些理论……真蠢……我不该那样大声嚷嚷的。”

En: What could it be about? He frets to himself, not taking your advice. "I probably talked too loud. In the Whirling. About some theories I had. Whatever it is, I'm done with it."
Zh: “那会是什么事呢？”他没有接受你的建议，自顾自的烦恼着。“也许是我说话太大声。在飞旋旅社时候。关于我的那些理论。不管是什么，我都不干了。”

En: Of course. Of course. He looks around nervously. "I won't do it again. If there's anything I can do to assist you -- or the Union -- just ask, okay? I'll try to help if I can."
Zh: “当然，当然。”他紧张地环顾四周。“我再也不会这样做了。如果你有什么需要我帮忙的——或者是工会——没问题，我都可以。我会尽我所能的帮你。”

En: This scared him proper. He's positively *melting* from fear. Has to prop himself up with a lot of anger to keep it together.
Zh: 这着实吓了他一跳。他肯定是因为恐惧而*融化*了。他必须用大量的愤怒来支撑自己，才能维持下去。

En: Great. The lieutenant mumbles. "First the sandwich, now a pie..."
Zh: “很好。”警督嘟囔道。“首先是三明治，现在又是馅饼……”

En: His massive musculature?
Zh: 他巨大的肌肉组织？

En: Something worn underneath it?
Zh: 衣服下面穿着什么？

En: No, he's scrawny. Try again.
Zh: 不，他骨瘦如柴的。再试一次。

En: Yes. Like a piece of ceramic armour, for example. One that makes a clicking sound when the plates meet each other, resembling pearls or marbles. Stolen from the corpse in the yard near where he lives.
Zh: 是的。举个例子，比如说一件陶瓷盔甲。当板块彼此碰撞的时候，就会发出一种嘀嗒的声响，很像珍珠或是弹球。就是从他家附近院子里那具尸体上偷来的。

En: I was ashamed of what I did. And I didn't want you to know. You see gleaming white ceramic shine underneath -- a thin layer of interlocking plates covers his gaunt torso.
Zh: 你看见陶瓷在他的衣服下面发出白色的亮光——薄薄的一层联锁板甲将他单薄的躯干包裹其中。“我为自己的所作所为感到羞愧。而且我不想让你知道。”

En: This *shame* is surprisingly sincere.
Zh: 这种*羞愧*真是惊人地真诚。

En: We're not detecting falsehoods, sire. He's gearing up to admit the truth.
Zh: 吾辈没有检测到谎言，大人。他准备好承认事实了。

En: GARY! WHAT'S GOING ON?!
Zh: “盖理！出什么事了？！”

En: LATER, MORELL! I'VE GOT APOLOGIZING TO DO.
Zh: “一会再说，莫雷尔！我要跟人道歉。”

En: No -- you've got *explaining* to do. The lieutenant's tone is icy.
Zh: “不——你需要*解释*。”警督的语气冷冰冰的。

En: Goddamit, Gary...
Zh: “该死，盖理……”

En: I know.
Zh: “我知道。”

En: Shame on you! Give the cuirass to the officer at once! The woman looks at you, her eyes soft and pleading.
Zh: “你太丢脸了！赶快把胸甲交给警官！”女人看着你，温柔的眼神中带着一丝恳求的意味。

En: Gary didn't mean to interfere with your investigation, officers, he's just... thick headed and poor as dirt. But he's always helped us, given us a place to stay. And he's followed Morell into god knows what jungles...
Zh: “盖理不是有意要干扰你们的调查，警官，他只是……太过愚蠢，还穷困潦倒。但是他一直在帮我们，给了我们安身的地方。而且他还跟着莫雷尔，去了那些天知道是什么鬼的丛林……”

En: He's a loyal friend.
Zh: 他是个忠诚的朋友。

En: Give me that armour. Now.
Zh: “把盔甲给我，马上。”

En: Why did you *really* put those clothes in the trash?
Zh: “*说真的*，你为什么要把衣服扔进垃圾堆？”

En: Why did you lie to me, Gary?
Zh: “你为什么要对我撒谎，盖理？”

En: Are we done here, Gary? (Conclude.)
Zh: “没别的事了吧，盖理？”（总结。）

En: He sighs again, hangs his head, and unbuttons his shirt fully. A cuirass that matches the dead man's boots comes into view. Soon it is in your hands -- smelling of his sweat.
Zh: 他又叹了口气，接着低下头，完全解开了自己的衬衫。一件跟受害者的靴子配套的胸甲出现在你眼前。很快又交到了你手中——上面沾满他的汗味。

En: But so, so light to hold. Like a bag of cotton.
Zh: 但是拿起来又是那么的轻。就像一包棉花。

En: This is it. This will protect your mortal shell. Don it and live.
Zh: 就是它了。它会保护你的人类躯壳。穿上它，然后生存下去。

En: Everyone was picking those pieces off him and I was watching them do it. And they scattered his clothes all over the yard, everything was smelling... He looks at his feet.
Zh: “所有人都在从他身上扒衣服，我就在边上看着他们。然后他们把衣服丢在院子的各个角落，搞得到处都是味道……”他看着自己的脚。

En: So I went there to take out my trash and started cleaning up. All those rags on the ground, him swinging up there, and... He swallows. "I had a lapse of honour, sir. I thought: He's a foreigner. They all say he wasn't from here."
Zh: “所以我去那里倒垃圾，然后开始清理。地上所有那些破布，他还挂在树上，而且……”他咽了咽口水。“是我失礼了，先生。我想：他是个外国人。他们都说他不是本地的。”

En: Only the cuirass was left, so I stripped it off him. It was early in the morning, no one saw me. I took it with me. It was a mistake. Had I known it'd give you guys' trouble, I wouldn't have... His lips start quivering. "Fuck..."
Zh: “只剩下了胸甲，所以我把它扒下来了。那是一个清晨，没人看到我。这是个错误。要是我知道这会给你们带来麻烦，我肯定不会……”他的嘴唇开始颤抖。“该死……”

En: We're detecting sincere contrition here, sire. He's not trying to flatter anyone.
Zh: 吾辈察觉到了真诚的悔悟，大人。他没有想奉承任何人。

En: Because I was weak. He says, staring at nothing in particular. "I should have told you the moment I saw you, but..."
Zh: “因为我很软弱。”他的目光有些涣散。“我看见你的瞬间，就应该告诉你的，但是……”

En: It's okay. The lieutenant jots something down in his notebook. "It was a loose end and you're tying it up now."
Zh: “没事的。”警督在笔记本里写了几笔。“这是个未了结的小问题，而你现在把它圆上了。”

En: I'm so fucking sorry I called you *Yellow Man*. He says silently. "Seolite officers commanded the Suzerain's navy. Most of them sided with the King, when..." He shakes his head.
Zh: “我真的很抱歉，我不该叫你*黄皮人*的。”他默默地说到。“西奥官员统领着宗主国的海军。大部分人都是站在国王这一边的……”他摇摇头。

En: They were thoroughly *conservative* men, he realizes suddenly.
Zh: 他突然意识到，他们是彻头彻尾的*保守派*。

En: It's difficult to say what the lieutenant thinks of this historic apology. His face does not belie emotions.
Zh: 很难看出警督对这个郑重其事的道歉作何感想。他的脸上没有流露出任何感情。

En: THE HELL, GARY?! YOU IN TROUBLE?
Zh: “搞什么鬼，盖理？！你惹上麻烦了？”

En: I'll explain later... He doesn't muster up the strength to yell.
Zh: “我之后再解释……”他没有力气大喊大叫。

En: He's a scaredy cat. The woman looks at Gary, her eyes motherly.
Zh: “他是个胆小鬼。”女人看着盖理，露出慈母般的眼神。

En: Yes. Absolutely. I will *never* do anything like this again. He looks around, relieved of some burden, his mouth still quivering. "I won't mess with Mr. Claire either, you have my word."
Zh: “是的。绝对的。我*永远*不会做这种事了。”他环顾四周，卸下了一些重担，但嘴唇还在颤抖。“我不会再去招惹克莱尔先生了，我向你发誓。”

En: Yes. Absolutely. I will *never* do anything like this again. He looks around, relieved of some burden, his mouth still quivering.
Zh: “是的。绝对没错。我*永远*不会做这种事了。”他环顾四周，卸下了一些重担，但嘴唇还在颤抖。

En: Man, I don't wanna talk about that... I was wrong to bring it up at all. Shouldn't have said those things about your partner...
Zh: “天啦，我不想提这个……我根本就不该提这件事。更不应该对你的搭档说出那种话……”

En: Most of the Seolites may be shifty, but the ones in Revachol have always been loyal to Revachol -- and the Suzerain too. I... He trails off.
Zh: “大多数西奥人也许是很狡诈，但是瑞瓦肖那些一直对这里很忠诚——还有宗主国也是。我……”他的声音越来越小。

En: It's for the best, dear. Lay that story to rest.
Zh: “这是最好的结果，亲爱的。别再提那个故事了。”

En: The man simply nods, his long blond hair falling on his shoulders.
Zh: 男人点点头，长长的金发落在他的肩膀上。

En: Do you know who killed the hanged man?
Zh: “你知道是谁杀了那个吊着的男人吗？”

En: I always thought it was the Union, but... I sure as hell won't go around saying that anymore. You have my word. I don't know -- and I won't be running my mouth on this subject anymore.
Zh: “我一直以为是工会，不过……我肯定不会到处瞎说了。这一点我可以保证。我不知道——而且我也不会再对这个话题喋喋不休了。”

En: This is all he knows.
Zh: 他知道的只有这么多。

En: I always thought it was the Union... some Union hard-asses. Lynched him because of the strike. But almost everyone in town knows that. I wish I could tell you more... He shakes his head.
Zh: “我一直以为是工会……工会的那些硬汉。因为罢工对他动了私刑。不过，城里几乎所有人都知道这一点。我也希望能多告诉你一点……”他摇摇头。

En: *Serious* question time. This man is no innocent. No one is.
Zh: *严肃*的问题时间。这个人不是无辜的。没有人是。

En: After all this time with Morell, he must have an opinion on cryptids -- this could lead to a good one.
Zh: 毕竟和莫雷尔待了这么长时间，他对神秘动物一定有自己的理解——也许会有一个好结果。

En: What *does* he do then? This feels like a good opportunity to DOMINATE him.
Zh: 那他到底做了*什么*？感觉是个支配他的好机会。

En: See? He's being evasive. Shake him up, show him who's boss.
Zh: 看见了吗？他在逃避。把他摇醒，让他看看谁才是这里的老大。

En: There *probably* is a follow-up to this, but you can't think of any. Doesn't matter. Waste of time anyway. Gotta keep moving.
Zh: 这里*可能*还需要跟踪调查，但你想不出到底是什么。不要紧。反正也是浪费时间。还是继续前进吧。

En: Never mind.
Zh: 不要紧。

En: Mhm, I imagine it's quite a sight, the man crosses his arms confidently.
Zh: “嗯，我能想象到那种景象，”男人自信地抱起双臂。

En: Super solid argument, Gary. Can't argue with that.
Zh: 非常有利的论据，盖理。这一点无可争辩。

En: It's clear the burning rhino is dear to him on many levels. Some even *spiritual*.
Zh: 显然，燃烧犀牛在很多层面上都更得他的欢心。有些甚至是*精神上的*。

En: I'm satisfied. Are you satisfied? Because I am.
Zh: 我很满意。你满意了吗？因为我很满意。

En: Here we go. Start pumping that sweet info.
Zh: 我们开始吧。开始灌输那些甜蜜的信息。

En: *Serious* question time. This man is no innocent. No one is.
Zh: *严肃*的问题时间。这个人不是无辜的。没有人是。

En: He pronounces 'Revachol' with a hard 'k,' unlike other people.
Zh: 他把‘瑞瓦肖’念成了‘瑞瓦肖’，跟其他人不一样。

En: He pronounces 'Revachol' with a hard k, unlike other people.
Zh: 他把‘瑞瓦肖’念成了‘瑞瓦肖’，跟其他人不一样。

En: You said 'Revakhol'?
Zh: “你说‘瑞瓦肖’？”

En: I like to pronounce it the *hard* way. The old way. The Vespertine way. He nods solemnly.
Zh: “我喜欢用*硬核*一点的发音方式。以前的方式。维斯珀的方式。”他郑重其事地点点头。

En: Garbage. You're a *true* patriot and you know how it's said. The Suresne way. The *fine* way. With an elegant shhh...
Zh: 屁话。你是一个*真正*的爱国者，你知道该怎么说。公社的方式。*好*方式。还带着一丝高雅和讲究……

En: He winks at you, trying to relay some hidden message. Inviting you to mispronounce it too perhaps? It's odd.
Zh: 他朝你眨眨眼，试着传达某种隐藏的信息。也许是邀请你也一起发错音？有点奇怪。

En: It's a secret rite. A very fringe-nationalist handshake, probably.
Zh: 这是一种秘密仪式。一个非常边缘民族主义的信号交换，大概吧。

En: The *stupid* way?
Zh: *愚蠢*的方式？

En: This is a considerable expense to him. One month's wage, most likely.
Zh: 这对他来说是一比相当大的开销。很可能要花一个月的工资。

En: It's, in a way, admirable how quickly he composes himself after such a blow. This man digs authority. Even when it's bullying him.
Zh: 在某种程度上，这是一种令人钦佩的事：在经历这样的挫折之后，他居然能这么快就镇定下来。甚至是在欺负他的时候。

En: The sound you heard was not the sound of something easily abandoned.
Zh: 你听见的声音，并不是那种容易被抛弃的东西的声音。

En: 'Tis true sire. This man simply did not know what he was getting caught up in.
Zh: 这是真的，大人。这个人的确不知道自己到底卷入了怎样的麻烦之中。

En: So you *do* know something about it?
Zh: “所以，你*确实*知道些什么了？”

En: So you *do* know something about it?
Zh: “所以，你*确实*知道些什么了？”

En: All right.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Maybe I can help you with something else? I keep my nose out of affairs like that. I'm a decent man.
Zh: “也许我可以帮你点别的？我对那种事总是三缄其口。我是个正派的人。”

En: No, no, he shakes his head emphatically. Then corrects his tie. "Nothing. He was some kind of mercenary, but everyone here knows that... I'm just glad to hear you're looking into it, that's all."
Zh: “不不，”他用力摇着头，然后调整了一下自己的领带。“没什么。他是某种雇佣兵，不过这里每个人都知道……只是听到你说在调查，我觉得很开心，仅此而已。”

En: He's not feeling very comfy in his clothes, is he? Strange...
Zh: 他穿的衣服感觉不是很舒服，是吗？有点奇怪……

En: He didn't *kill* him or anything, but there's something going on here.
Zh: 他没有*杀*他，或者是任何人，但是这里肯定有什么不对劲的地方。

En: Uncomfortable shifting around doesn't make him the killer, though. It's something else.
Zh: 不安地扭动并不代表他就是凶手。是别的什么。

En: He's not feeling too comfortable in his own skin. Odd, I'd say.
Zh: 他感觉有些不太自在。要我说，有点奇怪。

En: There is no need, officer, I won't do it again. Besides, my pay, it's... I'm barely getting by as it is with those Kojkos edging on the delivery business, sir. Please...
Zh: “没这个必要，警官，我再也不会这样做了。再说，我的薪水……那些克吉克正在蚕食快递业务，我也只是勉强度日，先生。拜托……”

En: Embarrassing. You should know what a misconduct fine is by now. Make it up by being especially *unkind* to him in future dealings.
Zh: 尴尬。现在你应该知道什么是不端行为罚款了。在之后的来往中，就用对他特别*不友好*的态度来补偿吧。

En: Using a standard form, officer. The lieutenant pulls out a sheet of carbon copy paper, scribbles something on it, then tears out the bottom page and hands it to Gary.
Zh: “用标准表格，警官。”警督抽出一张碳复印纸，在上面草草画了几笔，然后撕掉最下面那一页，递给盖理。

En: It's 50 reál, he explains. "You're getting off easy. In return, we expect *information*. You have questions to answer, sir."
Zh: “罚款50雷亚尔，”他解释到。“这次就这么放过你了。作为回报，我们想要一些*信息*。有些问题需要你回答，先生。”

En: Okay, I deserve that -- and I won't do it again. He accepts the slip of copy paper with a bow. "You have my word."
Zh: 他弯腰接过那张复印纸。“好吧，这是我应得的——我再也不会这样做了。这一点我可以保证。”

En: Oh god, 250? How am I gonna pay that? He looks at the slip of yellow copy paper, then gets a hold of himself. "Okay. I'll work harder. I'll pay it off, I promise."
Zh: “天啦，250雷亚尔，我怎么可能付得起？”他看着黄色的复印纸，然后稳定住自己的情绪。“好吧。我会更努力工作的。我保证，我会付清的。”

En: And I'll *never* do it again. I don't know what got into me, really... work has been stressful lately. Damn Kojkos price dumping us out of competition.
Zh: “而且我*永远*不会这么做了。我不知道自己是怎么了，真的……最近工作压力有些大。该死的克吉克，想用价格优势把我们挤出竞争。”

En: Whooh, he's visibly relieved. "Thank you, you won't regret this. I won't use another man's property to dump my garbage *ever* again."
Zh: “呼，”他显然是松了一口气。“谢谢你，你绝对不会后悔的。我*再也不会*用别人的财产来倾倒自己的垃圾了。”

En: I don't know what got into me, really... work has been stressful lately. Damn Kojkos price dumping us out of competition.
Zh: “我不知道自己是怎么了，真的……最近工作压力有些大。该死的克吉克，想用价格优势把我们挤出竞争。”

En: I don't know what got *into* me. Stuffing my garbage in another man's property, it's... I've been having trouble at work lately. The Kojkos are price dumping us out of competition.
Zh: “我不知道自己是*怎么了*。把我的垃圾丢进别人的财产里，简直是……我最近工作不太顺利。该死的克吉克，想用价格优势把我们挤出竞争。”

En: I'm sorry, okay? I thought I could cut costs. I shouldn't have. I shouldn't have disgraced myself.
Zh: “我很抱歉，好吗。我以为这样可以削减开支。我不应该这么做的。我不应该让自己蒙羞。”

En: He studies his reaction. Gary doesn't answer.
Zh: 他研究着他的反应。盖理没有回应。

En: I was only cleaning up. I live right across the yard from where he was hanged and I saw him stripped naked. All the clothes lying around in the yard, smelling... People are animals, you know...
Zh: “我只是清理而已。我就住在他被吊死的院子对面，然后看见他被扒了个精光。所有衣服都散落在院子里，那股味道……人都是动物，你知道的……”

En: Okay. Then what happened?
Zh: “好吧。之后发生什么事了。”

En: Yes-yes. What happened?
Zh: “是啊是啊。之后发生什么事了。”

En: Then I came out to clean up the rags because *no one else would*. I put them into the Whirling's trash -- along with a broken mug, admittedly... He changes his mind mid-sentence.
Zh: “然后我出去清理那些破布，因为*没人*愿意这样做。我把它们丢进飞旋旅社的垃圾箱——我承认，还有一个破马克杯……”他中途改变了主意。

En: Okay, I was coming to throw the mug away and, well, I threw the mug there and the clothes too.
Zh: “好吧，我打算去扔马克杯，然后，我把马克杯和衣服都丢进去了。”

En: Right, it was just *civic duty*, the lieutenant remarks drolly.
Zh: “好吧，这只是*公民义务*，”警督幽默地评论到。

En: Exactly! That's exactly what it was -- civic duty.
Zh: “没错！就是这样没错——公民义务。”

En: Maybe it *was* pinball?
Zh: 也许*是*弹球？

En: There's something going on here. You should observe him more closely, after this topic is concluded.
Zh: 好像有些不太对劲。这个话题结束之后，你应该仔细观察观察他。

En: An infant could see he's not telling the truth -- but he's too scared to admit more wrongdoing.
Zh: 就连婴儿都能看出他没讲真话——但是他太害怕，不敢承认更多坏事。

En: I hope I could help your investigation, in my small way. He's visibly relieved it's over.
Zh: “希望我能尽自己的一点微薄之力，协助你们的调查。”一切结束，他显然是松了一口气。

En: I'm sorry for what I said, he replies, then falls silent.
Zh: “很抱歉我说了那样的话，”他回复着，接着又陷入沉默。

En: This man is truly sorry for underestimating the honour and military might of the Seolite man.
Zh: 这个人真心为低估了西奥的荣誉和军事力量而感到抱歉。

En: Short means sincere in these things.
Zh: 在这些事情上，简短就意味着真诚。

En: Don't be so relieved yet, Gary. This bad cop may have been in *your* apartment, admiring your mug collection. Perhaps a little intimidation?
Zh: 先别这么安心，盖理。这个坏警察也许曾经去过*你的*公寓，欣赏过你的马克杯藏品。也许还会来一点点恐吓？

En: Hmm, does this mean you were in *his* apartment, admiring *his* colonial mug collection? Perhaps it would be *interesting* to tell him...
Zh: 嗯，这是不是意味着你进过*他的*公寓，欣赏过*他的*马克杯藏品？如果告诉他的话，也许会很*有趣*……

En: Wait, does this mean you've been in his apartment? On Evrart's behalf? That was *his* colonial mug collection -- perhaps this could soften him up further.
Zh: 等等，这是不是意味着你代表艾弗拉特进过他的公寓？那是*他的*殖民地马克杯藏品——也许这样可以进一步软化他。

En: This scared him proper. He's positively *melting* from fear. Has to prop himself up with a lot of anger to keep it together.
Zh: 这着实吓了他一跳。他肯定是因为恐惧而*融化*了。他必须用大量的愤怒来支撑自己，才能维持下去。

En: Okay, but... I told you everything. What else can I say?
Zh: “好吧，但是……我已经全都告诉你了。我还能说什么？”

En: All right. Of course, officer.
Zh: “好吧。当然了，警官。”

En: We'll return to this later. For now, let me ask you something else.
Zh: “我们稍后再聊这个问题。现在，我先问你点别的。”

En: Of course. Of course.
Zh: “当然，当然可以。”

En: Sure. If you want, I could hook you up with some topping pie no problem.
Zh: “当然。如果你想的话，我可以给你弄些馅饼，没问题。”

En: Yum. Did someone say topping pie?
Zh: 嗯。好像有人提到馅饼了？

En: No need to know what's on it. Just give it to me.
Zh: “没必要知道上面有什么。直接给我就好。”

En: Sounds like some conspiracy topic. You might be able to discuss it with him when the lieutenant isn't here. *If* you can remember it.
Zh: 听起来像是阴谋论的话题。等警督不在这里的时候，也许你们可以讨论一下。*如果*你能记得的话。

En: Oh no. Nothing like that. *Definitely* not scheming. He flashes an impenetrable smile.
Zh: “不，没有那种事。*肯定*不是什么诡计。”他挤出一个令人费解的微笑。

En: Hello! I'm Gary. How do you do, officer?
Zh: “你好！我是盖理。你好吗，警官？”

En: Yellow Man! He turns toward Kim. "I mean... officer."
Zh: 他转向金。“黄皮人！我是说……警官。”

En: Yellow Man! He turns toward Kim. "I mean... officer."
Zh: 他转向金。“黄皮人！我是说……警官。”

En: I'm so fucking sorry I kept calling you *yellow man* in my head. He says silently. "Seolite officers commanded the Suzerain's navy. Most of them sided with the King, when..." He shakes his head.
Zh: “我真的很抱歉，我不该叫你*黄皮人*的。”他默默地说到。“西奥官员统领着宗主国的海军。大部分人都是站在国王这一边的……”他摇摇头。

En: Gary, do you know how to turn back time?
Zh: “盖瑞，你知道怎么让时间倒流吗？”

En: Oh yeah, he's definitely one of us. Smart one too, tactical. He might very well know how to turn back time. Ask him!
Zh: 没错，他一定跟我们一样足智多谋。或许他很清楚怎么让时间倒流。问问他！

En: But still... he's definitely one of us. Longs for the Old Days - the *Old Ways*. He's likely to know how to turn back time. Ask him!
Zh: 但是……他一定跟我们一样，一样渴望着旧日时光——*旧日之道*。他可能知道如何让时间倒流。问问他！

En: Deep satisfying rumble runs through your small intestine. It feels affirming.
Zh: 你的小肠发出了深沉而满足的蠕动声。感觉像是在肯定。

En: Always a pleasure to see an officer of the law!
Zh: “很高兴见到警官你！”

En: I mean… He turns toward Kim. "Officers."
Zh: 他转向金。“我是说……警官们。”

En: Yes, officer?
Zh: “什么事，警官？”

En: Thank you for your cooperation. [Leave.]
Zh: “感谢你的配合。”[离开。]

En: Are you a cryptozoologist too?
Zh: “你也是神秘动物学家吗？”

En: Why's he shifting around like that? Analyze Gary's composure.
Zh: 他为什么要那样一直动来动去的？分析一下盖理的镇静度。

En: Clinking sound.
Zh: 叮叮当当的声音。

En: Scared him with apartment.
Zh: 用公寓的事吓他。

En: Want to wear armour.
Zh: 想穿盔甲。

En: Thinking about the armour.
Zh: 想起盔甲。

En: Uncomfortable in his clothes.
Zh: 衣服不舒服。

En: Grilled him about topping pie.
Zh: 盘问他馅饼的事。

En: Morell felt Gary's weird.
Zh: 莫雷尔感觉到盖理有古怪。

En: Icosahedral Red Die.
Zh: 二十面体红骰子。

En: What's that strange sound?
Zh: “什么奇怪的声音？”

En: No-no... I help Morell with research sometimes and I've learned some things along the way. But I don't usually go in for picnics like this on my own.
Zh: “不是……我有时候会帮莫雷尔一起研究，一路也学到了点东西。但我一般不会自己去野餐。”

En: I'm into cryptids. Do you have a favourite?
Zh: “我喜欢神秘动物。你最喜欢的是什么？”

En: What do you do, then? If not cryptozoology?
Zh: “那你在做什么呢？如果不是神秘动物学家的话？”

En: Fascinating. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “太好了。我们聊聊别的吧。”

En: Oh, yes! The Burning Rhino. Morell doubts he's real, but I don't much care -- because I won't be the one looking for him out in Safre Serai.
Zh: “哦，是的！燃烧犀牛。莫雷尔怀疑它是不是真的存在，但我不太在意——毕竟我不会是跑去萨弗里塞莱找他的人。”

En: What's a Burning Rhino?
Zh: “燃烧犀牛是什么？”

En: Yellow Man! He turns toward Kim. "I mean... officer."
Zh: 他转向金。“黄皮人！我是说……警官。”

En: Oh, this and that. He grins.
Zh: “哦，这个那个的吧。”他咧嘴一笑。

En: You were *surprised* to see my colleague, Lieutenant Kitsuragi.
Zh: “你看见我的同事曷城警督好像很*吃惊*啊。”

En: Not many Seolites here, or anywhere, other than Seol. I meant no offence, truly.
Zh: “西奥人在这里可不常见，除了西奥之外，哪里都不常见。我无意冒犯，真心的。”

En: Do you have a problem with Seolites?
Zh: “你对西奥人有什么意见吗？”

En: Because they're not really human, right?
Zh: “因为他们不是真正的人类，是吗？”

En: Yes. What *are* they doing in that Seol of theirs? Scheming?
Zh: “是的。他们在西奥到底在*做什么*？策划什么诡计吗？”

En: A rhinoceros that looks ordinary during the day, but burns brightly by night. Well, at least the males do.
Zh: “一种犀牛，白天看起来很正常，但是到了晚上就会熊熊燃烧。呃，至少雄性是这样的。”

En: How do they burn?
Zh: “它们是怎么燃烧的？”

En: Why only the males?
Zh: “为什么只有雄性会燃烧？”

En: They have special ducts just above their shoulder blades that secrete a combustible fluid. When the rhino is just beginning to light itself, it looks as though it has wings of fire.
Zh: “它们的肩胛骨上有特殊的管道，可以分泌易燃液体。当犀牛点燃自己的时候，看起来就像是生出了一对火焰翅膀。”

En: But how is this combustible fluid lit?
Zh: 那这个易燃液体是怎么点燃的？

En: The rhino starts running very fast to build heat, then stops, raises its head -- and sparks fly from its neck, setting its back ablaze.
Zh: “犀牛通过快速跑动来产生热量，接着停下来，抬起头——然后颈部冒出火花，把整个后背点燃。”

En: How does the lighting of this... fluid actually work?
Zh: “那这个液体……燃烧是怎么回事？”

En: This seems unlikely.
Zh: “好像不太可能。”

En: I want to be just like that rhino, running through the night with guns blazing!
Zh: “我也想像那头犀牛一样，在深夜耀眼的枪火中奔跑！”

En: Perhaps we should have more such *decisive action* in Revachol. You know -- this city used to be flaming rhino once, a long time ago... he says and then pauses, thoughtfully.
Zh: “也许瑞瓦肖需要更多*果断的行动*。你知道的——很久以前，这座城市也是一头燃烧犀牛……”他若有所思地停顿了一下。

En: We don't really do that around here.
Zh: “我们这里不会这么做的。”

En: Yeah, well -- Revachol used to be flaming rhino once, a long time ago... he says and then pauses, thoughtfully. "That seems unlikely too, doesn't it?"
Zh: “是啊，呃——很久以前，瑞瓦肖也是一头燃烧犀牛……”他若有所思地停顿了一下。“这好像也不太可能，是吧？”

En: The flames are not just for decoration. They are an integral part of the beast's mating behaviour.
Zh: “这些火焰可不是装饰用的。它们是野兽交配行为中不可分割的一部分。”

En: How so?
Zh: “怎么讲？”

En: During the Burning Rhino's mating season, herds of male rhinos, all aflame, encircle herds of female rhinos, forming a fiery ring as they begin to copulate, loudly.
Zh: “等到燃烧犀牛交配的季节，一大群熊熊燃烧的雄性犀牛会把雌性犀牛包围其中，组成一个炙热的圆环，然后大声地开始交配。”

En: The lieutenant sighs without looking up from his notes.
Zh: 警督叹了一口气，头都没有抬起来。

En: Local peasants call it the Passion Ring. They fear the rhinos. As, perhaps, they should. Anyway...
Zh: “本地农民都叫它热情之环。他们害怕犀牛。当然了，这是应该的。无所谓了……”

En: Sounds intriguing.
Zh: “听起来很有意思。”

En: Sounds shady.
Zh: “听起来有点见不得人。”

En: As long you're making ends meet and advancing your career objectives.
Zh: “只要你能量入为出，推进职业目标。”

En: Sometimes.
Zh: “有时候吧。”

En: That's not an answer.
Zh: “这不算答案。”

En: I just didn't want to bore you with unnecessary detail, officer.
Zh: “我只是不想讲些不必要的细节让你厌烦，警官。”

En: Officer, I would never. I just didn't want to bore you with unnecessary detail.
Zh: “警官，我永远不会的。我只是不想讲些不必要的细节让你厌烦。”

En: I'm doing all I can in this economy, officer.
Zh: “在这种经济状况下，我已经尽力了，警官。”

En: The lieutenant looks up at you, sighs, and goes back to his notes.
Zh: 警督抬头看了你一眼，叹了口气，然后又低头继续做笔记。

En: The lieutenant looks up at you, almost sadly.
Zh: 警督抬头看了你一眼，表情几乎有些悲伤。

En: No, no problem at all. He flashes an impenetrable smile at the lieutenant.
Zh: “不，完全没问题。”他对警督露出一丝让人费解的微笑。

En: You said you'd tell me about Seolites, when Kim isn't here. He isn't here.
Zh: “你说金不在的时候，可以跟我讲讲西奥人。现在他刚好不在这里。”

En: That's not what I was implying at all... He eyes the lieutenant nervously. "But, of course, you're just kidding, officer."
Zh: “我根本没有这个意思……”他紧张地看着金。“不过当然了，你只是在开玩笑，警官。”

En: The lieutenant is a true Vacholiere. Why did you call him a Seolite?
Zh: “警督是真正的瓦科莱。你为什么叫他西奥人？”

En: The lieutenant is a native of Revachol.
Zh: “警督是瑞瓦肖本地人。”

En: Oh, yes, of course he is. I was just speaking about his... connections. He flashes an impenetrable smile. "Let's change the subject, okay?"
Zh: “是啊，他当然是了。我只是在说他的……血脉。”他露出一丝让人费解的微笑。“我们换个话题，好吗？”

En: Yes, officer. I'm very glad we're getting a chance to speak about it candidly, despite the trouble I caused Mr. Claire -- and I will *never* do anything of the sort again, I assure you...
Zh: “没错，警官。很高兴我们总算可以率直地谈论这个话题了，尽管我给克莱尔先生惹了不少麻烦——而且我以后*再也不会*这么做了，我向你保证……”

En: Yes, officer. I'm very glad we're getting a chance to speak about it candidly, man to man.
Zh: “没错，警官。很高兴我们总算可以率直地谈论这个话题了，直言不讳。”

En: His smile, meant to convey total openness, is that of an experienced salesman.
Zh: 他的微笑，传达出绝对的率真，就像一个经验丰富的推销员。

En: I'm listening.
Zh: “我在听。”

En: Get on with it, then.
Zh: “那就开始吧。”

En: I'm not sure I like where this is heading, but I'm willing to hear you out.
Zh: “我不确定自己会不会喜欢这个走向，不过我还是愿意听你说说。”

En: Well, the Seolites -- they're a very smart people. Smart and secretive. Most think they have isolated themselves on their landmass because they disdain to mingle with the rest of us...
Zh: “好吧，西奥人——他们非常聪明。聪明又诡秘。大部分人觉得他们孤立在自己的陆地上，因为他们不屑于跟其他人混在一起……”

En: I'm not saying that *you* think that too. You seem like an incisive man, officer. You must be, being a detective.
Zh: “我并不是说*你*也是那样想的。你看起来像是个很有洞察力的男人，警官。作为一名警探，也理应如此。”

En: Yes, sir, I'm a regular Dick Mullen. But better, because I'm *real*.
Zh: “没错，先生。我只是个普通的迪克·马伦。不过要比他更好一些，因为我是个*真人*。”

En: I don't know how good of a detective I am, really...
Zh: “说真的，我不知道自己到底是个多优秀的警探……”

En: Right, sure. You were saying?
Zh: “好吧，当然了。你之前在说？”

En: I sense that you might be trying to flatter me.
Zh: “我感觉你是想奉承我。”

En: His eyes widen momentarily. "Right, of course you are. Well, as I was saying..."
Zh: 他的眼睛一下子睁大了。“没错，你当然是了。呃，我也说过了……”

En: Those who are best at their job are almost always the most humble. But, as I was saying...
Zh: “那些工作最出色的人总是表现的很谦虚。不过，我也说过了……”

En: Thank you, sir. It's not often that the RCM receives the recognition that it deserves.
Zh: “谢谢你，先生。RCM很少能得到应得的认可。”

En: It's a shame, too. What would we all do without law enforcement. But, as I was saying...
Zh: “这是个耻辱。要是没有执法机关，我们又能做什么呢。不过，我也说过了……”

En: Even as Seol remains closed off from the world, Seolite agents have been slowly advancing through the echelons of power on the other isolae.
Zh: “就算西奥一直与世隔绝，西奥间谍还是慢慢爬上了其它地区的权利阶梯。”

En: Perhaps I was presumptuous to suggest I knew anything about you, as we've only just met. My apologies. But, as I was saying...
Zh: “也许是我冒昧，想暗示自己很了解你，毕竟我们才刚刚见面。我很抱歉。不过，我也说过了……”

En: They come as apparently well-meaning immigrants. Sometimes students -- they learn our *violin* and *cello*. But more often tech workers in cybernetics and similar advanced industries.
Zh: “他们出现时显然是一幅善良移民的样子。有时候是学生——来学习我们的*小提琴*和*大提琴*。不过，更多的是神经机械学和类似先进工业的技术人员。”

En: Seol is known for being technologically advanced, although it is not entirely known how far they have progressed.
Zh: 西奥是以科技发达而闻名的，虽然没有人完全了解他们到底发展到了什么地步。

En: That seems harmless enough.
Zh: “听起来好像没什么害处啊。”

En: It sounds like their presence could contribute to economic growth and prosperity.
Zh: “听起来他们的存在可以为经济发展和社会繁荣做出贡献。”

En: It seems dangerous that immigrants would go into fields that affect information technology.
Zh: “移民进入影响信息技术的领域，听起来好像有些危险。”

En: And you think there's a problem with this?
Zh: “所以你觉得有什么问题呢？”

En: Yes, but consider: Seol is famous, or shall I say, *infamous*, for its spy tech. Don't you find it... convenient?
Zh: “是的，但是考虑一下：西奥很出名，或者应该说因为他们的间谍技术*臭名昭著*。你难道不觉得这样很……便利吗？”

En: Seol is indeed famous for its advanced microtechnology, and for exporting it to foreign governments. Kijion Eendracht, the owner of Eendracht Airworks, is Seolite...
Zh: 西奥确实以先进的微型技术而闻名，然而还出口给了外国政府。基恩·阿尔斯特，阿尔斯特航空公司的所有者，就是西奥的……

En: Consider, though: Seol is famous, or shall I say, infamous, for its spy tech. Don't you find it... convenient?
Zh: “不过你考虑一下：西奥很出名，或者应该说因为他们的间谍技术臭名昭著。你难道不觉得这样很……便利吗？”

En: Precisely. Seol is famous, or shall I say, infamous, for its spy tech. Don't you find it... convenient?
Zh: “就是这样。西奥很出名，或者应该说因为他们的间谍技术臭名昭著。你难道不觉得这样很……便利吗？”

En: But Seol makes their technology available for others.
Zh: “但是西奥把他们的技术分享给其他人了。”

En: I find it alarming.
Zh: “确实很让人担忧。”

En: It's only logical. Seol is known for its advanced technology and exports whatever is most profitable.
Zh: “这只是你的逻辑。西奥最出名的就是先进的技术，还有出口最赚钱的东西。”

En: Yes, it's the perfect way to create a false sense of security. They sell us fancy technology and then send in their experts, who pretend *not* to be experts...
Zh: “没错，这种完美的方式创造出一种虚假的安全感。他们把花哨的技术卖给我们，派出自己的专家——假装*不是*专家的专家……”

En: They play their violin and cello -- oh, we like your music! -- or work in unrelated fields, like, say, law enforcement, where they have access to a *lot* of sensitive data...
Zh: “他们奏起小提琴和大提琴——哦，我们喜欢你们的音乐！——或者是在不相关的领域工作，比如说，执法部门，这样他们就能获得*大量*的敏感数据……”

En: ...and use their superior technology to blackmail, mislead, and manipulate, turning high-ranking people in government and corporations into Seolite agents.
Zh: “……然后用他们出众的技术去勒索、误导、操纵，把政府和公司高层变成西奥间谍。”

En: Yes. They sell us fancy technology and then send in their experts, who pretend not to be experts...
Zh: “是的。他们把花哨的技术卖给我们，派出自己的专家——假装不是专家的专家……”

En: I'm not sure I like what you're implying, but go on.
Zh: “我不确定自己喜不喜欢你的暗示，不过你先继续吧。”

En: Well, you see... They sell us fancy technology and then send in their experts, who pretend not to be experts...
Zh: “呃，你也看见了……他们把花哨的技术卖给我们，派出自己的专家——假装不是专家的专家……”

En: Could it be that someone is listening in on *this* conversation? Are there bugs in the reeds -- not only insects, but tiny Seolite bugs -- or maybe even some of the bugs *are bugged themselves*...
Zh: 会不会有人正在监听*这次*的对话？芦苇丛中的这些虫子——也许不仅仅是昆虫，而是微型西奥昆虫——或者，也许有些虫子本身就在*窃听它们自己*……

En: I work as a special courier. You know, urgent deliveries, overnight deliveries, deliveries to out-of-the-way locations.
Zh: “我是一名特殊速递员。你知道的，紧急配送、夜间配送、偏远地区配送。”

En: So you deliver things. What kinds of things?
Zh: “所以你是送东西的。具体是什么东西呢？”

En: Oh, I don't know the contents, officer. Part of my job is discretion.
Zh: “哦，我不知道里面的内容，警官。我工作的一部分就是谨慎。”

En: He's trying too hard to seem untroubled by your question. The rigidity in his posture gives him away.
Zh: 他很努力地表现出没有被你的问题困扰的样子。但是他笨拙的姿势暴露了自己。

En: Do you deliver guns?
Zh: “你会送枪吗？”

En: Do you deliver drugs?
Zh: “你会送毒品吗？”

En: No, no, that's far too dangerous. Besides, dealing drugs isn't for people like me and you, officer.
Zh: “不不，没有，那也太危险了。再说了，贩毒可不是你我这种人能当的，警官。”

En: No, nothing like that. I leave that to companies with hundreds of years of tradition in arms manufacturing. No need for an amateur like me cutting in.
Zh: “不，没有那种东西。这种工作还是留给那些百年历史的传统武器制造商吧。不需要我这种业余爱好者搅合。”

En: Oh, I guess that's okay then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “哦，那差不多就这样吧。”（总结。）

En: He smiles broadly.
Zh: 他张大嘴笑了。

En: C'mon, you've already behaved suspiciously with the trash container business, stop evading the question
Zh: “拜托，垃圾箱的事，你已经表现地有点可疑，别再逃避问题了。”

En: Come on, man. You're not under suspicion yet, but you will be if you keep evading questions.
Zh: “拜托，伙计。你还不在嫌疑人名单上，不过如果你再这么逃避问题的话，那就说不定了。”

En: He sighs. "Okay, fine, you got me. I'm a special *topping pie* delivery courier."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“好吧，好吧，被你抓到了。我是个特殊的*馅饼*速递员。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: You heard me. I deliver topping pies. It's temporary. I'm looking for another job. Not many jobs for good men out there these days.
Zh: “你没听错。我配送馅饼。不过是临时的。我正在找其他工作。现如今，适合好人的工作可不太多了。”

En: They're wheat-free. And vegan. And... *huge*. He pauses.
Zh: “它们不含小麦，而且是素食。而且还很……*巨大*。”他停顿了一下。

En: Sure. If you want, I could get you one for free if we ever meet in the city, officer. Wheat free. Just ask me later.
Zh: “当然。如果你想要，什么时候我们在城里碰见的话，我可以免费送你一个，警官。不含小麦。以后再问我吧。”

En: Do you deliver letters to the secret mistresses of corporate and government officials?
Zh: “你会给公司高层和政府官员的秘密情人送信吗？”

En: What do I look like, a pansy? Besides, that kind of cavorting goes against the community values that would strengthen our city.
Zh: “我看起来像什么，同性恋吗？再说了，那种与社会价值观背道而驰的欢愉可以让我们的城市变得更坚强。”

En: You said you deliver *special* topping pies. What makes these topping pies special?
Zh: “你说自己配送*特殊*馅饼。那这些馅饼为什么特殊呢？”

En: He's thinking of a way to gain some advantage from his embarrassing situation.
Zh: 他在想办法，想从这个尴尬的境地中获取一点优势。

En: It makes sense that a technologically advanced nation that produces a lot of *microtechnology* would have foreign governments as customers. That in itself doesn't prove anything.
Zh: 一个拥有先进技术的国家，生产大量*微型技术*卖给外国政府是很合理的。这本身证明不了什么。

En: We must put an end to this. What can I do?
Zh: “我们必须阻止这一切。我能做点什么？”

En: I don't buy this theory.
Zh: “我不相信这个理论。”

En: I don't know how I feel about the Seolites knowing everything about me...
Zh: “如果西奥人知道我的一切，我不知道应该有什么感觉……”

En: Gary, I feel like you bring out the racist in me.
Zh: “盖理，我觉得你把我内心深处的种族歧视给勾出来了。”

En: He leans in so close you can feel his lukewarm breath against your jaw. "Just do your part as an officer of the RCM."
Zh: 他靠得很近，你的下巴可以感觉到他温热的呼吸。“只要做好你在RCM的本职工作就好。”

En: He pouts. "Most people don't... until it's too late. You have been warned. Do with this warning what you will."
Zh: 他有些闷闷不乐。“大部分人都不这么想……直到一切变得无法挽回。我已经警告过你了。随你怎么看待我的警告吧。”

En: Terrifying, right? He scowls. "But we've gotta keep doing the good work, officer. One day, when we're stronger..."
Zh: “很可怕，不是吗？”他怒目圆睁。“不过我们必须继续做好自己的工作，警官。等到有一天，我们变得足够强大……”

En: Officer, believe me, it's the Seolites who are the racists. They consider themselves superior to the rest of us -- and, thus, entitled to pull the strings of power around the world.
Zh: “警官，相信我，西奥人才是真正的种族主义者。他们觉得自己比其他人优越——而且有资格享有掌控整个世界的权利。”

En: You're blowing this out of proportion. The Seolites have better things to do.
Zh: “你把这件事过分夸大了。西奥人还有更重要的事要做。”

En: This is a lot of new information. I'll have to think about it, consult with my partner...
Zh: “新信息太多了。我要好好消化，跟我的搭档商量一下……”

En: Better things than take over the whole world on the sly? Sure you tell yourself that. He looks around, shadily. "Our talk here is concluded. It's not *safe* to discuss this anymore."
Zh: “比偷偷掌控整个世界更重要的事吗？当然了，你可以这么自我安慰。”他怀疑地看着周围。“我们这次对话该结束了。继续讨论下去会*不安全*的。”

En: Please, be careful! He waves his hands energetically. "Don't let on that you know about the Seolite Conspiracy. Who knows what he might do to you..."
Zh: 他用力地挥舞着双手。“请一定要小心！别暴露出你已经知道西奥的阴谋了。谁知道他会怎么对你……”

En: Lets talk about the mug-trash situation again.
Zh: “我们再说说垃圾箱里那个杯子的事吧。”

En: Is this your mug? (Hold up the Yellow Man Mug.)
Zh: “这是你的杯子吗？”（举起黄皮人杯子。）

En: My... mug? Why would you think that?
Zh: “我的……杯子？你为什么会这么想？”

En: So, Gary, you live nearby. In an apartment in Martinaise? (Point in its direction.)
Zh: “所以，盖理，你住在附近。在马丁内斯的公寓里吗？”（指向公寓的方向。）

En: I think I see where you're going with this...
Zh: “我想我好像明白你的意思了……”

En: And keep an eye on that partner of yours. He lets the silence linger, then concludes: "Good talk."
Zh: “还要注意你的搭档。”片刻寂静之后，他总结道：“我们真是相谈甚欢。”

En: He's disappointed in you and sees no point of arguing further.
Zh: 他对你很失望，认为没有必要再争论下去了。

En: In the meantime, keep an eye on that partner of yours... He lets the silence linger, then concludes: "Good talk."
Zh: “同时，记得盯紧你的搭档……”片刻寂静之后，他总结道：“我们真是相谈甚欢。”

En: He's disappointed in you and sees no point of arguing further.
Zh: 他对你很失望，认为没有必要再争论下去了。

En: His eyes widened at the sight of the mug. He's seen it before alright.
Zh: 一看见杯子，他的眼睛都瞪大了。他之前肯定见过。

En: You said 'Yellow Man'. That's not something many people go around saying.
Zh: “你说‘黄皮人’。这里可没多少人会说这种话。”

En: It seemed as if you were calling to it *longingly* when you cried 'Yellow Man'!
Zh: “当你喊出‘黄皮人’的时候，感觉就好像你非常*渴望*这样呼唤。”

En: I can *see* you recognize it. It's in your eyes.
Zh: “我可以*看出*你认得它。都写在你的眼睛里了。”

En: You look like the kind of guy who might have a *collection* of mugs like this. Home -- in his *colonial mug collection*.
Zh: “你看起来像是会*收藏*一整套这种杯子的人。就放在家里——*殖民地杯子系列*里。”

En: Really? I hear it all the time. All in jest, of course. No offence meant to anyone.
Zh: “真的吗？我总是听见别人这么叫啊。当然了，开个玩笑。不是有意想冒犯谁。”

En: Oh, no, that's not... why would I be calling to a broken mug?
Zh: “不，不会的……我为什么要对着一只破杯子喊呢？”

En: I may have had a similar-looking mug in the past, that's all.
Zh: “我过去也许是有类似模样的杯子，但也仅此而已。”

En: Just admit it, man. You put the mug in the trash container behind the Whirling.
Zh: “承认吧，伙计。是你把杯子丢进飞旋旅社背后的垃圾箱的。”

En: Alright, I believe you. You look like the kind of man who knows it's a *crime* to lie to an officer.
Zh: “好吧，我信你。你看起来像是那种知道对警官撒谎是*犯罪*的人。”

En: Maybe... okay, yes. I did. I know I shouldn't have, and I am very sorry, officer. He pauses. "You're not going to fine me, are you?"
Zh: “也许……好吧，是我做的。我知道不应该这样，真的很对不起，警官。”他停顿了一下。“你不会罚我款吧？”

En: Okay, okay, I admit it. I threw the mug away in the trash container behind the hostel. I know I shouldn't have, and I am very sorry, officer. He pauses.
Zh: “好吧，好吧，我承认。是我把杯子丢进了旅社后面的垃圾箱。我知道不该这么做，而且我真的感到很抱歉，警官。”他停顿了一下。

En: You're not going to fine me, are you?
Zh: “你不会罚我款的，对吧？”

En: How do you mean? Forgive me, officer, but we've only just met.
Zh: “你这话是什么意思？请原谅，警官，我们才刚刚见面啊。”

En: He is trying to avoid lying to you outright in case you really have been to his apartment.
Zh: 他试图避免直接对你撒谎，以免你真去过他的公寓。

En: You're acting kind of suspicious. Did I mention the mug was found at the scene of a lynching?
Zh: “你的行为有点可疑。我有没有提过在私刑现场找到一个马克杯？”

En: Still seems suspicious. Did I mention the mug was found at the scene of a lynching?
Zh: “还是很可疑。我有没有提过，这个杯子是在一个私刑现场找到的？”

En: How did you get into the trash container?
Zh: “你是怎么打开垃圾箱的？”

En: Gary, did you put the clothes of a murder victim -- the man who was hanged behind the Whirling-in-Rags -- into that trash container?
Zh: “盖理，你有没有把受害者的衣服——就是吊在褴褛飞旋背后的那个男人——丢进垃圾箱？”

En: Let's move on -- for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我们先继续吧——以后再说。”（总结。）

En: I know a guy who works with trash collection services -- CS Municipal. He gave me a master key for the trash containers of Martinaise.
Zh: “我认识一个人，他在垃圾收集服务工作——CS市政。他给了我一把马丁内斯所有垃圾箱的万能钥匙。”

En: Officer, please... He raises both hands. "Let me explain. It's not like *that...*"
Zh: “警官，拜托……”他举起双手。“请听我解释。不是*那样*的……”

En: Why would you need to get into everyone's trash?
Zh: “你为什么要管别人的垃圾呢？”

En: So I can use the Whirling's trash compactor to store my own stuff, he says, bowing shamefully like a fallen knight. "Garbage disposal is expensive as hell, the damn Himeans run it like a mob..."
Zh: “这样我就可以用飞旋旅社的垃圾压缩机来储存我自己的东西，”他像个堕落的骑士一样，羞愧地弯下腰说到。“垃圾处理贵的跟鬼一样，该死的希米恩搞得像抢劫一样……”

En: Do.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Disgraced? The lieutenant raises his eyebrows and looks up. "No need for the histrionics, sir. It was, after all, just a trash container."
Zh: “蒙羞？”警督扬起眉毛，抬起头。“没必要这么装腔作势的，先生。毕竟这只是个垃圾箱。”

En: What... sound?
Zh: “什么……声音？”

En: That clinking I just heard when you moved.
Zh: “你刚才动的时候，我听到一阵叮当声。”

En: Don't mess with me. I think you know what I'm talking about.
Zh: “别惹我。我想你知道我在说什么。”

En: I haven't the slightest. There's lots of weird *sounds* in a bar like this, though -- pinball, glasses clinking. Could be someone playing pinball?
Zh: “我绝对没有。不过这种酒吧总会有很多奇怪的*声音*——弹球啊，玻璃的叮当声。也许是有人在玩弹球？”

En: I haven't the slightest. There's lots of weird stuff out here in the reeds, though -- insects, trash. Could be the wind shifting some garbage nearby.
Zh: “我绝对没有。不过芦苇丛里总会发生很多奇怪的事——昆虫，垃圾。也许是风把附近的垃圾吹动了。”

En: Really? He fans his arms out slowly, and, this time, his motions are soundless.
Zh: “真的吗？”他慢慢地张开双臂，这一次，他的动作无声无息。

En: There's lots of weird *sounds* in a bar like this -- pinball, glasses clinking. Could be someone playing pinball?
Zh: “不过这种酒吧总会有很多奇怪的*声音*——弹球啊，玻璃的叮当声。也许是有人在玩弹球？”

En: There's lots of weird stuff out here in the reeds, though -- insects, trash. Could be the wind shifting some garbage nearby.
Zh: “不过，芦苇丛里确实有很多稀奇古怪的东西——昆虫啊，垃圾啊。也许是风把附近的垃圾吹动了。”

En: Every day, the wind shifts the reeds and whatever was left in them: tambourines and condom wrappers, plastic and glass bottles, the smell of decay.
Zh: 每一天，风吹动芦苇，还有那些留在芦苇丛中的东西：铃鼓、避孕套包装袋、塑料，还有玻璃瓶。到处都是腐烂的味道。

En: You're a simple cop asking a simple question. He can't beat around the bush much longer.
Zh: 你是个简单的警察，只会问简单的问题。他不可能继续这样拐弯抹角了。

En: Hello! I'm Gary. Very generous of you to help us out, officer.
Zh: “你好啊！我是盖理。非常感谢你的帮助，警官。”

En: Once more you hear that odd clicking sound coming from Gary.
Zh: 你又一次从盖理身上听到了那种古怪的叮当声。

En: Sure do, officer.
Zh: “当然了，警官。”

En: Have you found your door open lately?
Zh: “最近发现你家门被开过吗？”

En: And you're a big Man from Hjelmdall fan?
Zh: “所以你是*来自赫姆达尔的男人*的狂热粉丝？”

En: Mr. Claire must be very angry with you.
Zh: “克莱尔先生肯定对你很生气。”

En: I think I broke into your apartment. I'm very sorry.
Zh: “我想我好像闯进你的公寓了。我很抱歉。”

En: His eyes narrow slightly. He's wondering where this is going.
Zh: 他的眼睛微微眯起，在想这到底是怎么回事。

En: In my home, yes, when I was going to... His face turns pale. "How did you *know?*"
Zh: “在我家里，没错，当时我正要……”他的脸色变得苍白。“你怎么*知道*的？”

En: How...? The colour drains from his face.
Zh: 他的脸变得毫无血色。“你怎么知道……？”

En: Mr. Evrart...? The colour drains from his face.
Zh: 他的脸变得毫无血色。“艾弗拉特先生……？”

En: What?! He turns pale.
Zh: “什么？！”他的脸色变得苍白。

En: Mr. Evrart Claire thought it necessary to unlock your apartment.
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔先生觉得有必要打开你的公寓。”

En: Mr. Evrart Claire thought it necessary to unlock your apartment.
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔先生觉得有必要打开你的公寓。”

En: Yes, he insisted that I open the door to your apartment.
Zh: “是的，他坚持让我打开你公寓的大门。”

En: Yes, Mr. Claire really wanted me to open your door. And then I couldn't help looking around. Please accept my apologies.
Zh: “是的，克莱尔先生很想让我打开你家大门。然后我情不自禁地看了一圈。请接受我的道歉。”

En: I opened it for Mr. Claire. Figured I'd also take a look around.
Zh: “我是为克莱尔先生开的。而且我自己也得看看。”

En: I saw the poster in your apartment. You know, I'm a Hjelmdallermann fan myself.
Zh: “我看见了你公寓里的海报。你知道的，我自己也是赫姆达尔的粉丝。”

En: I saw that ridiculous poster in your apartment, along with all your other colonialist memorabilia.
Zh: “我看见你公寓里那张可笑的海报了，还有你的其他殖民地收藏品。”

En: I had to. For Mr. Claire. I'm a bad cop, I know, but I felt like I had to do it...
Zh: “我必须这么做，为了克莱尔先生。我是个坏警察，我知道，但是感觉我必须这么做……”

En: Mr. Claire unlocked my apartment?
Zh: “克莱尔先生打开了我的公寓？”

En: Mr. Claire unlocked my apartment?
Zh: “克莱尔先生打开了我的公寓？”

En: But... why were you in my apartment, officer?
Zh: “但是……你为什么要闯进我的公寓，警官？”

En: No, I did. Took a look around, too.
Zh: “不，是我做的。而且我还看了一圈。”

En: No, *I* did, per his request.
Zh: “不，是*我*做的，按照他的要求。”

En: No, I did. Don't worry, I didn't go in. (Lie.)
Zh: “不，是我做的。别担心，我没进去。”（撒谎。）

En: So you work for Evrart Claire! He realizes what's going on and changes his tone: "Officer, please tell him we're good. No, no, tell him I'll make it up to him..."
Zh: “所以你是为艾弗拉特·克莱尔工作的！”他终于意识到问题所在，马上变换了口气：“警官，请告诉他我们很好。不，不，告诉他我会补偿他的……”

En: Mr. Evrart Claire asked me to get your door open. I figured I'd take a look around while I was at it.
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔先生让我把你家门打开。所以我就想，进去的时候不妨看一圈。”

En: Just doing Mr. Claire a small favour.
Zh: “只是帮克莱尔先生一个小忙。”

En: What have I done? He'll send the muscle after me... The man looks around, whispering, he makes sure no one hears you talk.
Zh: “我到底做了什么？他会派打手来追我的……”男人一边低语，一边四下张望着。他想确定没人听到你说的话。

En: As he lowers his tone he hunches his back.
Zh: 他弓起背，压低了嗓子。

En: That shirt looks *very* uncomfortable on him. Look at the buttons, barely keeping that thing together -- as if something is ready to *rip out* from underneath.
Zh: 他身上那件衬衫看起来*非常*不舒服。看看那些扣子，勉强搭在一起——就好像有什么东西已经准备好要从下面把它们*扯掉*一样。

En: Okay, he keeps shifting uncomfortably, but you can't make head or tail of this sound -- and honestly it's rude to stare, so stop.
Zh: 好吧，他还在不自在地扭动，但你还是弄不清楚这到底是什么声音——老实说，这样盯着人家看很不礼貌，所以还是停下来吧。

En: I see you're a connoisseur of high-quality combat gear.
Zh: “我发现你是高品质战斗装备方面的行家。”

En: It sounds like he's wearing some kind of armour under his clothes -- you can't tell it's there just from looking at him. Probably stolen from the corpse in the yard near where he lives.
Zh: 听起来他像是在衣服下面穿了一些盔甲——光这样看着，你也没法看出个所以然来。也许是他从自己家附近院子里那具尸体上偷来的。

En: Is he? He's looking comfortable enough -- maybe it was just beads? Sounded like beads. But what kind of beads might a man like Gary be hiding beneath his clothes?
Zh: 他有吗？他看起来挺舒服的——也许真的只是串珠？听起来像是串珠的声音。但是像盖理这样的男人，到底会在衣服下面藏着怎样的串珠呢？

En: Gary, are you cross-dressing by any chance?
Zh: “盖理，你不会刚好有异装癖吧？”

En: Why would I do that, officer? He furrows his eyebrows.
Zh: 他皱紧眉头。“我为什么要这么做，警官？”

En: Are those prayer beads I keep hearing?
Zh: “我听见的是念珠的声音吗？”

En: Are you currently sporting some *anal beads*?
Zh: “你现在是不是在玩*肛珠*？”

En: I don't pray, officer. Faith in non-existent helpers is a sign of weakness. Not for proper Revacholian men such as ourselves.
Zh: “我不祈祷的，警官。信仰不存在的帮手是一种脆弱的标志。对于我们这样的瑞瓦肖爷们来说，是不可能的。”

En: The lieutenant leans in confidentially. "You wouldn't be able to hear if he were wearing anal beads," he whispers.
Zh: 警督悄悄探过身来。“如果他戴着肛珠，你是不可能听见的，”他低语到。

En: Of course, of course. He forces a grin, no doubt hiding some *colossal* anal beads up his butt as he does so.
Zh: “当然，当然了。”他挤出一个微笑，毫无疑问，他确实在后门里藏了些*巨大*的肛珠。

En: Please excuse my colleague's humour. Working this murder case is taking a toll on us both. You don't *quite* have to answer that -- yet.
Zh: “请原谅我同事的幽默。调查这起凶杀案给我们俩造成了很大的影响。其实你*不用*回答的——暂时不用。”

En: To feel closer to the fair sex. Ain't no shame in it, man!
Zh: “感觉更接近女性一些。没有什么好羞愧的，兄弟！”

En: Because you're a dangerous subversive, obviously.
Zh: “很明显，因为你是个很危险的破坏分子。”

En: To explode the gender binary. You should be more open about your views, though. Let those beads hang out.
Zh: “为了打破性别的二元对立。不过，你应该更开放地表达自己的观点。让那些串珠露出来一点。”

En: But, officer… He shakes his head slowly. "I'm not wearing any women's clothes."
Zh: 他慢慢摇头。“但是，警官……我没有穿女人的衣服啊。”

En: Good one. Good one. Joking around again, officer. He forces a grin. "You really do have a courageous sense of humour."
Zh: “不错。很不错。你又开玩笑了，警官。”他挤出一丝笑容。“你的幽默感还真是大胆啊。”

En: Good one. Yeah, good one officer. He forces a grin. "You really have a courageous sense of humour."
Zh: “不错。是啊，这笑话很不错，警官。”他挤出一丝笑容。“你的幽默感还真是大胆啊。”

En: I knew you'd figure it out, officer. He sighs heavily. "I'm sorry I didn't tell you at once. I was..." He unbuttons the shirt.
Zh: 他浑身僵硬，然后重重地叹了一口气。“我就知道最后会被你发现的，警官。很抱歉我没有马上告诉你。我……”他解开衬衫。

En: Gary, what's going on?
Zh: “盖理，出什么事了？”

En: I took the hanged man's cuirass. I... I didn't mean to.
Zh: “我拿走了那个上吊男人的胸甲。我……我不是有意的。”

En: Yellow Man? Interesting. This is something to ask him about, after a little probing first...
Zh: 黄皮人？有意思。这可要问问他了，不过要先试探一下……

En: Yellow Man! That sounds awfully familiar. Something to keep in mind for later...
Zh: 黄皮人！这句话听起来熟悉地可怕。好像是你之前记在心里的东西……

En: What he means is: working on the case is taking a toll on *you*, and working with you is taking a toll on *him*.
Zh: 他的意思是：调查这起案子给*你*造成了不好的影响，而跟你一起工作，对*他*造成了不好的影响。

En: So how about that topping pie?
Zh: “所以馅饼呢？”

En: I thought you'd forgotten! I happen to have one on me right now. He smiles broadly as he walks behind the bar and takes a large, flat box out of a black bag. "It should still be warm."
Zh: “我还以为你忘记了！现在我身上正好有。”他开心地笑了，接着他走到酒吧后面，从一个黑袋子里取出一个又大又平的盒子。“它应该还是热的。”

En: What's on it?
Zh: “上面有什么？”

En: Meatless bacon, avocado, glazed figs, basil, and tiny chunks of pineapple, seasoned with lime zest, freshly ground pepper -- and just a pinch of paprika for the officer!
Zh: “素食培根，牛油果，无花果，罗勒，小块菠萝，再加上青柠皮和现磨胡椒粉调味——为了警官，再专门来一撮辣椒粉！”

En: That sounds too fancy for an officer of the law.
Zh: “对于执法人员来说，好像有点太昂贵了。”

En: Yummm. I can taste it already!
Zh: “嗯。我已经能感受到它的美味了！”

En: What kind of devilry is this?
Zh: “这是什么鬼把戏？”

En: He chuckles. "Not devilry, just Revachol's best topping pie. It's the least I can do for an officer -- and a Union man too!"
Zh: 他咯咯笑了。“不是鬼把戏，是瑞瓦肖最棒的馅饼。这是我至少能为一个警官——而且还是一名工会成员做的！”

En: He chuckles. "Not devilry, just Revachol's best topping pie!"
Zh: 他咯咯笑了。“不是鬼把戏，是瑞瓦肖最棒的馅饼。”

En: He smiles broadly. "Enjoy. It's the least I can do for an officer -- and a Union man too!"
Zh: 他开心的笑了。“请慢用。这是我至少能为一个警官——而且还是一名工会成员做的！”

En: Enjoy. He smiles broadly.
Zh: 他开心的笑了。“请慢用。”

En: Revachol's protectors ought to enjoy Revachol's best topping pie. It's the best I can do for an officer -- and a Union man too! He smiles broadly.
Zh: 他开心的笑了。“瑞瓦肖的守护者就应当享用瑞瓦肖最棒的馅饼！这是我能为一个警官——和一名工会成员能做的最好的事了！”

En: Revachol's protectors ought to enjoy Revachol's best topping pie! He smiles broadly.
Zh: 他开心的笑了。“瑞瓦肖的守护者就应当享用瑞瓦肖最棒的馅饼！”

En: Dark times will do that to good men. He nods gravely, then shifts his gaze to the pile of soggy logs at his feet.
Zh: “黑暗时代会对好人造成这种影响。”他严肃地点点头，然后把目光转向脚下那堆浸水的原木。

En: Yeah, I'm more of a city boy too.
Zh: “是啊，我更像是一个城市男孩。”

En: I like nature, just not this bloody coast. It's mostly drunks and degenerates that come here.
Zh: “我喜欢大自然，只是不喜欢这片该死的海岸。只有酒鬼和瘾君子会来这里。”

En: Sadly, I think I might be a drunk. Or a degenerate. Maybe even both.
Zh: “可惜，我好像就是个酒鬼，或者是瘾君子，甚至两者都是。”

En: Nobody's perfect. I'm sure you've been tempted to drink.
Zh: “人无完人嘛。我肯定你也受到过喝酒的诱惑。”

En: Not a lover of the great outdoors?
Zh: “不喜欢户外活动吗？”

En: Oh, I've been tempted. He nods gravely. "But someone has to stay strong for Revachol." His gaze shifts to the pile of soggy logs at his feet.
Zh: “哎，我也动过这个念头。”他严肃地点点头。“不过总得有人为瑞瓦肖保持一颗坚强的心。”他把目光转向脚下那堆浸水的原木。

En: I'm just waiting for my friend Morell to finish up with his insect traps, he quickly changes the subject, "so we can return to civilization."
Zh: “我在等我的朋友莫雷尔完成昆虫陷阱，”他很快换了个话题，“然后我们就可以返回文明世界了。”

En: I'm just waiting for my friend Morell to finish up with his insect traps so we can return to civilization.
Zh: “我在等我的朋友莫雷尔完成昆虫陷阱，然后我们就可以返回文明世界了。”

En: Always a pleasure to see an officer of the law!
Zh: “很高兴见到你，警官！”

En: He turns toward Kim. "I mean... *officers*."
Zh: 他转向金。“我是说……*警官们*。”

En: Very generous of you to help us out, officer. I'm sure glad to be back from that little excursion.
Zh: “很感谢你的帮忙，警官。如果能结束这次小短途旅行，我一定很会开心的。”

En: Hello again! Very generous of you to help us out, officer. I can't wait to get out of here.
Zh: “又见面了！很感谢你的帮忙，警官。我已经等不及要离开这里了。”

En: Hello, officer! Name's Gary. Boy, am I ever so grateful to you! But I'm not the only one who wants to thank you... He nods toward Lena.
Zh: “你好，警官！我是盖理。天啦，我真是太感激你了！但我不是唯一想感谢你的人……”他朝莉娜的方向点点头。

En: Very generous of you to help us out, officer. I'm sure glad to be back from that little excursion.
Zh: “很感谢你的帮忙，警官。如果能结束这次小短途旅行，我一定很会开心的。”

En: Boy, am I ever so grateful to you, officer! But I'm not the only one who wants to thank you... He nods toward Lena.
Zh: “天啦，我真是太感激你了，警官！但我不是唯一想感谢你的人……”他朝莉娜的方向点点头。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Yellow Man! That sounds awfully familiar. Something to ask about later?
Zh: 黄皮人！这句话听起来熟悉地可怕。也许之后再找个时间问问？

En: Yellow Man! He turns toward Kim. "I mean... officer."
Zh: 他转向金。“黄皮人！我是说……警官。”

En: The lieutenant raises his eyebrows slightly and takes out his notebook.
Zh: 警督微微扬起眉头，拿出了他的笔记本。

En: Yellow Man? Might this be the owner of that mug you found in the trash? This is something to ask him about, after a little probing first...
Zh: 黄皮人？还记得你在垃圾箱里找到的那个马克杯吗，难道他就是主人？一定要好好问问他这个，不过要先试探一下……

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Hello again! Very generous of you to help us out, officer. I can't wait to get out of here.
Zh: “又见面了！很感谢你的帮忙，警官。我已经等不及要离开这里了。”

En: Hello again! How do you do, officer? I'm *still* waiting for Mr. F-ing Field Research over there to be done with his traps.
Zh: “又见面了！你还好吗，警官？我*还在*等那边的野外考察员先生，他的陷阱还没弄完。”

En: I HEARD THAT, GARY!
Zh: “我听见你的话了，盖理！”

En: YOU WERE SUPPOSED TO! Gary grimaces, examining the dirt under his fingernails.
Zh: “你就应该听见！”盖理做了个鬼脸，仔细查看着指甲下面的泥土。

En: Drunks and degenerates -- that's my crew!
Zh: “酒鬼和瘾君子——那不是我的同伴嘛！”

En: The lieutenant raises his eyebrows slightly and takes out his notebook.
Zh: 警督微微扬起眉头，拿出了他的笔记本。

En: Degenerates? I've been trained to identify the slightest hint of degeneracy by the pre-eminent authority on it.
Zh: “堕落的人？我受过权威专家的训练，哪怕是一丁点堕落的迹象，我也能辨认出来。”

En: Oh, uh... I didn't mean it in any *scientific* way. His gaze shifts nervously to the pile of soggy logs at his feet.
Zh: “哦……我的意思没有包含任何*科学*的成分。”他紧张地把目光转移到脚下那堆浸水的原木上。

En: I am neither of those things, I can assure you. I'm a by-the-books, clean-as-a-whistle officer of the law. I'm not even tempted to touch intoxicants.
Zh: “我可以跟你保证，我既不是酒鬼也不是瘾君子。我是干干净净、毫无污点、教科书级的执法人员。甚至连致醉物都无法诱惑我。”

En: He nods gravely. "I've been tempted on occasion. But someone has to stay strong for Revachol." His gaze shifts to the pile of soggy logs at his feet.
Zh: 他严肃地点点头。“我有时也会被诱惑。但是总得有人为瑞瓦肖保持一颗坚强的心。”他把目光转移到脚下那堆浸水的原木上。

En: Degenerates? This man respects authority too much to see the truth inscribed upon thine own visage. Pretend thou art a paragon of virtue.
Zh: 堕落的人？这个人太尊重权威，完全无法看清铭刻在您脸上的真相。假装自己是道德的典范。

En: I really owe you one for getting us out of those reeds, officer. Finally got all that soot out of my hair. Name's Gary.
Zh: “谢谢你把我们从芦苇丛里带出来，这是我欠你的，警官。终于把我头发里那些煤灰清干净了。对了，我叫盖理。”

En: I'll talk to her then. [Leave.]
Zh: “我会跟她聊的。”[离开。]

En: Nobody's perfect. Have you never been tempted to drink?
Zh: “人无完人嘛。你有没有对喝酒动心过？”

En: Oh, I've been tempted. He nods gravely. "But someone has to stay strong for Revachol." His gaze shifts to the floor tiles.
Zh: “哎，我也动过这个念头。”他严肃地点点头。“不过总得有人为瑞瓦肖保持一颗坚强的心。”他把目光转移到了地板上。

En: I've been tempted on occasion. He nods gravely. "But someone has to stay strong for Revachol." His gaze shifts to the floor tiles.
Zh: 他严肃地点点头。“我有时也会被诱惑。但是总得有人为瑞瓦肖保持一颗坚强的心。”他把目光转移到了地板上。

En: Dark times will do that to good men. He nods gravely, then shifts his gaze to the floor tiles.
Zh: “黑暗时代会对好人造成这种影响。”他严肃地点点头，然后把目光转移到了地板上。

En: I like nature, just not this bloody coast. It's mostly drunks and degenerates that come here.
Zh: “我喜欢大自然，只是不喜欢这片该死的海岸。只有酒鬼和瘾君子会来这里。”

En: Sadly, I think I might be a drunk. Or a degenerate. Maybe even both...
Zh: “可惜，我好像就是个酒鬼。或者是堕落的人。甚至两者都是。”

En: I am neither of those things, I can assure you. I'm a by-the-books, clean-as-a-whistle, teetotaling officer. I'm not even tempted to touch intoxicants.
Zh: “我可以跟你保证，我两者都不是。我是干干净净、毫无污点、教科书级的执法人员。甚至连致醉物都无法诱惑我。”

En: Oh, uh... I didn't mean it in any *scientific* way. His gaze shifts nervously to the floor tiles.
Zh: “哦……我的意思没有包含任何*科学*的成分。”他紧张地把目光转移到了地板上。

En: Drunks and degenerates -- that's my crew!
Zh: “醉鬼和堕落的人——那不是我的同伴嘛！”

En: This man respects authority too much to see you for what you are. Pretend thou art a sober man!
Zh: 这个人太尊重权威，完全无法看清您到底是什么样的人。假装自己清醒的人！

En: Degenerates? I've been trained to identify the slightest hint of degeneracy by the pre-eminent authority on it.
Zh: “堕落的人？我受过权威专家的训练，哪怕是一丁点堕落的迹象，我也能辨认出来。”

En: Yeah, I'm more of a city boy too.
Zh: “是啊，我更像是一个城市男孩。”

En: Not a lover of the great outdoors?
Zh: “不喜欢户外活动吗？”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: As he shifts uncomfortably, a series of clicks, like the clinking of glass beads against one another as they roll across a hardwood floor. You've heard this sound before, but where?
Zh: 他不安地扭动着，你听见一阵滴答声，就像玻璃珠子在硬木地板上彼此碰撞的声音。你之前也听过，不过是在哪里呢？

En: As he shifts uncomfortably, a series of clicks, like the clinking of glass beads against one another as they roll across a hardwood floor. A strange sound.
Zh: 他不安地扭动着，你听见一阵滴答声，就像玻璃珠子在硬木地板上彼此碰撞的声音。一种很奇怪的声音。

En: That's basically it. I'm a pie delivery man. How about we change the subject?
Zh: “基本就是这样。我是个馅饼配送员。不如我们换个话题吧。”

En: Seeing if there are any signs of phasmid activity. And taking a leak.
Zh: “看看有没有竹节虫的活动迹象。然后解个手。”

En: But everything's so... moist.
Zh: “但是一切都很……潮湿。”

En: I'd take a bullet for you, but I won't spend another hour out here.
Zh: “我宁愿为你挡子弹，也不愿意再在这里多待一个小时。”

En: Gary, do you know how to turn back time?
Zh: “盖瑞，你知道怎么让时间倒流吗？”

En: Do I know how to turn back time...? He gives you a sideways glance. "Is that a trick question?"
Zh: “我知道怎么让时间倒流吗……？”他瞥了你一眼。“你在给我下套吗？”

En: Are you *still* pursuing this? The lieutenant gives you a tired look.
Zh: “你*还*在纠结这个？”警督疲惫地看了你一眼。

En: The lieutenant crosses his arms and turns to look at you.
Zh: 警督抱着双臂，转身看着你。

En: You'd better be going somewhere case-related with this.
Zh: 你最好把这件事扯到案子上来。

En: What's wrong? Material not good enough to send back to your Seolite masters?
Zh: “怎么了？光凭物质没法让你回到西奥主人那里去吗？”

En: Yes. This is important.
Zh: “是的，这件事很重要。”

En: I'm not going to dignify that with an answer. The lieutenant's voice is cold and official as he opens his notebook. "Make it quick, we're in the middle of a murder investigation."
Zh: “我可不会回答你，不然可太给你面子了。”警督的声音冷酷而官方，他打开了笔记本。“快点吧，我们还在调查谋杀案呢。”

En: More important than our murder investigation?
Zh: “比调查谋杀案还重要吗？”

En: Now *that's* a trick question, right Gary? (Wink at him.)
Zh: “*这*才是给人下套，对吧，盖瑞？”（对他眨眼。）

En: Potentially more important than *anything* ever before.
Zh: “可能比*任何*事都重要。”

En: I guess not.
Zh: “我觉得不是。”

En: Ugh... yeah, yeah. I guess. He shrugs, looking confused.
Zh: “呃……行吧行吧。”他困惑地耸耸肩。

En: Make it quick, remarks the lieutenant dryly and opens his notebook. "And the question was rhetorical, not a trick."
Zh: “快点吧，”警督冷冷地说，并打开了笔记本。“顺便说句，这个问题我是在明知故问，而不是给你下套。”

En: No, the lieutenant replies with a sigh and opens his notebook. "It really isn't. Just please make it quick, so we can get back to our low-priority murder case."
Zh: “不，”警督叹着气回道，然后打开了笔记本。“真的不是。快点吧，我们还有低优先级的谋杀案等着办呢。”

En: In that case please make it extra quick, so we can get back to it, says the lieutenant and opens his notebook. "I mean it."
Zh: “那样的话请务必抓紧，我们好接着回去工作，”警督说着，打开了笔记本。“真的，没跟你开玩笑。”

En: No? he asks, surprised. "Because I could've sworn that's what you're doing right now."
Zh: “不是吗？”他诧异地问道。“我觉得你现在就在干这个。”

En: It *will* make sense, I promise.
Zh: “*一定*是有道理的，我保证。”

En: Just... please, Kim, trust me on this.
Zh: “至少这件事上……请相信我吧，金。”

En: Fine. He opens his notebook and starts leafing through the pages. "Just make it quick. We need to get back to our case."
Zh: “好吧。”他打开笔记本，翻阅起来。“快点吧。我们还得接着办案。”

En: Make what quick? He shrugs. "You're asking me about time travel. Is this some kind of a trick?"
Zh: “什么快点？”他耸耸肩。“你问的可是时间旅行。这是在抖什么机灵吗？”

En: I'm all out of tricks, Gary. Tell me what you know.
Zh: “我可没抖机灵，盖瑞。把你知道的告诉我。”

En: A question for an answer, huh? The answer must be really obvious.
Zh: 用问题来回答你？看来答案一定相当明显。

En: He gave you a question for an answer. Must mean the answer's obvious.
Zh: 他用问题来回答你。看来答案一定很明显。

En: Yes, Gary. A trick. Now humour me.
Zh: “是啊，盖瑞，就是抖机灵。迁就我一下吧。”

En: I don't joke around about time, Gary. (Stare at him intently.)
Zh: “我可不拿时间开玩笑，盖瑞。”（直直地盯着他。）

En: Phew... He scratches his head. "I don't know what to tell you. I mean of course there are *theories* out there..."
Zh: “呼……”他抓了抓脑袋。“我不知道该怎么跟你说。确实是有这种*理论*没错……”

En: The further out, the better.
Zh: 越深入越好。

En: Well, there's a very interesting cryptid known as the Semper Maa. And a certain tribe of ancient kipts who practiced 'digital castigation,' though frankly I don't know how *compelling* you'll find that theory...
Zh: “好吧，有种非常有意思的物种叫做桑帕玛。此外，还有支吉皮特部落会实行‘数字惩戒’，不过说实话，你应该不会觉得这些理论有多*可信*……”

En: And then you hear rumours... He flashes a quick side-glance at the lieutenant. "... that the Seolites possess technology that actually lets them move through time like it's a corridor. Back and forth, you see."
Zh: “然后你听到传言说……”他快速瞥了一眼警督。“……西奥人掌握了可以像时光隧道一样穿越时间的科技。能去也能回。”

En: But then, there are rumours going around that the Seolites have developed a technology that allows them to move through time like it's a corridor. Back and forth, you see.
Zh: “不过，有传言说西奥人掌握了可以像时光隧道一样穿越时间的科技。能去也能回。”

En: The lieutenant stops writing in his notebook for a moment. It's only a fraction of a second, but you catch it.
Zh: 警督在笔记本上写写画画的手顿了一下。虽然只是短短一瞬，但却被你发现了。

En: No. They haven't.
Zh: 不，他们没有。

En: What about something pale-related, any theories?
Zh: “有什么和灰域有关的吗，什么理论都行？”

En: Tell me about this cryptid, the Semper Maa.
Zh: “跟我说说这个叫桑帕玛的物种。”

En: You really think the Seolites can really move *through* time?
Zh: “你真觉得西奥人能*穿越*时间？”

En: What's this 'digital castigation'?
Zh: “‘数字惩戒’是什么？”

En: Gary, *none* of these sound like realistic options. (Conclude.)
Zh: “盖瑞，这些东西*全都*不太现实。”（总结。）

En: I'm not sure these are realistic options. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我觉得这些不太现实。”（总结。）

En: I don't know what to tell you, officer. I got all my information straight from 'Truth Hunt'. If you can't believe a radio programme called 'Truth Hunt', who *can* you believe?
Zh: “我不知该怎么跟你说，警官。我的情报都是从‘真相猎人’那里得来的。如果你不相信名叫‘真相猎人’的电台节目，你*还能*相信谁呢？”

En: Anyone whose livelihood isn't predicated on creating and maintaining preposterous mysteries?
Zh: 有谁不是靠创造和维持荒唐的神秘感来维生的？

En: 'Truth Hunt' is a popular radio programme. Each week, its two eccentric hosts explore a variety of topics, from cryptids and conspiracy theories to ethnic warfare and the 'historical' Man from Hjelmdall. A radio counterpart to the Graadian fringe science magazine 'Paradox B'.
Zh: “真相猎人”是个流行电台节目。两名神秘兮兮的主持人每周都会讨论各种话题，从神秘物种到阴谋论再到种族战争，还有来自赫姆达尔男人的“历史”。跟格拉德的边缘科学杂志《悖论B》差不多的那种电台。

En: Pale-related... he repeats and pauses to think. "An interesting idea, but I can't say anything really comes to mind, no."
Zh: “和灰域有关啊……”他重复着，然后思考起来。“有意思，但我什么也没想到。没有。”

En: *Cryptids*, actually. It's a so-called 'colonial animal', presenting as a large singular body, when in actuality, it's a congregation of tiny organisms working in unison.
Zh: “应该说是*物种们*。那是一种所谓的‘集群生物’，看起来像有一个巨大的身体，但实际上则是无数微生物的集合。”

En: It's found in the subtropical jungles of Samara. Extremely difficult to spot as it appears to the untrained eye like common mud. It's thought to be impossible to catch and *very* dangerous.
Zh: “是在萨马拉的亚热带雨林里发现的。在普通人眼里，它就像是普通的泥巴，因此很难发现。据说它很难捕捉，而且*极其*危险。”

En: Wait, it looks like mud?
Zh: “等等，看起来像泥巴？”

En: Yes, it blends in with the jungle floor. Unsurprisingly, it's become the subject of many local myths -- some have even described it as an angry forest spirit or a manifestation of the forest itself.
Zh: “是的，它与丛林的地面融为了一体。难怪很多当地神话都以它为主题——甚至有人将它描绘成愤怒的森林精魂或者是森林本身的具象化。”

En: What if *you* only appear as a large singular body, are actually a congregation of tiny organisms working in unison?
Zh: 如果*你*其实也只是看起来是一个巨大的身体，而实际上是由无数共同运作的微型器官组成的呢？

En: Get out of here, dreamer! Don't you think we'd know about it?
Zh: 行了，别做白日梦了！你以为我们不知道吗？

En: If it were true those organisms would *not* be working *in unison*.
Zh: 如果真是这样，那这些器官可*不会**共同*运作。

En: Shut up, we can't hear what he's saying!
Zh: 闭嘴，我们都听不见他说话了！
