En: That's because some them just don't have the best interests of the colony in mind.
Zh: 那是因为某些人不懂得什么叫大局为重。

En: Hey, maybe if the rest of you took a chill-pill every now and then, they'd be more motivated?
Zh: 喂，如果你们这些人时不时吃点镇静药，他们就会更有动力？

En: How dangerous is it?
Zh: “有多危险？”

En: I once heard a story about a whole village disappearing overnight, without a trace. Can you imagine that? He shakes his head. "Just a large patch of mud where the settlement was."
Zh: “我听说有个村子在一夜之间消失得无影无踪。你能想象吗？”他摇摇头。“原先的村落只剩下一大团泥巴。”

En: Whole area is completely abandoned now. Locals won't even go near it. They swear the mud writhes and occasionally grows strange shapes.
Zh: “整片区域现在都被遗弃了。当地人甚至都不会接近那里。他们发誓说看到泥巴在扭动，偶尔还会变成奇怪的形状。”

En: Yes, vividly.
Zh: “嗯，非常生动。”

En: No, I'm sorry, but I can't.
Zh: “抱歉，我想象不出来。”

En: I don't *want* to imagine that.
Zh: “我压根*不想*去想象。”

En: (Scoff.) "I've seen the sky cry blood while everything turns to vapour. I think I can manage, Gary."
Zh: （嘲笑。）“我甚至见过天空泣血，让一切化为蒸汽。我想象得到，盖瑞。”

En: Ahm... He's so surprised he even forgets to close his mouth. "That's... yes, that's also horrible. I mean in a much grander scale. Exactly what happened to that village."
Zh: “咳……”他甚至惊到忘记闭上嘴。“那……确实很可怕。我的意思是，就像是那个村子遭遇的惨剧扩大到了更多的地方。”

En: This event will be beyond all scales.
Zh: 这件事将扩大到所有的地方。

En: I asked you about temporal manipulation, not mud.
Zh: “我问的是操纵时间的事情，不是泥巴。”

En: Jungles, villages, mud... he better not be wasting your time.
Zh: 丛林，村镇，泥巴……他最好别是在浪费你的时间。

En: Gary, are you wasting my time right now?
Zh: “盖瑞，你是在浪费我的时间吗？”

En: No, no, not at all... You see, the Semper Maa has also evolved a curious defensive mechanism. When threatened, it has the ability to revert back to an earlier stage in its own lifecycle. It may even be able to do this *continually*, ad infinitium...
Zh: “不是，完全没有……是这样，桑帕玛还进化出了一种奇特的防护机制。当它受到威胁的时候，就能够倒转时间，回到生命周期的早期阶段。这种能力甚至可以*重复*，也就是无限循环……”

En: So the scary sentient mud is also immortal? That's good news.
Zh: 这么说，这团有知觉的可怕泥巴还是不死不灭的？真不错。

En: Now, here's where it gets *really interesting*. It's believed that the mechanism by which the Semper Maa reverts actually reverses the flow of time itself...
Zh: “这才是*真正有意思*的地方。据说桑帕玛的这种机制实际上会让时间本身倒流……”

En: Hang on, I *still* don't see how this immortal mud monster is gonna help me turn back time.
Zh: “等等，我*还是*没明白这团死不了的泥巴怪怎么能帮我扭转时间。”

En: Okay, I'm convinced. What's our next step?
Zh: “好吧，我相信了。那我们接下来怎么办？”

En: This sounds like a dead end to me, Gary.
Zh: “感觉对我来说行不通啊，盖瑞。”

En: He seems to have an unrealistic idea of the RCM's budget and manpower...
Zh: 他对RCM的预算与人力的想法似乎有点不切实际……

En: I don't think the RCM is gonna go for it, Gary.
Zh: “我觉得RCM恐怕做不到，盖瑞。”

En: That would take forever! Isn't there just like a pill I could take or something...?
Zh: “这一辈子也弄不完！就不能像是直接吃颗药丸什么的吗……？”

En: I haven't really heard of any time-travel pills, officer... He rubs his chin, thinking.
Zh: “我真没听说过什么时间旅行药丸，警官……”他若有所思地揉着下巴。

En: Oh, yes it can. Easily.
Zh: 哦，当然可以。很简单。

En: It's true that every expedition up to now has failed, and that several have *vanished* without a trace, but the potential is there, officer. It just can't be denied...
Zh: “确实目前为止每一次远征都失败了，而且有几次还*消失*得无影无踪，但确实有这个可能性，警官。这是不容否认的……”

En: To be able to revert back to a previous version of yourself, to the one before all the mistakes... that's why we're here!
Zh: 回到先前的自己，回到没有犯这些错误之前的自己……我们不就是为这个来的吗！

En: Well, first we'd need to secure funding for an expedition, then assemble a team, gather supplies... There's probably a million other little things, but with the resources of the RCM behind you... He shrugs. "It could be feasible."
Zh: “好吧，我们先要为远征筹集资金，然后组建团队，准备补给……可能还有其他一百万件琐碎的小事，但你背后有RCM的资源……”他耸耸肩。“不是没有可能。”

En: Well, you see, the fascinating thing about it is that this temporal reversion is said to extend to anything *touching* the Semper Maa at the moment of reversal.
Zh: “是这样，最奇妙的部分是，在桑帕玛进行回溯的时刻，任何与它*接触*的东西都会受到时间倒流的影响。”

En: So if I cover myself with this sentient mud...
Zh: “所以，如果我把自己包在这团有知觉的泥巴里……”

En: So if I *attack* this sentient mud...
Zh: “所以，如果我*攻击*这团有知觉的泥巴……”

En: Well, if you *cover yourself* in it first, *then* attack it, you could theoretically basically punch your way back to the Franconigerian era!
Zh: “嗯，如果你先*包在里面*，*然后*攻击它，那从理论上来说，你差不多可以回到弗兰考格斯时代了！”

En: Like a diving suit powered by pain.
Zh: 就像以痛苦为能量的潜水服一样。

En: Like a submarine fuelled by suffering.
Zh: 就像以苦难为燃料的潜水艇一样。

En: Like an exo-skeleton powered by fear.
Zh: 就像用恐惧来驱动的外骨骼一样。

En: ... and attack it over and over again, you could theoretically punch your way back to the Franconigerian era!
Zh: “……然后反复攻击它，从理论上来说，你就可以回到弗兰考格斯时代了！”

En: Seems like a dick move.
Zh: “这么干有点缺德啊。”

En: Very well. This Semper Maa will pull my ox-cart through the ages.
Zh: “很好。就让桑帕玛拉着我的牛车穿越时代吧。”

En: There's balance in nature, Gary. Man was not meant to play god.
Zh: “自然界是有平衡的，盖瑞。人不应该扮演神的角色。”

En: Assuming you survive the encounter... But that's way down the line of course...
Zh: “首先你要在遇到它之后活下来……应该说，你在整个过程中都得活下来……”

En: I'm getting there, officer. He smiles. "You see, the Semper Maa has also evolved a curious defensive mechanism. When threatened, it has the ability to revert back to an earlier stage in its own lifecycle. It may even be able to do this *continually*, ad infinitum..."
Zh: “我正要说重点呢，警官。”他微笑着。“是这样，桑帕玛还进化出了一种奇特的防护机制。当它受到威胁的时候，就能够倒转时间，回到生命周期的早期阶段。这种能力甚至可以*重复*，也就是无限循环……”

En: Well... His eyes instinctively dart to the lieutenant before he continues *sotto voce*: "How else did they get so far ahead of everyone in the tech-race?"
Zh: “嗯……”他的目光本能地转向警督，随后用*低沉的声音*说道：“那不然他们是怎么在科技竞赛中领先其他人那么多的？”

En: Oh, definitely! He nod and repeats. "Definitely. How else did they get so far ahead of everyone in the tech-race?"
Zh: “哦，当然了！”他点点头，重复道。“当然了。不然他们是怎么在科技竞赛中领先其他人那么多的？”

En: By working longer and harder than *everyone* else?
Zh: “因为比*其他人*更勤奋、更努力？”

En: By funnelling a larger percentage of their country's budget to research?
Zh: “通过把更大部分的国家预算投在了研究上？”

En: By seeking answers from behind the veil, from the void?
Zh: “因为从面纱之后、从虚空之中寻找答案？”

En: By letting the free market dictate the course of technological progress?
Zh: “因为放任自由市场来推动技术进步？”

En: I'm sure they have a strong central government keeping things running smoothly.
Zh: “他们一定有个强力的中央政府来推进事情顺利进行。”

En: Isolation obviously, keeping Seol for Seolites. Very motivating.
Zh: “显然是因为隔离，把西奥留给西奥人。很有激励作用。”

En: By all joining in solidarity, everyone doing their part to keep the big machine moving?
Zh: “因为大家团结一致，所有人都各尽其责地让国家机器运转起来？”

En: By developing an aesthetics of pure functionality, separated from traditional notions of beauty?
Zh: “因为发展出了一种纯粹功能性的美学，与传统的美学观念分离开来？”

En: Yeah, how *did* you guys get so far ahead? (Turn to the lieutenant.)
Zh: “是啊，你们这些人*到底*怎么领先那么多的？”（转向警督。）

En: Strange, I don't have a clue.
Zh: “奇怪，我也不清楚。”

En: No part of *me* is part of *this* conversation. Oh, no. The lieutenant keeps his eyes fixed on his notebook. "That's a special thing you two are enjoying. With each other."
Zh: “*这些*话题与*我*无关。完全无关。”警督继续盯着他的笔记本。“这是你俩的事情。你们自己聊。”

En: Everything's cool. Nothing wrong with a little speculative inquiry. The man shrugs awkwardly. "But really, officer, how *do* you explain how the Seolites developed such a lead over the rest of us?"
Zh: “一切正常。做一点推测性的询问又没什么。”他尴尬地耸了耸肩。“但讲真的，警官，你到底*怎么*解释西奥人的发展比我们领先那么多？”

En: He's *really* trying to convey how cool and natural this all is for him.
Zh: 他是*极力*想把这一切表达得平静而又自然。

En: Sure, they got the place locked down tighter than a Himean asshole, but that alone? He's shaking his head. "No way, I don't buy it..."
Zh: “是啊，他们把那地方锁得比西米恩人的屁眼还紧，可仅仅是这样吗？”他摇摇头。“不可能，我不信……”

En: Wait, what kind of technological advantages are we talking about?
Zh: “等等，我们说的是什么科技优势？”

En: What's their secret, then?
Zh: “那他们的秘密是什么？”

En: Isn't it obvious? *Someone's* been feeding them a little trickle of tech down from someplace *no-one* else can reach...
Zh: “还不够明显吗？*有人*给他们教授了一点小技巧，这些技巧来自*无人*能抵达的地方……”

En: From the blue door in the Whirling!?
Zh: “来自飞旋旅社那个蓝色的门！？”

En: From the pale?
Zh: “来自灰域？”

En: From the future?
Zh: “来自未来？”

En: The blue...? No, *the future*. The Seolites are helping themselves from the future. And every little incremental improvement they receive in the present has *major* ripples all along the timeline...
Zh: “蓝色的什么……？不是，是*未来*。西奥人从未来帮助过去的自己。而他们现在取得的每一点进步，都会在时间轴上激起*巨大*的涟漪……”

En: That creates a loop, right? The more advanced tech they send here, the more advanced they become in the future, which means that by smuggling ever more advanced tech down to the past... I'm sure you can see where this is heading.
Zh: “这样就是循环了，对吧？他们送到这里的科技越发达，未来的自己就会更加先进，这就意味着能够将更加发达的科技走私到过去……我相信你一定明白这样的结果是什么吧。”

En: Then we have already lost. (Look down at your clenched fist.)
Zh: “那我们已经输了。”（看着自己紧握的拳头。）

En: Gary, that's insane. You are an insane man, sick in the head.
Zh: “盖瑞，这太疯狂了。你一定是疯了，脑子有问题了。”

En:
You lost me there with the loop.	“你这循环把我搞糊涂了。”
0x0100005100006202	Dialogue Text	WESTCOAST / GARY FASCHA DQ	You	Yes, that's the only explanation that makes sense."
Zh: “是啊，这是唯一合理的解释了。”

En: Wait, so how did they start the loop in the first place?
Zh: “等等，所以他们是怎么开始这个循环的？”

En: Sure, the Seolites are hard workers, everyone knows that... He quickly glances at the lieutenant again. "But hard work alone? I don't buy it..."
Zh: “当然了，人人都知道西奥人很努力……”他又瞥了一眼警督。“可是光靠努力？我可不信……”

En: Permission to laugh, officer? He rolls his eyes. "No way, I don't buy it..."
Zh: “我可以笑吗，警官？”他翻了个白眼。“不可能，我可不信……”

En: Absolutely! He nods enthusiastically, shooting a quick glance at the lieutenant. "That definitely *helps*, but that alone? I don't buy it..."
Zh: “这当然没错！”他热情地点点头，快速瞥了一眼警督。“这当然*有所帮助*，可光是这样吗？我可不信……”

En: The man forces out an unpleasant and artificial laugh. "C'mon, officer, you *know* that's not it. No way!"
Zh: 那人刻意挤出一声不悦的笑声。“行了，警官，你自己*清楚*不是这回事。不可能！”

En: I've personally never witnessed a Seolite commit corporate espionage, he says, determined not to glance at the lieutenant. "*However* there are rumours, articles, respected opinions..."
Zh: “我个人从来没有见过西奥人从事间谍活动，”他说着，并且努力不去看警督。“*不过*，也有谣言、文章和可靠消息称……”

En: And he glanced!
Zh: 他还是瞥了一眼！

En: He clears his throat and continues. "That's actually peas, nothing, no amount of corporate espionage would ever would ever put them *so far* ahead of everyone else..."
Zh: 他清清嗓子，继续说道。“这些其实都是无稽之谈。哪有间谍活动会*这样*堂而皇之地放在公众面前谈论的……”

En: You're underestimating corporate espionage, Gary.
Zh: 你小看间谍活动了，盖瑞。

En: From the void? He gives you an intent look and crosses his arms. "I don't think that's it. I think there's a *much* more sinister explanation..."
Zh: “虚空之中？”他抱起胳膊，急切地看了你一眼。“我不明白什么意思。我觉得答案其实*更加*凶险……”

En: Cannibalism?
Zh: 同类相食？

En: Could be, could easily be. He nods enthusiastically, shooting a quick glance at the lieutenant. "I mean it definitely *helps*, but the market alone? I don't buy it..."
Zh: “可能，很有可能。”他热情地点点头，快速瞥了一眼警督。“这当然*有所帮助*，可光是市场吗？我可不信……”

En: Officer, he says with confidence, "the average consumer is a moron. Leave it to them and we'd all be living in fake-bamboo huts, burning carcinogenic incense and playing the bongos. No way that's the reason!"
Zh: “警官，”他自信地说道，“普通消费者都是傻子。要是一切都交给他们，我们就会住在假竹子做成的屋子里，打着邦高鼓，点着致癌的熏香。怎么可能是因为这个！”

En: Officer, wake up. The man snaps his fingers in front of your face. "No *way* that's the reason why they're winning the race!"
Zh: “醒醒吧，警官。”那人在你面前打了个响指。“他们*不可能*是因为这个赢得竞赛的！”

En: Like clockwork, I'm sure, no doubt about it. But is that *really* enough to take such an astronomical lead? I don't think so...
Zh: “就像时钟一样精准，这我承认。但*光*靠这个，能在天文学方面领先那么多吗？我觉得不是……”

En: I don't know... Sounds a bit *incestuous* to me, but sure -- none of my business, I'm not a Seolite. He quickly glances at the lieutenant. "But no way *that's* the reason they're winning the race..."
Zh: “我不知道……感觉听上去像是*乱伦*一样，不过——这跟我没什么关系，我又不是西奥人。”他快速瞥了一眼警督。“但他们能赢得竞赛肯定不是因为*这个*……”

En: Oh, trying to keep only one type of people around you is suddenly 'incestuous', Gary?
Zh: 哦，所以你觉得只和同一人种在一起就算*乱伦*了吗，盖瑞？

En: Save it for the phyrro-heads, he says with a dismissive hand-wave. "No *way* that's the reason!"
Zh: “这话留着给那些嗑药的人说去吧，”他不屑地挥了挥手。“*不可能*是因为这个！”

En: You know, I don't think that's it. He shakes his head. "I bet there's plenty of beauty over there, art and inspiration too, but that's not why they're winning..."
Zh: “告诉你吧，我觉得不是这样。”他摇摇头。“他们确实有不少美丽的艺术与灵感，但这说明不了他们为什么能赢……”

En: I can tell you it isn't something easy and immediately obvious, that's for sure. No way, that would be too easy...
Zh: “这确实不是什么容易的事情，但事情不可能这么简单……”

En: Come on, officer, everybody knows about it. He rubs the back of his head, looking down. "The stuff they've got, most people here can't even *dream* of it..."
Zh: “行了，警官，这谁都知道。”他低着头，挠了挠后脑勺。“他们得到的东西，大多数人甚至根本无法*想象*……”

En: He's just discovered that he can't think of any examples, though.
Zh: 但是他刚刚发现自己举不出任何例子。

En: That's crazy, man.
Zh: “太疯狂了，兄弟。”

En: You think that's crazy, just wait till you learn *how* they've managed to get so far ahead of us...
Zh: “等你明白他们是*怎么*比我们领先这么多的时候，再觉得疯狂也不迟……”

En: No, why would they be... No, I'm talking about *the future*! The Seolites are helping themselves from the future. And every little incremental improvement they receive in the present has *major* ripples all along the timeline...
Zh: “不不，他们为什么会……不是，我说的是*未来*！西奥人从未来帮助过去的自己。而他们现在取得的每一点进步，都会在时间轴上激起*巨大*的涟漪……”

En: *Exactly*, officer. He leans in closer. "The Seolites are helping themselves from the future. And every little incremental improvement they receive in the present has *major* ripples all along the timeline..."
Zh: “*正是*，警官。”他倾过身。“西奥人从未来帮助过去的自己。而他们现在取得的每一点进步，都会在时间轴上激起*巨大*的涟漪……”

En: Give me some examples, Gary.
Zh: “给我举几个例子，盖瑞。”

En: You're right, I can't even begin to imagine what you're talking about.
Zh: “你说得对，我根本想象不出你在说什么。”

En: Believe me, it's there. Technology so advanced it defies description... He's avoiding your gaze.
Zh: “相信我，就是这样。技术先进到语言无法形容……”他避开了你的目光。

En: Oh, you know like... uhm... He exhales loudly and continues to massage his neck. "Stealth aerostatics, blaasma-powered weaponry, all kinds of deep-secret projects..."
Zh: “哦，就比方说……嗯……”他喘了口粗气，然后揉着自己的脖子。“隐形飞机，等离子武器，还有各种各样的绝密工程……”

En: But that's nothing. Just wait till you learn *how* they've managed to get so far ahead of us...
Zh: “那都不算什么。等你发现他们是*怎么*比我们领先这么多的时候……”

En: There's no such thing as blaasma-powered weaponry, because there's no such thing as 'blaasma.'
Zh: 没有什么等离子武器，因为根本没有“等离子”这东西。

En: Within this self-contained logic-bubble, -- a loop -- he is not wrong.
Zh: 就在这个独立的逻辑气泡——一个循环——中，他说得对。

En: I don't know officer... I think Future-Revachol might also have a few aces up its own sleeve. The people are starting to wake up, you see.
Zh: “我不知道，警官……我觉得未来的瑞瓦肖可能手里也有些王牌吧。你看，人们已经开始觉醒了。”

En: Hey man... he spreads his arms wide. "No one likes to hear it, but I'm not here to give you a false sense of security. It is what it is. People are starting to wake up and take notice."
Zh: “喂，兄弟……”他张开双臂。“没人喜欢听这话，但我不想给你虚假的安全感。这就是事实。人们开始觉醒，开始注意到这件事了。”

En: Now *that* is a paradox, he smiles an all-knowing smile, like he just explained everything.
Zh: “*这*才是悖论，”他展露出一个全知全能般的微笑，仿佛自己刚刚解释了一切。

En: A statement, seemingly contradictory or opposed to common sense, and yet perhaps true. Or perhaps not.
Zh: 一种看似矛盾或是违背常理的说法，但却可能是事实。也可能不是。

En: You lost me there with the paradox.
Zh: “你用悖论把我弄晕了。”

En: I'm telling you, he nods confidently. "The truth can't be contained, people are starting to wake up and take notice."
Zh: “我告诉你吧，”他自信地点点头。“真相是包不住的，人们开始觉醒，开始注意到这件事了。”

En: A frustrated sigh. "I've been through this with you. I am *not*..." He changes his mind. "Just please make it quick, we're in the middle of a murder investigation, remember?"
Zh: 一声沮丧的叹息。“我已经和你说过了。我*不是*……”他改变了主意。“快点吧，我们还有谋杀案要办呢，记得吗？”

En: You notice the lieutenant flinch and rub his forehead as if hit by a sudden migraine.
Zh: 你发现警督瑟缩着揉起前额，仿佛突然犯了偏头痛一样。

En: God I hope he meant it sarcastically. If not, I don't really see how he can continue being a police detective.
Zh: 天，我真希望他是在挖苦。不然的话，我真不知道他该怎么继续当警探了。

En: Oh, people *are* noticing, Gary. They've probably *dispatched* a team already. The pen in the lieutenant's hand glides on the rough paper of his notebook.
Zh: “哦，人们*已经*注意到了，盖瑞。他们没准已经*派遣*团队了。”警督手中的笔在笔记本粗糙的纸张上来回动着。

En: Not to the present, of course, to past-Gary. They prefer to deal with problems before they become *unmanageable*. Just something to think about.
Zh: “当然不是对现在的，而是对过去的盖瑞。他们更喜欢在问题*失控*之前处理它们。想想看吧。”

En: Hahaha... His laughter is quick and nervous. "I mean I'm basically just repeating what I heard on 'Truth Hunt' or read in 'Paradox B.' I'm just a messenger, and not even that, really!"
Zh: “哈哈哈……”他急促而紧张地笑着。“我的意思是，我基本上只是在重复在‘真相猎人’里听到或者在‘悖论B’里读到的东西。我只是个传话的，可能传话的都算不上，真的！”

En: And I suppose none of this would help *you* manipulate time personally, in any case. So why don't we just forget this whole business?
Zh: “总而言之，我觉得这没有一样能帮*你*亲自操纵时间。所以我们干脆就把这事忘了吧？”

En: Mmmm... the most beautiful smell in the world, fear.
Zh: 嗯……世界上最美的味道，恐惧。

En: Well, he glances at the lieutenant. "Essentially it's a grief ritual practiced by certain ancient Semenese tribes. Traditionally, after the death of a village leader, the bereaved are supposed to cut off a segment of their own fingers..."
Zh: “嗯，”他瞥了一眼警督。“本质上来说，这是某个远古塞美尼人部落举行的哀悼仪式。按照传统，在村庄领袖死去后，死者的亲属需要割下一部分手指……”

En: Essentially it's a grief ritual practiced by some old kipt tribes. Traditionally, after the death of a village leader, the bereaved are supposed to cut off a segment of their own fingers...
Zh: “本质上来说，这是某些远古吉皮特人部落举行的哀悼仪式。按照传统，在村庄领袖死去后，死者的亲属需要割下一部分手指……”

En: The really sick bit is that they bite off bits of their *children's* fingers too. Even babies. He frowns. "Imagine that."
Zh: “最残忍的地方是，他们还要咬掉*孩童*的手指。甚至连婴儿也不例外。”他皱起眉头。“想想看吧。”

En: What makes it really disturbing is the fact that they bite off bits of their *children's* fingers too. Even babies. He frowns. "Imagine that."
Zh: “最让人不能接受的是，他们还要咬掉*孩童*的手指。甚至连婴儿也不例外。”他皱起眉头。“想想看吧。”

En: Small hut, pregnant with screams of anguish and terror. The lowered head of the mother as she gnaws at the child's joint. Chewing, screams, agony. Finally there's a tiny crunch, followed by the sound of weeping.
Zh: 小小的茅屋中孕育着痛苦与恐惧的尖叫声。母亲低垂着头，啃咬着孩童的关节。咀嚼声，尖叫声，挣扎声。终于，随着一声小小的咔嚓声，接下来便是暴风骤雨般的哭泣声。

En: Agonizing pain...
Zh: 何其痛苦……

En: What does any of this have to do with turning back time, Gary?
Zh: “这些跟扭转时间有什么关系，盖瑞？”

En: You know what? That's enough for me.
Zh: “行了，我听够了。”

En: Yes, *imagine* it!
Zh: 是啊，*想想看*吧！

En: Gary, you're putting images in my head and I don't like it.
Zh: “盖瑞，你让我脑子里有了不喜欢的画面。”

En: I'm getting there. See, the practice is derived from Semenese finger counting. Each segment of the finger represents one hour, so they count up to 12 with one hand, disregarding the thumb, of course.
Zh: “我正准备说呢。是这样，这件事是从塞美尼人的指算法演变出来的。每一个指关节代表一个小时，因此一只手可以代表12个小时，当然大拇指除外。”

En: Absolute nonsense across the board.
Zh: 简直胡说八道。

En: By removing one segment from their fingers, they remove the corresponding hour from their life -- that's what they believe, anyway.
Zh: “从手上去掉一个指节后，他们的人生中也就去掉了对应的时光——至少他们是这么认为的。”

En: None of this is helpful, Gary.
Zh: “这些都没用，盖瑞。”

En: So you're saying if I cut off both my hands I can go back to yesterday?
Zh: “所以你的意思是，如果我把两只手都切了，就能回到昨天吗？”

En: No-no... He laughs nervously. "There's a discrepancy between how these Semenese tribes count and how they tell time."
Zh: “不不……”他紧张地笑起来。“塞美尼人的计数方式与计时方式之间存在差异。”

En: No-no... He laughs nervously. "There's a discrepancy between how the kipts count and how they tell time."
Zh: “不不……”他紧张地笑起来。“吉皮特人的计数方式与计时方式之间存在差异。”

En: As I explained, they count up to 12 on their fingers, but they divide the day into 15 parts. But that's a whole other discussion...
Zh: “就像我刚才说的，他们用手指的12个部分计数，但却把一天分成了15个部分。不过这完全是另一件事了……”

En: One you absolutely don't have time for. Let's be done with this idiocy.
Zh: 是你完全没时间闲扯的事。赶快跟这个白痴说完吧。

En: This wouldn't help me even if it was *remotely* believable.
Zh: “就算这件事有那么*一点*可信，也帮不到我什么。”

En: Gary, you owe me at least a finger or two for all my time you've wasted.
Zh: “盖瑞，你浪费我的时间至少值一两个手指了。”

En: I guess I have to keep looking...
Zh: “看来我还得继续找……”

En: Hey, don't blame me, officer. He puts his hands up. "These are barbaric people and barbaric times we're talking about. Dark magic."
Zh: “喂，这不怪我啊，警官。”他举起手。“我们谈的就是那些野蛮人的野蛮时代啊。是黑魔法啊。”

En: In truth, these sound more like scenes from some old fantastique novel, the product of the author's fevered imagination rather than reality.
Zh: 说实话，这些听起来更像是什么从前的奇幻小说里的场景，完全是作者狂热想象的产物，而不是现实。

En: This guy has proved to be a dead end. (A dissatisfied growl in you abdomen.) But the hard endure and the true kingsman keeps searching. 'Vaillance,' brööder, the night is darkest before the dawn.
Zh: 看来跟这家伙没什么可说的了。（你的腹部发出了一声不满的咆哮。）但真正的王下骑士懂得忍耐，懂得继续寻找。这是“勇气”，是黎明前的黑暗。

En: Please note: Tales of digital mutilation are a common trope of racist pseudo-histories, completely unsubstantiated by the archaeological record...
Zh: 请注意：有关数字惩戒的故事是种族主义者虚构历史的常见手法，这些故事完全没有考古记录的证实……

En: What kind of theories are we talking about here?
Zh: “我们说的是哪种理论？”

En: ... by about 4.5 seconds.
That secret inaccessible bunker near Land's End!?
Zh: “地之角旁边那个进不去的隐秘地堡！？”

En: That old thing...? No, *the future*. The Seolites are helping themselves from the future. And every little incremental improvement they receive in the present has *major* ripples all along the timeline...
Zh: “那个老东西……？不是，是*未来*。西奥人从未来帮助过去的自己。而他们现在取得的每一点进步，都会在时间轴上激起*巨大*的涟漪……”

En: I don't know, this sounds made up to me.
Zh: “不知道，我感觉像是编的。”

En: Hey, you asked me, officer. He puts his hands up. "These are barbaric people and barbaric times we're talking about. Dark magic."
Zh: “喂，是你问我的，警官。”他举起手。“我们谈的就是那些野蛮人的野蛮时代。黑魔法。”

En: Tropes of fantastique literature combined with mundane racial prejudice -- this is what the term 'pseudo-history' was invented to describe.
Zh: 奇幻文学与种族偏见相结合的隐喻手法——所谓“虚构历史”这个词说的就是这种东西。

En: Oh... He looks surprised. "Well, this is embarrassing, but I'm really not sure what else to tell you..."
Zh: “哦……”他看起来有些吃惊。“好吧，有点尴尬，但我真不知道还有什么能跟你说的了……”

En: Oh... He looks surprised. "Well, I suppose there's still the deep-secret Seolite technology to consider."
Zh: “哦……”他看起来有些吃惊。“好吧，至少还可以考虑一下西奥人的绝密科技。”

En: Oh... He looks surprised. "Well, there's still the Semper Maa to consider."
Zh: “哦……”他看起来有些吃惊。“好吧，至少还可以考虑一下桑帕玛。”

En: Listen, there's no problem, officer. There's still the Semper Maa to consider, as well as this advanced Seolite technology.
Zh: “没问题的，警官。至少还可以考虑一下桑帕玛，还有先进的西奥科技。”

En: Unfortunately, I suppose this *still* doesn't help *you* manipulate time personally. He shrugs. "Sorry about that."
Zh: “很遗憾，这可能*依然*没办法帮助*你*亲自操纵时间。”他耸耸肩。“抱歉了。”

En: That said, if you're interested in manipulating time *personally*, I suggest you consider the Semper Maa or digial castigation.
Zh: “这么说的话，如果你想*亲自*操纵时间，那建议你考虑一下桑帕玛或者数字惩戒。”

En: Though I suppose this still doesn't help *you* manipulate time personally. Perhaps you should consider digital castigation?
Zh: “不过，我觉得这可能依然没办法帮助*你*亲自操纵时间。或许可以考虑下数字惩戒？”

En: Though I suppose this still doesn't help *you* manipulate time personally. Perhaps you should consider the Semper Maa?
Zh: “不过，我觉得这可能依然没办法帮助桑帕玛？”

En: And speaking personally, arranging an expedition seems like the most promising option overall, so I'm not really sure what else to tell you...
Zh: “就我个人来说，安排一次远征算是希望最大的选择了，所以我也不知道还有什么能跟你说的……”

En: And personally, it seems easier than accessing deep-secret Seolite technology and more civilised than digital castigation, but to each his own.
Zh: “就我个人来看，这比获取西奥人的绝密科技容易得多，也比数字惩戒文明得多，但各有各的想法吧。”

En: And personally, it seems easier than, say, accessing deep-secret Seolite technology, but to each his own.
Zh: “就我个人来看，这比获取西奥人的绝密科技容易得多，但各有各的想法吧。”

En: And personally, it seems more civilised than, say, digital castigation, but to each his own.
Zh: “就我个人来看，这比数字惩戒文明得多，但各有各的想法吧。”

En: I concur, detective, the lieutenant interjects without looking up from his notes. "It's completely *unfeasible*."
Zh: “我同意，侦探。”警督从笔记本中抬起头，插了一句。“这根本就是*匪夷所思*。”

En: His voice betrays relief, he really didn't want to have to argue with you over this.
Zh: 他的声音中透着欣慰，看来是真的不想跟你争论这件事。

En: Hmmm, what else have you got?
Zh: “嗯，你还有什么办法？”

En: Listen, officer. I know it sounds like quite the task, but the potential, it just can't be denied...
Zh: “我说，警官，我知道这任务听起来很难，但确实有这个可能性，警官。这是不容否认的……”

En: Because he's only now discovering he has no idea what he's talking about.
Zh: 因为他现在才发现根本不知道自己在说什么。

En: How, then?
Zh: “那要怎么办？”

En: Terrifying...
Zh: “真可怕……”

En: It gets even scarier. You see, the Semper Maa has also evolved a curious defensive mechanism. When threatened, it has the ability to revert back to an earlier stage in its own lifecycle. It may even be able to do this *continually*, ad infinitium...
Zh: “其实还有更可怕的。是这样，桑帕玛还进化出了一种奇特的防护机制。当它受到威胁的时候，就能够倒转时间，回到生命周期的早期阶段。这种能力甚至可以*重复*，也就是无限循环……”

En: Why are you so interested in this stick bug? Aren't there more sensational animals out there?
Zh: “你为什么对竹节虫这么感兴趣？不是还有比它更有意思的动物吗？”

En: Khm... The lieutenant interjects. "Just out of curiosity -- if there's no proof of its existence, how do you know it's real?"
Zh: “咳咳……”警督突然插嘴。“纯属好奇——如果没有证据能证明它的存在，你怎么知道它是真的？”

En: Got it. Parthonosis.
Zh: “明白了。单性生殖。”

En: Parthe-no-genesis. Meaning the females don't need males to reproduce. Makes it easier for a species to survive in adverse conditions.
Zh: “是单性繁殖，意思是说，雌性不需要通过交配来繁衍。让物种能够更容易地在恶劣的情况下生存。”

En: Lena designed the traps?
Zh: “莉娜设计了这个陷阱？”

En: Yes, he says with some pride.
Zh: “是的，”他有些骄傲的回答着。

En: Yes. What?
Zh: “可以。你问吧？”

En: Inside the traps, a number of locusts crawl and tumble over one another in a tiny, chittering swarm.
Zh: 一群蝗虫在陷阱里爬行着，围成小小的一团，叽叽喳喳，跌跌撞撞。

En: A meat-eating stick insect? Does it pretend to be the reeds as part of it's ambush behaviour? This seems unlikely.
Zh: 一种食肉的竹节虫？难道它假装成芦苇是伏击行为的一部分？好像不太可能啊。

En: A carnivorous stick insect? Seems unlikely.
Zh: “一种食肉的竹节虫？好像不太可能啊。”

En: Thank you for your opinion. We have also included plant material in the traps -- to satiate your *scepticism*.
Zh: “谢谢你的意见。我们也在陷阱里加入了植物材料——为了满足你的*怀疑论*。”

En: Not a big fan of scepticism, this one.
Zh: 这人，可不是怀疑论的狂热粉丝。

En: By *wöman* you mean my wife, I assume. I will return to her -- but I can't leave before we've finished with these traps.
Zh: “我猜你口中的女人，是指我的妻子吧。我会回去找她的——但是这些陷阱还没完成，我不能离开。”

En: My wife understands that just as well as anyone. He looks south -- where Lena would be.
Zh: 他看向南边——莉娜可能就在那里。“像其他人一样，我的妻子能理解的。”

En: I didn't know the phasmid was so important to Lena.
Zh: “我不知道这个竹节虫对莉娜来说那么重要。”

En: Okay. I understand. I don't give up on things either.
Zh: “好吧。我明白。我也不是轻言放弃的人。”

En: Good. A good quality to have -- both for a police officer *and* an experimental zoologist.
Zh: “很好。这是一种很好的素质——对于一名警察，*还有*实验动物学家来说都是。”

En: Of course it's important to her, she's *seen* it. A verified sighting, on record, one of only four this century and it's hers.
Zh: “对她当然重要了，她*见过*它。本世纪有4次经过证实、被记录在案的目击情报，其中一次就是她的。”

En: Really? She's *sighted* the phasmid? She didn't tell me that.
Zh: “真的吗？她*见过*竹节虫？她没告诉我这个。”

En: Really? She's *sighted* the phasmid?
Zh: “真的吗？她*见过*竹节虫？”

En: Yes. That's... how we first came to know one another, in fact. But that's *her* story to tell, not mine. He coughs, then continues...
Zh: “是的。这就是……其实这就是我们最初了解彼此的契机。但那是*她的*故事，不是我的。”他不停地咳嗽起来……

En: He's dead set on this.
Zh: 他一心想要这么做。

En: ...would be *glory* itself.
Zh: ……这就是*荣耀*本身。

En: I'm *all in* with this cryptid shit. I'm hooked.
Zh: “我算是跟这个鬼神秘动物*杠上了*。我上瘾了。”

En: That it will. The man cracks an unwieldy smile. "That it *absolutely* will. I hope you brought your good boots."
Zh: “会的。”男人挤出一个笨拙的笑容。“*一定*会的。希望你把好靴子带上了。”

En: Caught the bug, I see. He cracks an unwieldy smile. "It's easier to get caught up in the *search* than you'd imagine."
Zh: “捕捉昆虫，我明白了。”他挤出一个笨拙的笑容。“沉迷*研究*要比你想象的容易的多。”

En: My partner was joking, he says, unsure of himself. "We're on a search of our own. It wouldn't hurt to check the reeds while we're at it... I assume that's what we'll be doing?"
Zh: “我的搭档在开玩笑，”他自己的口气都不太确定。“我们自己还有东西要搜查呢。当然，搜查过程中稍微检查一下芦苇也没什么大不了的……我想我们应该会这么做吧？”

En: Oh yes, officer. Very much so. The traps are all in the reeds -- that is where we expect the phasmid to prowl.
Zh: “当然了，警官。的确如此。陷阱都在芦苇丛里——我们期望竹节虫会在那里徘徊。”

En: There are four in total. One is to the south, on this little peninsula -- by the boat houses there. He points south. "It's very near."
Zh: “一共有4个陷阱。一个在这座小半岛的南边——就在船屋附近。”他指向南边。“很近的。”

En: You should check at least one of those before returning to *this* one, since I just set it. This one's more of a *technicality*, but still... better safe and stupid than sorry.
Zh: “你至少要检查其中一个，之后再回到*这里*，毕竟这是我刚布置好的。这更像是个*技术性细节*，不过……安全和愚蠢总比抱歉好。”

En: Another we set in Land's End, to the northeast. It's behind a small sand dune there, on your way to the old radio tower. After the church.
Zh: “还有一个布置在地之角，东北边。就在前往旧无线电塔的半路上，一个小沙丘的背后。就是教堂后面那个。”

En: The third is set near the canal, where you crossed. By a concrete slab. A big thicket of reeds going up the slope, and among them... He gestures to the trap in front of him.
Zh: “第三个在你跨过的运河附近。一块混凝土板旁边。斜坡上有一大片芦苇丛，就在它们中间……”他朝着眼前的陷阱比了个手势。

En: If you think it's important -- you *have* been right before.
Zh: 如果你觉得它很重要——你*之前*也成功过。

En: It *is* precious. A single dose cost me 50 reál to develop. Not that I expect *you* to understand self-financing one's own research. He looks at the lieutenant with disdain, then puts the spray back in his pocket.
Zh: “它*非常*珍贵。开发一小剂就要50雷亚尔。我并不指望*你*能理解为自己的研究自筹经费的行为。”他轻蔑地看着警督，然后把喷壶放回口袋。

En: Wise choice. He douses you with the odd-smelling spray and then gives you a satisfied nod.
Zh: “明智的选择。”他给你用了一点奇怪味道的喷雾，然后朝着你满意地点点头。

En: He douses you with the odd-smelling spray and then gives you a satisfied nod.
Zh: 他给你用了一点奇怪味道的喷雾，然后朝着你满意地点点头。

En: Lay it on me.
Zh: “喷到我身上吧。”

En: Lay it on me -- thick. (Present your arm pit.)
Zh: “喷到我身上吧——浓一点。”（张开你的腋窝。）

En: I don't want this, thank you very much.
Zh: “我不想要这个，非常感谢。”

En: He douses you with the odd-smelling spray -- a double helping as you present your other arm pit -- and then gives you a satisfied nod.
Zh: 他给你用了一点奇怪味道的喷雾——当你露出另一侧腋窝时又加了一点——然后朝你满意地点点头。

En: Your choice. He puts the spray back in his pocket. "As I said, the chances of you encountering the phasmid are next to zero."
Zh: “你自己决定。”他把喷壶放回口袋。“我之前说过了，现在你遇到竹节虫的几率基本为零。”

En: But you... you *feel* you should have it on you. Something bad might happen.
Zh: 可你……你*觉得*你应该把它喷在身上吗。可能会发生不好的事。

En: I don't. It's a profession, just like any other.
Zh: “我不会。这是一种职业，就跟其他的一样。”

En: Honestly -- being a cryptozoologist trumps most of the garbage I've seen people do.
Zh: “坦白说——做一名神秘动物学家，胜过了之前遇见的大部分垃圾职业。”

En: Hmh, a hum and a nod. "My methods do not differ from other scientists, I simply draw upon a wider variety of evidence, and I have more hope that something truly surprising might happen."
Zh: “嗯，”他嗯了一声，点头赞同。“我的方法和其他科学家没什么不同，我只是利用更广泛的证据，而且更希望某些真正惊人的事能够发生。”

En: Indeed. My methods do not differ from other scientists, I simply draw upon a wider variety of evidence, and I have more hope that something truly surprising might happen.
Zh: “确实。我的方法和其他科学家没什么不同，我只是利用更广泛的证据，而且更希望某些真正惊人的事能够发生。”

En: So you have never discovered a cryptid?
Zh: “所以你从没发现过神秘动物了？”

En: Have you ever discovered a cryptid?
Zh: “你有没有发现过什么神秘动物？”

En: No. Very few cryptids are ever discovered -- and not for a lack of trying. To stay hidden is a cryptid's primary quality. It's even in the *name* cryptid.
Zh: “没有。神秘动物很少能被发现——但并不是因为缺少尝试。保持隐蔽是神秘动物的第一属性。甚至连*名字*都很神秘。”

En: So how many cryptids have been found?
Zh: “所以已经有多少神秘动物被你发现啦？”

En: Of the list of cryptids kept by the Cryptozoological Society of Chemnie, which is four thousand and eighty two items long, about two thousand have been confirmed as hoaxes.
Zh: “西尔维亚神秘动物社团保存着一份神秘动物清单，上面一共有4082个条目，其中大约有2000个被证实是恶作剧。”

En: Two are categorized as 'confirmed discoveries'. The rest are in differing stages of discovery, refutation, and data collection.
Zh: “两个被归类为‘确认的发现’。其余的则处于发现、辩驳和数据收集的不同阶段。”

En: Only *two* have proven to be real?
Zh: “只有*两个*被证实是真的吗？”

En: Yes -- the Shautauquan forest pygmy who turned out to be an extinct species of primate; and a cave salamander from Yugo-Graad, who is... honestly quite unremarkable. It's in a zoo somewhere...
Zh: “是的——肖塔戈森林侏儒其实是一种已经灭绝的灵长目动物；南格拉德的洞穴蝾螈，其实……真的挺不显眼的。它现在就在某个动物园里……”

En: We cryptozoologists are brutally honest with ourselves, more so even than the public. Most cryptids are hoaxes or they are never found. That does not mean we should stop searching.
Zh: “我们神秘动物学家对自己诚实地近乎残忍，相比公众是有过之而无不及的。大部分神秘动物都是恶作剧，或者从未被发现。那也并不意味着我们会停止搜寻。”

En: Two out of four thousand is not even *one* percent!
(Nod approvingly.) "Then the Insulindian phasmid will be the *third*."
Zh: （赞许地点点头。）“那伊苏林迪竹节虫就会成为*第三例*。”

En: I don't even know what to say. That's a small number of discoveries.
Zh: “我真是不知道该说什么好。这个发现数量可真够小的。”

En: In fact -- it is 0.05%. Ever more magnificent, should our search contribute to making that number 0.075%!
Zh: “其实——只有0.05%。如果我们的搜寻能够让这个数字变成0.075%，那将会更加地辉煌！”

En: He has clearly done his math on this. There is no surprising him -- or swaying his opinion.
Zh: 他显然在这方面做过计算。这并不会让他感到吃惊——也不会左右他的观点。

En: Do you know what the success rate in pharmacological research is? 0.000003% of bio-reactive agents have reproducibly beneficial effects. Yet science persists in the search for medicine. *As* we persist in our search for new species.
Zh: “你知道药理研究的成功率是多少吗？只有0.000003%的生物反应剂拥有可复制的有益效果。然而科学还在坚持药物研究。*就像*我们还在坚持寻找新物种一样。”

En: Indeed. He does not smile, just looks you in the eye. It's a forceful gaze. "If our expedition is successful, every paper in the world will report on it. From Revachol to Doushantou -- it will be a zoological miracle."
Zh: “确实。”他没有笑，只是盯着你的双眼。目光很有说服力。“如果我们的探险能够成功，世界上的所有媒体都会报道它。从瑞瓦肖到道斯尚托乌——它会成为动物学上的奇迹。”

En: The hair on your arms stands up... electricity. Sounds like reeds hissing.
Zh: 你胳膊上的汗毛竖了起来……仿佛触电一般。听起来就像是芦苇的嘶嘶声。

En: The lieutenant takes a short note in his notebook, then gestures for you to proceed.
Zh: 警督在笔记本里做下简短的笔记，然后做了个手势让你继续。

En: He tries to play it cool -- remain professorial -- but inside, this man is itching for some news on those *traps*.
Zh: 他想要表现地很冷静——保持专业性——但是内心深处，这个男人渴望得到那些*陷阱*的消息。

En: Good, okay... He breaks his calm. "*And?*"
Zh: “很好，好吧……”他打破了平静。“*然后呢*？”

En: And one of them was *empty*.
Zh: “其中有一个是*空*的。”

En: Nothing. Nothing. Maybe I didn't check them after all...
Zh: “没什么。没事。也许我根本就没有检查过……”

En: Sweetie, you'll give Morell a heart attack. This is no laughing matter for us...
Zh: “亲爱的，你快让莫雷尔心脏病发作了。对于我们来说，这可不是什么好笑的事情……”

En: I'm fine, Lena... He coughs. "And I definitely won't have a heart attack. Rather a cough fit..."
Zh: “我没事，莉娜……”他咳嗽起来。“而且我肯定不会心脏病发作的。只是有点咳嗽……”

En: Surprise -- tempered with fear and trepidation. He doesn't know what to think yet. Maybe you're joking?
Zh: 惊喜——伴随着恐惧和忧虑。他不知道该怎么想。也许你在开玩笑？

En: No but really -- I *did* check all the traps.
Zh: “不，但是说真的——我*确实*检查过所有陷阱了。”

En: Yes-yes -- *and*?
Zh: “是吗，是吗——*所以呢*？”

En: Yes. That's *exactly* what it is. What a deft hunter, this phasmid!
Zh: “是的。*的确*如此。这个竹节虫，真是个灵巧的猎人！”

En: Of course -- be *sarcastic*. He misinterprets your words. "Unless you have an *alternative* hypothesis you'd like to venture, mine stands, okay?"
Zh: “当然——你尽管*挖苦*吧。”他误解了你的话语。“除非你有*其他*假设，否则我仍然不会动摇，明白吗？”

En: Actually, no... His tone changes. "Excuse me for getting emotional, this is a big deal for us. You've helped us *twice* now -- and brought some great news, too. My gratitude, *and* the gratitude of the Société Cryptozoologique de Revachol, is yours."
Zh: “事实上，没有……”他的口气有些变化。“请原谅我有些情绪化，这对我们来说是一件大事。你已经帮了我们*两次*——还带来了一些很棒的消息。感谢你，*还有*瑞瓦肖社会神秘动物学会也向你表示感激。”

En: Pardon me. This is... a big deal for us. You've helped us *twice* now -- and brought great news too. My gratitude, *and* the gratitude of the Société Cryptozoologique de Revachol, is yours.
Zh: “请原谅。这对我们来说……是一件大事。你已经帮了我们*两次*——还带来了一些很棒的消息。感谢你，*还有*瑞瓦肖社会神秘动物学会也向你表示感激。”

En: Heartfelt gratitude -- but does it feel like closure? What *really* happened?
Zh: 衷心的感激——不过，感觉像是结束吗？*到底*发生了什么？

En: Thank you for the vote of no-confidence, Gary. And *officer*, I appreciate your concern, but please leave this to the *experts*. Unless you have an *alternative* hypothesis you'd like to venture...
Zh: “感谢你投出不信任的一票，盖理。还有*警官*，我感激你的关心，但是请把这件事留给*专业人士*吧。除非你还想冒险提出*其他*假设……”

En: Really, Gary? The woman's voice is a little shaky suddenly. "We're *getting* somewhere here. I'd love to play 'Suzerainty', but..."
Zh: “真的吗，盖理？”女人的声音突然有些颤抖。“我们已经有些*进展*了。我也很想玩大领主，但是……”

En: Lena, I'm sorry, but you're not *getting* anywhere, it was some kids. I know the little mutants around here -- leave anything out in the open and they'll steal it. Even if it's bugs. He looks at his tea.
Zh: “莉娜，很抱歉，但是你没有任何*进展*，是小孩做的。我知道这里有些小变种人——任何放在外面的东西，他们都会偷走。就算虫子也一样。”他看着自己的茶杯。

En: Morell, it's been fun, really. But I need a bath and I have deliveries to handle. When this tea is done, I gotta run.
Zh: “莫雷尔，其实真的挺开心的。但我需要洗个澡，还要去送货。这杯茶喝完，我就走了。”

En: No-no. No need to apologize, Gary. You've been more than helpful. We'll have to take a rain check on that game of 'Suzerainty' today though -- we're gonna follow this through.
Zh: “不不。不用道歉，盖理。你已经帮了我们很多忙。不过，今天‘大领主’的游戏局要延期了——但我们会有始有终的。”

En: He keeps the language unemotional but it's in there -- disappointment.
Zh: 他想让自己的话语尽量不情绪化，但里面还是透露出——失望的情绪。

En: The first man to break formation is always a blow to leadership. This is bigger than he lets on.
Zh: 第一次有人打破规矩，对他的领导地位造成了冲击。虽然他装作若无其事，但是这件事比他表现出来的更重要。

En: We've come too far to quit. I'm gonna restock the trap. Lets *do* this. (Accept enthusiastically.)
Zh: “我们已经走了很远，不能就这样放弃。我会把陷阱补充好。我们*开始*吧。”（满腔热情地接受。）

En: We're getting *really* carried away with this, aren't we? He makes a show of suppressing a sigh. "Fine, it's better than having these people get pneumonia on the coast. But after this..."
Zh: “我们*真的*被这件事冲昏了头脑，不是吗？”他强忍住一声叹息。“好吧，总比让这群人在海岸染上肺炎的好。不过在这之后……”

En: Man, I'm really feeling this is costing me time on my main investigation...
Zh: “伙计，我真感觉这件事浪费了太多时间，主要的调查都被耽搁了……”

En: Of course, sweetie, you've helped us so much already. Everyone would understand if you...
Zh: “当然了，亲爱的，你已经帮了我们很多。所有人都会理解的，如果你……”

En: Wow! Co-discovery? You'd be famous. You'd show them all. This *does* tingle the pleasure centre...
Zh: 哇哦！联合发现人？你会出名的，让所有人好好看看。这*确实*刺激了你的快感中心……

En: This would *show* them all. We need to get you on that list of discoverers, no question about that.
Zh: 这样就能在其他人面前好好*秀*一下。毫无疑问，你一定要出现在那张发现人名单上。

En: Ack! Of course. Thank you for passing along the message. That damned water lock is broken, and we can't go all the way around the 8/81...
Zh: “啊！当然了。谢谢你来送信。那个该死的水闸坏了，我们也不能绕着8/81走……”

En: Ack, so that's what this is. He ignores your jab. "Lena sent you. Can't say I blame her -- we've been here for days. That damned water lock is broken, and we can't go under the 8/81..."
Zh: “啊，所以是这么回事啊。”他无视了你的批评。“莉娜派你来的。我也不会责怪她——毕竟我们来这里已经好多天了。那个该死的水闸坏了，我们没办法从8/81下面走……”

En: But I cannot describe how these defences work -- much less how they evolved -- without studying a live specimen.
Zh: “但是我也不能描述这些防御机制是怎么运作的——更不知道它们是怎么进化出来的——如果没有活体样本可以研究的话。”

En: Your nerve endings tell you there is no such thing as a *positive* surprise.
Zh: 你的神经末梢告诉你，根本就没有什么*好意味*上的惊喜。

En: If it's more cryptid-related business you want to discuss, you'll have time for that later too.
Zh: 如果你想讨论的是跟神秘动物有关的东西，那之后再找时间也可以。

En: But what if the information is vital -- on *the hunt*...
Zh: 不过如果这个信息是至关重要的呢——对于*狩猎*来说……

En: The empty trap was the one at your campsite. Maybe this factors into it somehow?
Zh: “你设置在营地的陷阱是空的。也许这也可以作为考虑的一个因素？”

En: Right from the camp site... The old woman's face lights up. "It just means the Insulindian phasmid is even more clever than we thought!"
Zh: “就在露营地……”老妇人的脸突然亮了起来。“这就意味着伊苏林迪竹节虫比我们想象的还要更聪明！”

En: I definitely left that one stocked. Hmm...
Zh: “我肯定把那个陷阱填满了。嗯……”

En: I found the locusts in a nearby shack. A kid called Cuno's built a city of them.
Zh: “我在附近的棚屋里找到了蝗虫。一个叫坤诺的小孩用它们造了一座城市。”

En: I found the locusts in a nearby shack. Some kid's built a city of them.
Zh: “我在附近的棚屋里找到了蝗虫。有个小孩用它们造了一座城市。”

En: What the devil is a Cuno? And why would he be making a *city of bugs*?
Zh: “这个叫坤诺的魔鬼是谁？他为什么要做一座*昆虫之城*？”

En: What the devil... Why would kids be making a *city of bugs*?
Zh: “这个魔鬼……小孩子为什么要做一座*昆虫之城*？”

En: A shadow of worry passes over the woman's face. "Oh, my dear Morell. You've been an old man for too long. Kids love to torment insects, almost as much as they love to torment old folks..."
Zh: 一阵担忧的阴影掠过女人的脸。“哦，我亲爱的莫雷尔，你已经老了太久了。小孩就喜欢折腾昆虫，几乎就跟他们喜欢折腾老年人一样……”

En: I'll talk to the little gremlin, ask him to stop taking them.
Zh: “我去跟那个小鬼聊聊，让他不要再拿虫子了。”

En: I'll talk to the kids, ask them to stop. It's probably the two kids in the yard near the shack.
Zh: “我会去找小孩们聊聊，让他们以后不要再这么做。也许是棚屋附近的那两个小鬼。”

En: Oh, you've been such a dear to us. Please, let us know. If the kids can stop hampering us... I have a feeling we're getting *so* close!
Zh: “哦，你对我们真是太好了。拜托了，一定要告诉我们。如果那些小孩以后不妨碍我们……我有种感觉，我们已经*如此*接近了。”

En: Did *you* know Gary was hiding the armour?
Zh: “*你*知道盖理把盔甲藏起来了吗？”

En: Have you noticed anything *off* about your friend Gary?
Zh: “你有没有注意到你的朋友盖理，他是不是有些*不对劲*？”

En: Hell no, I had no idea. And I'm still cross with him to be honest. It's not like him. He's got his quirks, but dishonesty -- or disloyalty -- are not one of them.
Zh: “天啦，没有，我不知道。老实说，我还在生他的气呢。这一点也不像他的作风。他是有怪癖，不过不正直——还有背信弃义——绝对不是其中之一。”

En: Thanks, the man mutters in the distance. He doesn't dare say more.
Zh: “谢谢，”那人在远处嘟囔着。他不敢说太多。

En: He's still glad his friend stood up for him.
Zh: 朋友为他挺身而出，他还是很高兴的。

En: I don't know what got into him, officer... Thank you for letting him off easy. He won't forget it; we'll make sure he won't.
Zh: “我不知道他怎么了，警官……感谢你放他一马。他不会忘记的；我们保证他不会的。”

En: Huh? No. No, of course not. Why?
Zh: “哈？不，不，当然没有了。为什么这么问？”

En: Are you sure?
Zh: “你确定吗？”

En: Well... He lowers his voice. "He's been a little unenthusiastic about the trip. More so than usual. But that's understandable. He just wants to get home and warm up."
Zh: “呃……”他压低声音。“他对这趟旅途有些不太热心。

En: He doesn't want to say it, but this *is* unusual. Gary's been to the jungle with him -- to places *way* more challenging than the coast. Why so eager to go home?
Zh: 他不想说，但是这*很*不寻常。盖理和他一起去过丛林——去过*远比*海岸更有挑战性的地方。为什么这么着急想回家？

En: She's *seen* it?
Zh: 她*见过*？

En: Needless to say, you *must* ask her about the mysterious phasmid.
Zh: 不用说，你*肯定*要问她神秘竹节虫的事。

En: Uhm...
Don't mess around, take the pheromone, it'll make you sexier.
Zh: 别浪费时间了，拿走费洛蒙，它会让你变得性感一些。

En: What about his eager-to-leave friend Gary there? Talk to him too, perhaps.
Zh: 那位迫切想要离开的朋友盖理呢？也许也应该跟他聊聊。

En: A sigh. She doesn't think you'll need it.
Zh: 一声叹息。她觉得你并不需要。

En: Okay, it's 1113 Tabernacle Road. Jamrock, but...
Zh: “好吧，圣堂路1113号。加姆洛克，不过……”

En: Wait, can I have your address? In case there's news.
Zh: “等等，能把你的地址告诉我吗？如果有消息的话，我可以告诉你。”

En: The lieutenant sighs at your flagrant misogyny.
Zh: 你一目了然的厌女症让警督叹了口气。

En: The cryptozoologist's companion says nothing but nods approvingly in your direction.
Zh: 神秘动物学家的同伴什么也没说，只是朝着你的方向赞许地点点头。

En: Hmm! Well, first of all it's damn difficult to find -- which is why we've been knee-deep in the reeds laying traps for it.
Zh: “嗯！这个嘛，首先它很难找到——所以我们才会在这片齐膝深的芦苇丛里放陷阱。”

En: What makes it so difficult to find?
Zh: “为什么那么难发现呢？”

En: So no one's ever found one?
Zh: “所以还没人发现过？”

En: Good question. Being a phasmid, of the order *phantasmodea* -- a ghost insect -- it disguises itself as plant-matter. In this case the reeds... He looks around. "Awful lot of reeds around, aren't there?"
Zh: “这是个好问题。作为竹节虫，*竹节虫目*属——它是一种幽灵昆虫——能把自己伪装成植物的样子。在这种情况下是芦苇……”他看向周围，“这里的芦苇真是多得吓人，不是吗？”

En: Not *yet* He holds up an index finger. "That's what makes it a *cryptid*.
Zh: “还*没有*”，他举起食指。“这就是它能成为*神秘动物*的原因。”

En: What have you discovered about it so far? (Continue.)
Zh: “那目前你都发现什么了？”（继续。）

En: *And*, I suspect it may also have developed other *specialized techniques* to protect itself from predators... or scientists, in our present case.
Zh: “*而且*，我怀疑它还开发出了其他*特殊技能*，来保护自己不受捕食者的伤害……或者在现在的情况下，是科学家的伤害。”

En: Very little, I'm sorry to say. No one's ever captured a specimen, so all our information is based on first- and third-hand accounts.
Zh: “很遗憾的说，非常少。没有人抓到过哪怕是一个样本，所以我们的全部信息都是基于一手和第三方描述的。”

En: But if no one can prove it exists, how do you know it's real?
Zh: “但是，如果没人能证明它的存在，你怎么知道它是真的呢？”

En: I *know* it's real, the cryptozoologist says, brusquely enough that even he seems taken aback by it...
Zh: “我*知道*它是真的，”神秘动物学家说到，唐突地甚至就连他自己也大吃一惊……

En: By which I mean, he says, gathering himself, "I've heard enough first-hand accounts to believe quite firmly that the Insulindian phasmid is more than mere superstition."
Zh: “我的意思是想说，”他重新调整了一下情绪，“我听到的一手信息，已经足够让我坚定地相信：伊苏林迪竹节虫绝不仅仅是一种迷信。”

En: It's clear that his obsession with the phasmid is driven by something more than the pure pursuit of scientific advancement.
Zh: 很显然，他对竹节虫的痴迷不仅仅来自于对科学进步的纯粹追求。

En: It's time to return to your wöman, Morell.
Zh: “是时候回到你的女人身边了，莫雷尔。”

En: Lena seems pretty eager for you to return.
Zh: “莉娜好像很希望你回去。”

En: And I'm eager to return to her, I assure you -- but I can't leave before we've finished with these traps.
Zh: “我也很想回到她身边，这一点我可以保证——但是陷阱还没完成，我还不能离开。”

En: COME ON, MORELL. WE'VE BEEN SOAKING OUT HERE FOR DAYS. IT'S TIME TO GO BACK.
Zh: “拜托，莫雷尔。我们已经在外面泡了好几天，是时候回去了。”

En: AND LEAVE THE TRAPS? ABSOLUTELY NOT! he yells in response. "I won't let Lena down..."
Zh: “放着这些陷阱不管吗？绝对不行！”他大喊着回应到。“我不会让莉娜失望的……”

En: COME ON, SHE WANTS US BACK. I'M SOAKED UP TO MY NUTS OVER HERE. WE'LL BOTH CATCH REED CRABS IF WE DON'T DRY OUT SOON.
Zh: “拜托，她希望我们回去。我已经浑身湿透了。如果不尽快弄干的话，我们都会感染芦苇虱子的。”

En: What will you do if these traps don't work?
Zh: “如果这些陷阱不起作用的话，你打算怎么做？”

En: Let me ask you about something else.
Zh: “我问问你其他问题。”

En: They'll *work*, I assure you. The predatory hypothesis -- using locusts as bait -- accounts for the failure of previous efforts by other teams, which used plants. We have given this some thought.
Zh: “它们会*起作用*的，我保证。捕食者假说——用蝗虫做诱饵——之前其他团队的努力失败，是因为用了植物做诱饵。我们已经考虑过了。”

En: The traps do seem to be deftly and thoughtfully constructed. It's clear the cryptozoologist's wife knows what she's doing.
Zh: 陷阱看起来确实很灵巧，也很有心思。显然那个神秘动物学家的妻子知道自己在做什么。

En: Won't *let Lena down*? Sounds like the cryptozoologist's wife shares a *special connection* to the phasmid somehow...
Zh: 不想*让莉娜失望*？听起来这位神秘动物学家的妻子好像跟竹节虫有什么*特殊的联系*……

En: Suffice to say, it's long been our dream to find proof of the Insulindian phasmid *together*. I can't abandon course now. Another cough, into his fist this time.
Zh: “可以说，长久以来，我们*共同*的梦想就是找出伊苏林迪竹节虫存在的证据。我不能现在放弃这个课题。”又是一声咳嗽，这次是用拳头捂住的。

En: Maybe you could go back to the Whirling, warm up, come back to check the traps *later*?
Zh: “也许你可以先回飞旋旅社暖和一下，*之后*再回来检查陷阱？”

En: You should just give up on this bug hunt.
Zh: “你应该放弃这次的昆虫狩猎。”

En: No, no, no. The traps need to be monitored on a regular schedule. What would we do if the phasmid were to *starve* while we were sipping tea at the hostel?
Zh: “不不，不行。陷阱需要定期监控的。如果我们在旅社喝茶的时候，竹节虫*饿死*了怎么办？”

En: *Give up*? After we've come so far? Not a chance!
Zh: “*放弃*？在我们已经走了这么远之后？绝对不可能！”

En: You're dealing with a subject near and dear to their hearts. It might behove you to tread *lightly*.
Zh: 你在处理一个他们非常非常用心的话题。最好不要*轻举妄动*。

En: Wait, Morell... the old woman raises a hand. "He may have a point. We have an obligation to rule out other hypotheses..."
Zh: “等等，莫雷尔……”老妇人举起一只手。“他可能说到点子上了。我们有义务排除其他假设……”

En: You're right, dear. His face relaxes. "It's a fair point. But what other explanation could there be?" He turns to you.
Zh: 他的面庞放松了一些。“你是对的，亲爱的。这很公平。但是还能有什么别的解释呢？”他转向了你。

En: But whose?
Zh: 不过是谁呢？

En: Maybe it was a *different* cryptid who took the bugs?
Zh: “也许是其他*不同的*神秘动物拿走了昆虫？”

En: Okay. I've been thinking and I'm just not sure that it was the *phasmid* that took the locusts.
Zh: “好吧。我一直在考虑，但就是不能确定是*竹节虫*拿走了蝗虫。”

En: A *different* cryptid? The cryptozoologist furrows his heavy brow.
Zh: “其他*不同的*神秘动物？”神秘动物学家严肃地皱起眉头。

En: Really, officer, we can take it from here. The cryptozoologist furrows his heavy brow.
Zh: 神秘动物学家严肃地皱起眉头。“说真的，警官，我们可以从这里开始。”

En: Oh, sweetie. Maybe you should stick to human detective work?
Zh: “哦，亲爱的。也许你应该继续人类的侦探工作？”

En: Yes, sweetie, we've already taken too much of your time.
Zh: “没错，亲爱的，我们已经占用你太多时间了。”

En: Oh, you've been such a dear to us. Please, let us know whatever you turn up. I have a feeling we're getting *so* close!
Zh: “哦，你对我们真是太好了。拜托了，不管你发现什么，一定要告诉我们。我有种感觉，我们已经*如此*接近了。”

En: Delinquents -- my favourite. It doesn't sound like it's really his favourite.
Zh: “小流氓——我的最爱。”看起来并不真是他的最爱。

En: There's no mystery here. You've seen the Locust City and its spiralling mud towers. All that's left is to confront the city father himself...
Zh: 这里没有什么秘密。你见过蝗城，还有螺旋形的泥巴塔。接下来的就是面对城市之父本人……

En: I'm pretty sure it was Cuno who took the locusts. He's built a whole city for them in his shack.
Zh: “我很肯定是坤诺拿走了蝗虫。他在自己的棚屋里给它们建了一整座城市。”

En: What the devil is a Cuno? And why would he be making a *city of bugs*?
Zh: “这个叫坤诺的魔鬼是谁？他为什么要做一座*昆虫之城*？”

En: The ageing cryptozoologist breaks into a hideous coughing fit.
Zh: 年迈的神秘动物学家突然咳嗽起来。

En: He has a 38 degree fever. His resilience has given way.
Zh: 他发了38度的烧。他的恢复能力已经失去控制了。

En: She looks at him with tender concern. "Darling, I told you to take it easy. You're getting sick. Maybe it's time to go home?"
Zh: 她担忧地看着他，眼神十分温柔。“亲爱的，我就让你不要这么着急。你都生病了。也许我们该回家了？”

En: You're right, you're right... He breathes carefully, not to start coughing again. "We can come back next season... when it's warmer..."
Zh: “你说得对，你说得对……”他小心翼翼地呼吸着，不想让自己再咳出来。“我们可以下个季节再回来……等天气暖和一点……”

En: Self-deceit, sire.
Zh: 这是自欺欺人，大人。

En: It's not worth risking your health. You should call it a day and go home. (Refuse.)
Zh: “不值得你这样拿健康冒险的。今天就到此为止，回去休息吧。”（拒绝。）

En: I'd offer to help, but I have my own things to do. (Refuse.)
Zh: “我也想帮忙，但我还有自己的事要做。”（拒绝。）

En: Damnit, maybe I can still restock the trap for you? (Accept.)
Zh: “该死的，也许我能帮你把陷阱的诱饵给补上？”（接受。）

En: He's right, dear Morell. Come now, we've waited so long, what's one more season?
Zh: “他说得对，亲爱的莫雷尔。走吧，我们已经等了这么久，还怕再多等一个季节吗？”

En: You're right, dear. We'll get our shot yet, I'm sure of it...
Zh: “你说的对，亲爱的。我们还有机会的，我肯定……”

En: The slouch in his shoulders tells you otherwise. He doesn't know how many more field expeditions he has left in him...
Zh: 那下垂的双肩告诉了你，事实并非如此。他不知道自己还剩下多少次野外考察……

En: Of course, sweetie. We couldn't ask you to do more than you already have. Come now, Morell, we've waited so long, what's one more season?
Zh: “当然了，亲爱的。我们不能再要求你做的更多了。走吧，我们已经等了这么久，还怕再多等一个季节吗？”

En: You *can*? The lieutenant makes a show of suppressing a sigh. "Fine. It's better than having these people get pneumonia on the coast, but after *this*..."
Zh: “你*可以*吗？”警督强忍住一声叹息。“好吧。最好别让这群人在海岸边染上肺炎，但是*在这*之后……”

En: What Morell means is, we're grateful for your help. She nods to her husband.
Zh: “莫雷尔的意思是，我们很感激你的帮助。”她朝自己的丈夫点点头。

En: Really, it's too much, officer... He starts coughing again.
Zh: “说真的，太麻烦你了，警官……”他又开始咳嗽了。

En: Here's a fresh batch of locusts. They should slide right down the funnel. And thank you again. We will definitely mention you, should this lead to a discovery. I'm not talking co-discovery, of course, but...
Zh: “这是最新一批的蝗虫。它们可以顺着漏斗滑下去。再次感谢。如果能有发现，我们一定会提到你的名字。当然了，我说的不是联合发现人，不过……”

En: This place is *much* better for field work in the summer, believe me.
Zh: “相信我，如果是夏天的话，这个地方要*更适合*野外考察一些。”

En: What sorts of *specialized techniques* is the phasmid using to hide itself?
Zh: “这个竹节虫到底用了什么*特殊技能*来隐藏自己？”

En: It's my hypothesis that it has evolved certain electro-chemical defences that allow it to interfere with animal perception -- impeding pattern recognition, confusing the visual cortex.
Zh: “根据我的假想，它进化出了某种电化学防御，能够用它干扰动物的知觉——妨碍它们的图像识别能力，造成视觉皮层混乱。”

En: Yes, it makes perfect sense. You're beginning to suspect there's something *para-natural* about this phasmid.
Zh: 没错，这很有道理。你开始怀疑这个竹节虫是不是有什么*超能力*。

En: Lena said there has been a sighting of it, here in Martinaise.
Zh: “莉娜说马丁内斯有人见到过。”

En: Yes, the most *recent* sighting was by a couple of teenagers along the coast here. That's what brought us to Martinaise specifically.
Zh: “是的，*最近*一次是一群年轻人在海岸边目击到的。所以我们才专门赶到了马丁内斯。”

En: There's nothing *dispassionate* about the way he talks about the insect.
Zh: 他谈论昆虫的方式一点也不*平心静气*。

En: His spirit may be willing, but his body might be too old to endure the rigours of the coast.
Zh: 他的精神也许愿意，但他的身体可能太老了，经受不住海岸的严酷环境。

En: He wants to see this tale through as much as *you*. Otherwise he'd have stopped this already. But he *cannot* let it drag out after this.
Zh: 他跟*你*一样，想把这个传说看个清楚。否则他早就已经放弃了。不过这次之后，他*不能*再继续拖延下去了。

En: A *ghost insect*, he said? These people are looking for a ghost.
Zh: 他说，一种*幽灵昆虫*？这些人在找幽灵啊。

En: Ghost insect... so you're ghost hunters.
Zh: “幽灵昆虫……所以你是神鬼猎人。”

En: No -- that is precisely what we're *not*. We are zoological specialists, looking for an extant species of phasmid.
Zh: “不——我们绝对*不是*。我们是动物学专家，正在寻找一种现存的竹节虫物种。”

En: Seems puny, to be honest.
Zh: 说实话，好像挺弱的。

En: This arouses no special feelings in me.
Zh: “并没有唤起我特别的情感。”

En: And it shouldn't. Nature does not concern herself with ethical propositions. As a scientist, my interest is *strictly dispassionate*.
Zh: “就不应该这样。大自然不关心伦理命题。作为一个科学家，我的兴趣是*完全不带任何感情的*。”

En: More than some. He admires this about her.
Zh: 还远远不止呢。他很欣赏她这一点。

En: Hmm... I could go for some trap-setting.
Zh: 嗯……设置陷阱什么的我可以。

En: I've changed my mind. Spray me with lots of pheromone. (Present your arm pit)
Zh: “我改变注意了。给我喷点费洛蒙吧。”（露出你的腋窝。）

En: It's truly not that special. What is *cryptozoology* anyway? A derogative. I just like puzzles, like any scientist.
Zh: “其实真没那么特别。再说了，*神秘动物学*到底是什么？它是一种损毁的东西。我不过像其他科学家一样喜欢谜题罢了。”

En: Hmmm... interesting. Something for later. This close call.
Zh: 嗯……有点意思。以后可以考虑。这次算是幸免于难。

En: C'mon. You're doing this for yourself, not her.
Zh: “拜托。你做这个是为了你自己，不是她。”

En: I'm doing this for *science*. And so is she.
Zh: “我做这一切都是为了*科学*。她也一样。”

En: Some kind of foul play might be afoot. Theft?
Zh: 可能发生了某种不正当行为。盗窃？

En: Thank you, it's an honour, he says with a straight face, then turns to you. "We should probably return to our *main* investigation here. This has been refreshing, but..."
Zh: “谢谢你，我很荣幸，”他板着脸，然后转向你。“我们应该继续*最重要*的调查了。这次的体验是很提神，不过……”

En: ...helping cryptozoologists isn't really a priority for our organization, is it? The lieutenant looks out the window, impatiently.
Zh: ……帮助神秘动物学家真的不是我们组织的优先事项，对吧？警督不耐烦地看向窗外。

En: Dammit lieutenant, have you no intellectual curiosity?
Zh: 该死的警督，你就没有求知欲的吗？

En: After this is your last chance to talk to Gary.
Zh: 在这之后，就是你最后一次跟盖理说话的机会了。

En: There won't be a next season. Not for this. Find the phasmid or admit defeat, people.
Zh: 不会有下一个季节。没有了。朋友们，要么找到竹节虫，要么就此承认失败。

En: That's just something people tell themselves when they fail.
Zh: 当人们失败的时候，他们就会对自己说这种话。

En: Is that a pang of sadness, lieutenant?
Zh: 那是一阵悲痛的感觉吗，警督？

En: Thank you dear. She bows to the lieutenant -- then turns to you. "And you, detective Fox..." A sad smile. "You've been great company to an old lady and her stubborn husband."
Zh: “谢谢你，亲爱的。”她向警督鞠了一躬——然后转向你。“还有你，狐狸警探……”她的笑脸有些悲伤。“对于一位老妇人和她固执的丈夫来说，你真的是个很棒的伙伴。”

En: You're right, Lena. The RCM is worth their salt -- more so than they say on the radio.
Zh: “你说得对，莉娜。RCM还是名副其实的——比广播里说的要好得多。”

En: See you around, wheels.
Zh: “回见了，朋友们。”

En: Good bye, Lena.
Zh: “再见，莉娜。”

En: I live to serve. (Tip an imaginary police hat.)
Zh: “为人民服务。”（戴上想象中的警帽）

En: Just nod.
Zh: 只是点点头。

En: Good bye. She smiles and they leave -- two old cryptozoologists and one gas-powered wheelchair.
Zh: “再见。”她笑了笑，随后两人一起离开了——两个老神秘动物学家，还有一台气动轮椅。

En: You too -- Morell? Let's roll? She smiles and they leave -- two old cryptozoologists and one gas-powered wheelchair.
Zh: “你也是吗——莫雷尔？我们走吧？”她笑了笑，随后两人一起离开了——两个老神秘动物学家，还有一台气动轮椅。

En: And you, detective. She turns to you with a sad smile. "You've been great company to an old lady and her stubborn husband. Thank you, truly."
Zh: “你也是，警探。”她转向你，笑容有些悲伤。“对于一位老妇人和她固执的丈夫来说，你真的是个很棒的伙伴。真的谢谢你。”

En: More cardio? Let's *do* this.
Zh: 多来点有氧运动？那就赶紧*开始*吧。

En: Thank you for telling us, sweetie. She turns to smile gently up at her husband. "This is good news, right? It means we can try *again*."
Zh: “感谢你告诉我们，亲爱的。”她转过脸，对自己的丈夫露出温柔的笑容。“这是个好消息，不是吗？这就意味着我们可以*再试*一次了。”

En: Thank you for telling us, sweetie. She turns to smile gently up at her husband. "This is good news, right? It means we can try *again*."
Zh: “感谢你告诉我们，亲爱的。”她转过脸，对自己的丈夫露出温柔的笑容。“这是个好消息，不是吗？这就意味着我们可以*再试*一次了。”

En: She acts chipper, but something's changed in her tone. A hidden worry.
Zh: 她表现地很有活力，但语气却有所变化。那是一种隐约的担忧。

En: Something is secretly gnawing at her confidence. It's not this Cuno kid, or the missing locusts, it's something else.
Zh: 有什么东西在悄悄啃噬着她的信心。不是这个叫坤诺的孩子，或是不见的蝗虫，而是别的什么。

En: I'll get going. [Leave.]
Zh: “我得走了。”[离开。]

En: Here we go. Nice and easy. No way out, little guys, not out of this jam...
Zh: “好了。愉快又轻松。出不去了，小家伙们，别想从这里出去……”

En: Hello hello! He gets up. "What can I do for you, officer?"
Zh: “哎呀，你好啊！”他站起身来。“有什么能帮你的吗，警官？”

En: There's a cylinder on the ground in which the man is arranging some netting. It looks like some kind of trap. He notices you.
Zh: 地上放着一个圆筒，这个男人好像在布网。看起来像是某种陷阱。他注意到了你。

En: You don't seem too happy to see the RCM.
Zh: “你好像不太乐意看见RCM。”

En: Man to man, you shouldn't leave your wöman unattended in a cafeteria surrounded by *communists*.
Zh: “坦白说，你不应该把自己的女人独自留在一间满是*康米党主义者*的餐厅里。”

En: Oh no, it's alright. I'm just busy. He glances at the cylinder. "What's this about?"
Zh: “当然没有，没关系的。我只是有点忙。”他瞥了一眼圆筒。“有什么事吗？”

En: Ack, so that's what this is. He ignores your jab. "Lena sent you. Can't say I blame her -- we've been here for days. That damned water lock is broken, and we can't go under the 8/81..."
Zh: “啊，所以是这么回事啊。”他无视了你的批评。“莉娜派你来的。我也不会责怪她——毕竟我们来这里已经好多天了。那个该死的水闸坏了，我们没办法从8/81下面走……”

En: Oh good. We should really be getting back. Gary could use a hot shower and a warm bed...
Zh: “好吧。我们真的该回去了。盖理需要好好洗个热水澡，还有一张温暖的床……”

En: Are you doing some kind of science here? You look like a science person.
Zh: “你是在搞什么科学研究吗？你看起来很像科研人员。”

En: I'm looking for a rare insect, the *Insulindian phasmid*. Field work -- nothing quite like it...
Zh: “我在找一个珍稀物种，*伊苏林迪竹节虫*。野外考察——看起来一点也不像……”

En: 'A science person'? He snarls. "The *so-called* science community hasn't accepted me as one of their own quite yet."
Zh: “‘科研人员？’”他咆哮着。“那些*所谓的*科学团体还没有接纳我成为他们中的一员呢。”

En: Tell me more about these traps.
Zh: “再跟我说说这些陷阱吧。”

En: Tell me about this *phasmid* you're looking for.
Zh: “跟我说说你正在找的这个*竹节虫*。”

En: What if we checked the traps for you?\n
Zh: “如果我们帮你去检查陷阱呢？”

En: What if I checked the traps for you?
Zh: “如果我帮你去检查陷阱呢？”

En: You must be Morell, the cryptozoologist?
Zh: “你一定是莫雷尔，那个神秘动物学家？”

En: To what do I owe the pleasure?
Zh: “是什么风把你给吹来了？”

En: That's sarcasm. He takes no pleasure from your appearance.
Zh: 这是在挖苦你。他不喜欢你的样子。

En: The 8/81 is a raised motorway that separates Martinaise from Jamrock. The labyrinth of streets underneath it makes it difficult to pass. Not like walking over a nice water lock...
Zh: 8/81是一条高速公路，把加姆洛克和马丁内斯分隔开来。公路下面的街道像迷宫一般，很难通过。跟从那个漂亮的水闸走过去可完全不一样……

En: Lena sent me. She's been really worried about you and is waiting for you to get back.
Zh: “莉娜让我来的。她一直很担心你，在等着你回去。”

En: Ack! Of course. Thank you for passing along the message. That damned water lock is broken, and we can't go all the way around the 8/81...
Zh: “啊！当然了。谢谢你来帮我送信。那个该死的水闸坏了，我们也不能绕着8/81下面走……”

En: His self-conscious enthusiasm renders his movements ungainly. He looks like your understanding of a scientist.
Zh: 他那份自发的热情让他的动作显得有些笨拙。完全就是你理解的科学家的形象。

En: I hope you haven't been littering here. This is unspoiled wilderness.
Zh: “希望你没在这里到处乱丢垃圾。这片可是还没有被人类破坏的原野。”

En: Well... He points at the cage of mesh and wiring on the ground. "They may not *look* impressive, but Lena designed them quite cleverly, so I'm sure they'll do the trick."
Zh: “呃……”他指着铁丝笼和地上的线。“也许*看起来*是很一般，不过莉娜的设计非常巧妙，它们肯定能派上用场的。”

En: Tell me how you became a cryptozoologist again.
Zh: “再跟我说说你成为神秘动物学家的事。”

En: How did you become a *cryptozoologist*?
Zh: “你是怎么变成一个*神秘动物学家*的？”

En: Why are you so interested in this stick bug? It doesn't seem to be as colourful as some of the other cryptids I've heard about.
Zh: “你为什么对竹节虫这么感兴趣？跟我之前听过的那些神秘动物相比，它好像不是很有趣啊。”

En: Typical rookie assumption. He flashes you a sideways smile. "Insects are much more sophisticated creatures than those unversed in zoology give them credit for."
Zh: 他对你侧目一笑。“典型的菜鸟假说。昆虫比那些不懂动物学的人自以为是的想法要复杂的多。”

En: So I checked all the traps.
Zh: “我已经检查完所有陷阱了。”

En: I had a chat with this kid, Cuno. He promised to stop stealing the locusts.
Zh: “我跟那个孩子，坤诺聊了聊。他保证不再偷蝗虫了。”

En: Even simply catching a glimpse of the Insulindian phasmid would be the apex of my -- of *any* -- cryptozoologist's career. But to study it and its defences, find out how it's stayed hidden so long... He shakes his head.
Zh: “哪怕是能瞥到一眼伊苏林迪竹节虫，都将会是我的——或者*任何人*的——神秘动物学生涯的巅峰。不过要是说研究它和它的防御机制，找出它一直以来都隐藏在哪里……”他摇了摇头。

En: No, officer. You've got the wrong person. I deal with *living* organisms.
Zh: “不，警官。你找错人了。我是跟*活*生物打交道的。”

En: Wilderness? He looks around. "You must be joking, officer."
Zh: “原野？”他看了看周围。“你一定是在开玩笑吧，警官。”

En: You got me. But what *are* you doing here?
Zh: “你明白我的意思了吧。不过你*到底*在这做什么呢？”

En: I'm serious. I am here to make sure adventurers aren't disturbing nesting areas.
Zh: “我是认真的。我来是为了保证冒险家不要打扰筑巢区。”

En: I'm not an *adventurer*, I'm a *scientist*. I know how to tread lightly and clean up after myself.
Zh: “我不是*冒险家*，我是个*科学家*。我知道怎么小心处理，事后也会清理干净。”

En: Morell, cryptozoologist. I'd shake your hand, but... He displays his gloved hands, which are glistening with sewage and silt.
Zh: “莫雷尔，神秘动物学家。我很想跟你握手，不过……”他伸出戴着手套的手，污水和淤泥在上面闪闪发光。

En: I'm Morell, a cryptozoologist. I'm out here looking for a rare insect called the *Insulindian phasmid*. I'd shake your hand, but... He displays his gloved hands, which are glistening with silt and sewage.
Zh: “我是莫雷尔，一名神秘动物学家。我在这里找一个名叫*伊苏林迪竹节虫*的珍稀物种。我很想跟你握手，不过……”他伸出戴着手套的手，污水和淤泥在上面闪闪发光。

En: How do the traps work?
Zh: “陷阱是怎么工作的？”

En: What are you using as bait?
Zh: “你用什么做诱饵呢？”

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么事？”

En: I didn't expect you to take such an interest in our work here, officer. He looks at you with obvious surprise.
Zh: 他看着你，显然有些吃惊。“没想到你居然会对我们的工作感兴趣，警官。”

En: So it *was* just a child... He purses his lips, crestfallen.
Zh: 神秘动物学家咬紧嘴唇。“所以*只是*一个小孩……”他看起来有些气馁。

En: *Completely* empty? The cryptozoologist's eyes grow wide.
Zh: “*完全*空了？”神秘动物学家的双眼圆睁。

En: Yes, you're right. We just need to restock the empty trap. Then we'll need to inspect the traps one more time, and then *maybe*... we can...
Zh: “是的，你是对的。我们只要把空陷阱的诱饵再补上。然后再检查一次陷阱，*说不定*……我们就可以……”

En: No locusts... but no phasmid either...
Zh: “没有蝗虫……但是也没有竹节虫……”

En: Locusts. He gestures toward the trap. "Nearly all *known* phasmids are herbivores, of course, but we've hypothesized that the Insulindian phasmid might occasionally prey on other insects."
Zh: “蝗虫。”他朝陷阱做了个手势。“基本上所有*已知*的竹节虫都是食草的，当然了，我们猜测也许伊苏林迪竹节虫偶尔也会以其他昆虫为食。”

En: Simple -- attracted by the locusts, the phasmid crawls down the funnel, and, having eaten its fill, can't get back out.
Zh: “简单——竹节虫被蝗虫吸引，然后沿着漏斗爬下去，吃光里面的诱饵之后，就出不来了。”

En: At least, that's the intention. The net isn't a perfect solution, but we didn't want to use anything that might damage the specimen's delicate exoskeleton.
Zh: “至少，是这么打算的。网不是最完美的解决方案，但是我们不想使用任何可能损伤样本精致外骨骼的东西。”

En: Cryptozoology and detective work are very similar.
Zh: “神秘动物学和警探的工作很相似。”

En: I'm looking for someone hiding on this very coast. Looking for *another* thing may lead me to her.
Zh: “我在找一个藏在这片海岸的人。寻找*其他*东西的时候，也许会引领我找到她的踪迹。”

En: We're looking for someone hiding on this very coast. Looking for *another* thing may lead us to her.
Zh: “我们在找一个藏在这片海岸的人。寻找*其他*东西的时候，也许会带着我们找到她。”

En: Chaos is my method. I am its scion.
Zh: “混乱就是我的方法。我是它的子孙。”

En: Yes, indeed! Both require a great deal of research, attention to detail, and, above all, *persistence*.
Zh: “是的，没错！两者都需要大量的研究，关注细节，而且最重要的是，*毅力*。”

En: Sometimes it's helpful to take a break and return to one's *primary pursuit* with fresh eyes. He nods.
Zh: 神秘动物学家点点头。“有时候稍微休息一下，用全新的眼光回归*最初的追求*是很有帮助的。”

En: Well... He doesn't know what to say. "Be that as it may, I'd really appreciate the help!"
Zh: “呃……”他不知道该说些什么。“话虽如此，我真的很感谢你的帮助！”

En: Where are these traps?
Zh: “那些陷阱在哪？”

En: What do I do if there's a phasmid in one of the traps?
Zh: “如果某个陷阱里有竹节虫的话，我该怎么做？”

En: What if I encounter the phasmid in the wild?
Zh: “如果我在野外遇到竹节虫呢？”

En: Bring it to me at once. Just make sure the trap is closed tight.
Zh: “马上把它带给我。只要保证把陷阱关严实就好。”

En: That seems like a lot. Do we really have time for this... extracurricular venture?
Zh: “看起来好像挺多的。我们真有时间……去搞什么课外探险吗？”

En: Thank you for your help. Gary and I will start breaking down camp. If you have any more questions, now's the time to ask!
Zh: “感谢你的帮助。盖理和我要开始拆营地了。如果你还有什么问题，现在赶紧问吧！”

En: Why not? The lieutenant shrugs. "At least it will give us the excuse to look into a *lot* of reeds."
Zh: “为什么不呢。”警督耸耸肩。“至少给了我们一个调查*大片*芦苇地的借口。”

En: Yes, there was nothing in the trap. No locusts, no phasmid.
Zh: “是啊，陷阱里面什么也没有。没有蝗虫，也没有竹节虫。”

En: That's not *ideal*, but... He rubs his chin.
Zh: “这不太*理想*，但是……”他摸了摸下巴。

En: The old woman's face lights up. "It just means the Insulindian phasmid is even more clever than we thought!"
Zh: 老妇人的脸突然亮了起来。“这只能意味着，伊苏林迪竹节虫要比我们想象中聪明的多！”

En: Yes! The *phantasmodea* picked off the locusts and escaped. This is good news! Though we'll have to reconsider the design of the traps, make them *more* secure...
Zh: “没错！这个*竹节虫目*把蝗虫叼了出来，然后逃跑了。这可是好消息！虽然我们要重新考虑一下陷阱的设计问题，让它们*更*安全一些……”

En: His companion sighs. "Another trip to the reeds..."
Zh: 他的同伴叹了口气。“又要去芦苇地了……”

En: Lets talk about cryptids some more.
Zh: “我们再多聊聊神秘动物吧。”

En: Let's talk about *specific* cryptids.
Zh: “我们聊聊*特定的*神秘动物吧。”

En: He takes the dangers and discomforts that come with his work for granted, but just imagine the unforgiving desert heat he's endured, the wetlands filled with venomous reptiles he has crossed...
Zh: 他认为工作带来的危险与不适是理所当然的，不过想象一下，需要忍受沙漠酷暑，还有穿过布满毒虫的湿地……

En: I'm the most rugged of men. That sounds like my kind of fun.
Zh: 我是最粗旷的男人。听起来像是我喜欢的东西。

En: I don't know how he does it. I *already* want a bath.
Zh: 我不知道他怎么样。我*已经*想洗个澡了。

En: Seems silly to expend so much effort on studying stuff that *isn't even real*.
Zh: 把这么多精力用在研究一个*甚至并不存在*的东西身上，好像有点愚蠢。

En: You could get a heat stroke and faint and hurt your head. You could get bitten by a snake. You could get beaten up for trespassing.
Zh: 你可能会中暑、头晕、还有脑部受伤。你可能会被蛇咬，还可能因为非法入侵而挨揍。

En: With nice bath salts, to soothe your bruised and battered soul.
Zh: 用美妙的浴盐，抚平你青肿淤紫、备受虐待的灵魂。

En: Absolutely. You'd never do something like that to yourself, nope.
Zh: 没错。你永远也不会对自己做出这种事，不会的。

En: Suffering will only make me stronger.
Zh: 磨难只会让我变得更坚强。

En: That's pretty much where I am right now.
Zh: 差不多就是我现在的处境。

En: Doesn't sound like he particularly wants to be lumped in with them either. His is a scientific community of one.
Zh: 听起来他也不太想跟他们混在一起。他自己就是一个科学团体。

En: What would it be like to grasp and hold onto something you think is next to you -- or just behind you -- like a trace of vapour you exhaled one spring morning as a child? This is what he's searching for. A spectre.
Zh: 紧紧抓住你以为就在自己身边——或者是身后——的东西会是什么感觉——比如小时候，春季清晨呼出的水蒸气的痕迹？他在寻找的就是这种东西。一个幽灵。

En: What makes you think the phasmid is here in Martinaise?
Zh: “你为什么会觉得竹节虫就在马丁内斯呢？”

En: There was a credible sighting here recently. A pair of teenagers described a creature that matched the Insulindian phasmid to an *uncanny* degree, *despite* not knowing what it was themselves.
Zh: “最近有一个可靠的目击情报。有群年轻人描述的生物跟伊苏林迪竹节虫的匹配度高的*离奇*，*尽管*他们自己也不知道到底是什么。”

En: It's the first credible sighting in several decades. Admittedly, it's an unusual location for this species, but with all the sewage run-off upstream, it probably doesn't matter much anymore.
Zh: “几十年来，首次最可靠的目击证明就在这里。诚然，对于这个物种来说是个不太寻常的地点，但是有了这些逆流而行的下水道，可能也就不那么重要了。”

En: I'm afraid not, officer. I've been busy digging around in the reeds for days, looking for signs of insect activity -- I'm less interested in mammalian concerns, to be perfectly honest.
Zh: “恐怕没有，警官。我最近这些天一直在芦苇地里忙来来去，想要寻找昆虫活动的信号——老实说，我对哺乳动物可没多大的兴趣。”

En: Be careful where you tread in the reeds. Some less conscientious researchers may have left their traps out there -- armed, hidden.
Zh: 踩芦苇的时候一定要小心。有些不负责的研究员把他们的陷阱留在那里——不仅隐蔽，还带着武器。

En: I'm ready. Let's get to it. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我准备好了，我们开始吧。”（总结。）

En: But, Kim, maybe our suspect is hiding out in the reeds along with the traps?
Zh: “但是，金，也许我们的嫌疑人就跟这些陷阱一样躲在芦苇丛里？”

En: The pursuit of knowledge is its own justification.
Zh: “追寻知识本身就是正当的。”

En: What? Aren't you having *fun*?
Zh: “什么？难道你不觉得很*有意思*吗？”

En: The man surveys the cold coast. "I have a feeling the only people foolish enough to spend their time skulking among the reeds are these poor fools... and us. But fine. It's not like we're drowning in leads."
Zh: 男人调查着冰冷的海岸。“我有种感觉，只有笨到家的人才会花时间潜伏在这片芦苇丛里，跟这些可怜的傻瓜……还有我们一样。不过也没事。反正我们也没有被线索淹没。”

En: Is it? he doesn't look too convinced but the small shrug indicates -- why not?
Zh: “是吗？”他看起来不是太相信，但是那下微微的耸肩意味着——为什么不呢？

En: Even relative to examining a week-old corpse, I'm not sure mucking about in the reeds qualifies as *fun*... but have it your way, detective...
Zh: “就算跟检查一具放置一周的尸体相比，我都不确定在芦苇丛中闲荡能不能算得上是*有意思*……不过随你便吧，警探……”

En: I've just always liked animals, and puzzles. Searching for cryptids is a bit of both.
Zh: “我只不过一直都很喜欢动物，还有解谜。搜寻神秘动物两者都能满足。”

En: He seems reluctant to talk about himself, but he'll open up -- if you prod a little.
Zh: 他好像不愿意提自己的事，但他会开口的——如果你稍微刺激一下下的话。

En: Why not just be a zoologist? Real animals are puzzling too.
Zh: “那当个动物学家不就可以了吗？真正的动物也充满谜题啊。”

En: What kinds of evidence do you use?
Zh: “你用的是什么类型的证据？”

En: Thanks for explaining that. Now, about something else. (Conclude.)
Zh: “感谢你的解释。现在我有些别的问题。”（总结。）

En: Maybe the Insulindian phasmid has... died out? (Proceed.)
Zh: “也许伊苏林迪竹节虫已经……灭绝了？”（继续。）

En: I have to resist the thought. He shakes his head vigorously. "Such an extraordinary creature is doubtlessly *highly resilient*. After all, it's generally thought to be capable of parthenogenesis."
Zh: 神秘动物学家用力地摇摇头。“我必须抗拒这种想法。这种非凡的生物无疑拥有*强大的复原力*。毕竟，大家普遍认为它能单性繁殖。”

En: He means asexual reproduction. The females of the species don't need to mate to produce viable eggs. This makes it easier for a species with a small population to survive.
Zh: 他的意思是无性繁殖。这个物种的雌性不需要交配就能产卵。这样一来，即使物种数量很少，生存下来也会简单许多。

En: Par...the...no...genesis?
Yes. The females don't need males to reproduce. Makes it easier for a species to survive in adverse conditions.
Zh: “是的。雌性不需要交配就能繁殖。这样一来，即使是在不利的条件下，这个物种也更容易生存下来。”

En: Females reproducing without males!? A travesty. A crime against passion *and* common sense.
Zh: “雌性不用交配就能繁殖？！真是滑稽。这是一种针对激情*和*常识的犯罪。”

En: The male is a deficient life form. The world would be better off if women didn't need men to reproduce.
Zh: “雄性是一种有缺陷的生命形式。如果女人不需要男人就能繁殖的话，这个世界会变得更加美好。”

En: That's pretty clever!
Zh: “那也太聪明了吧！”

En: Nature does not concern herself with ethical propositions. As a scientist, my interest is *strictly dispassionate*.
Zh: “大自然不关心伦理命题。作为一个科学家，我的兴趣是*完全不带任何感情的*。”

En: *Real*... He scoffs at the concept. "I know you think one is a respectable profession while the other is superstition, everyone does!"
Zh: “*真正的*……”他嘲笑着这个概念。“我知道你认为一种是受人尊敬的职业，另一种是迷信行为，所有人都这么想！”

En: Cryptozoology does seem like a lot of wishful thinking.
Zh: “神秘动物学看起来很像是异想天开。”

En: My methods are the same as those of other scientists, I simply draw upon a wider variety of evidence, and I have more hope that something truly surprising might happen.
Zh: “我的方法跟其他那些科学家一样，我只是利用了更广泛的证据，而且更希望某些真正惊人的事能够发生。”

En: But nothing 'truly surprising' has ever happened to you?
Zh: “但是从来就没有发生过什么‘真正惊人’的事吗？”

En: And has anything 'truly surprising' ever happened to you?
Zh: “那，有没有什么‘真正惊人’的事发生在你身上呢？”

En: Life's surprises are mostly miserable.
Zh: “生命中的惊喜大部分都是痛苦的。”

En: Agreed. Yet there is always a chance, albeit a small one, of a truly *good* surprise. One simply needs to look at the history of science -- serendipities abound!
Zh: “同意。但是机会仍然存在，即使很小，但却是真正的*好惊喜*。只需要看看科学的历史，就会发现——惊喜无处不在！”

En: No. As I said, I have yet to *catch* a cryptid. Although I have come close. Close enough to keep trying.
Zh: “没有。我说过了，我还没有*抓到*过神秘动物，不过已经很接近。近到只要继续尝试就好。”

En: I have yet to *catch* a cryptid, if that's what you're getting at, but I have come close.
Zh: “我还没有*抓到*过神秘动物，如果这就是你想问的话，不过我已经很接近了。”

En: So you're living your childhood dream out here.
Zh: “所以说，你还活在少年时代的梦想里。”

En: It's not child's play just because I have to traipse through the mud every so often. His eyes narrow.
Zh: 他眯起双眼。“这不是什么小孩的把戏，因为我经常要在泥地里走来走去。”

En: It's probably this way of thinking that got you where you are.
Zh: 也许正是这种思维方式让你走到了今天。

En: It could be worse. So much worse.
Zh: 也许会更糟，特别特别糟糕。

En: Everything from forgotten regional lore to newspaper accounts like the one that brought us here to look for the phasmid. I keep a very open mind.
Zh: “一切的一切——从被遗忘的地方传说，再到这种把我们引来寻找竹节虫的新闻报道。我一直保持着比较开放的心态。”

En: He's interested in things that people believe that scientists don't.
Zh: 他对人们认为科学家不感兴趣的东西感兴趣。

En: You think other scientists don't listen to ordinary people enough?
Zh: “你觉得其他科学家没有充分听取普通人的意见吗？”

En: Most *establishment* scientists only care about reputation and remuneration, not *real* research, and certainly not the truth. They're a cowardly lot, and both *the field* and basement archives can be dangerous places.
Zh: “大部分*机构*的科学家只在乎名声和报酬，不在乎*真正的*研究，当然也就不在乎事实。他们怯懦胆小，*现场*和地下档案室都可能是极为危险的地方。”

En: Yes, the Insulindian phasmid is a *very* clever insect -- that's why it's so damn difficult to catch! But as a scientist, I try my best to remain dispassionate.
Zh: “是的，伊苏林迪竹节虫是一种*非常*聪明的昆虫——所以才这么难抓到！但是作为一个科学家，我会尽最大努力让自己保持平和的心态。”

En: Who's there? Oh, the police. Hello, officers.
Zh: “谁啊？哦，是警察。好啊，警官们。”

En: Who's there? Oh, the police. Hello, officer.
Zh: “谁啊？哦，是警察。你好啊，警官。”

En: IS THAT THE POLICE? WHY ARE THE POLICE HERE?
Zh: “是警察吗？为什么警察会在这里？”

En: DON'T WORRY, GARY. I'LL HANDLE IT.
Zh: “别担心，盖理。我来处理。”

En: Develop an alternative theory about the missing locusts.
Zh: 想出另一套蝗虫丢失的理论。

En: Enthusiastic about phasmid.
Zh: 对竹节虫的热情。

En: Something up with the trap.
Zh: 陷阱有些问题。

En: Insulted Morell.
Zh: 羞辱莫雷尔。

En: Cuno's a hooligan.
Zh: 坤诺是个小流氓。

En: Saw Locust City.
Zh: 见过蝗城。

En: Considered alternatives.
Zh: 考虑替代选择。

En: Of course, the detective whispers to himself. "More clever..."
Zh: “当然了，”警探自言自语到。“更聪明一些……”

En: It's not very *scientific* to reason backward from a conclusion you *want* to be true.
Zh: “从你*想*得到的结论反推原因并不*科学*。”

En: I don't know. I'm not persuaded.
Zh: “我不知道。我不太相信。”

En: Are you sure you've exhausted all the alternative explanations?
Zh: “你确定已经用尽了所有可能的解释吗？”

En: She's engaging in a well-known self-deception called "motivated reasoning." You should *correct* them.
Zh: 她在进行一种众所周知的自欺欺人的行为，名为“动机性推理”。你应该*纠正*他们。

En: Hmph. I appreciate your concern, *officer*, but please leave this to the *experts*. Unless you have an *alternative* hypothesis you'd like to venture...
Zh: “感谢你的质疑，*警官*，但是请把这个问题留给*专家*来处理。除非你打算大胆提出*另一*假设……”

En: Why don't *you* try convincing Morell his *hypothesis* is invalid? He scowls.
Zh: 他愤怒地看着你。“*你*为什么不说服莫雷尔：他的*假设*是无效的呢？”

En: The cryptozoologist's face flushes with indignation. "Of course we have!"
Zh: 神秘动物学家的脸因为愤怒而胀的通红。“我们当然有了！”

En: You're jabbing at the soft underbelly of his psyche. He realizes he's gotten defensive.
Zh: 你戳中了他灵魂中柔软而薄弱的位置。他意识到自己必须开始防卫。

En: Consider the way the empty trap was disturbed, as though *shaken*. Most likely the hands of a young person -- hands small enough to fit inside the trap too.
Zh: 考虑到空陷阱被破坏的方式，好像是*被摇动过*。最有可能是一个年轻人的手——一双足够小的手，刚好能够伸到陷阱里去。

En: You have your suspicions, but nothing you can form into a *viable explanation*.
Zh: 你有自己的怀疑，但是还不能形成一个*可行的解释*。

En: Here's an idea. Maybe the locusts ate *themselves*?
Zh: “我有主意了。也许是蝗虫自己吃掉*自己*的？”

En: You should ask the redheaded boy. Cuno.
Zh: 你应该问问那个红发男孩。坤诺。

En: A little 'hooligan'? But what would a *child* want with bugs?\n
Zh: “一个小‘流氓’？哪来的*小孩*会愿意要*虫子*啊？”

En: Kids? What would a *child* want with bugs?
Zh: “小孩？哪来的*小孩*会想要虫子啊？”

En: A shadow of worry passes over the woman's face. "Oh, my dear Morell. You've been an old man for too long. Kids love to torment insects, almost as much as they love to torment old folks..."
Zh: 一阵担忧的阴影掠过女人的脸。“哦，我亲爱的莫雷尔，你已经老了太久了。小孩就喜欢折腾昆虫，几乎就跟他们喜欢折腾老年人一样……”

En: *Autocannibalistic locusts*? I appreciate your *unconventional* thinking, officer, but really, cryptids aren't your area of expertise.
Zh: “*自噬蝗虫*？我欣赏你这种*超出常规*的想法，警官，但是说真的，神秘动物学不是你的专长。”

En: It's okay, dear. *Most* hypotheses turn out to be false.
Zh: “没事的，亲爱的。*大部分*假想最后都是错的。”

En: You look down at your hands, but they don't have anything to tell you.
Zh: 你低头看着自己的双手，但它们没什么要告诉你的。

En: Right. Which means you two can pack up and go back to the Whirling.
Zh: “好吧。这就意味着你们俩可以卷起行李，回到飞旋旅社了。”

En: Whatever he thinks about this detour, it's clear that these men are exhausted and in need of assistance.
Zh: 不管他怎么看待这次旅行，这些人显然已经筋疲力尽，很需要帮助。

En: Thank you for your help. Gary and I will start breaking down camp. If you have any more questions, now's the time to ask! We'll be gone once you get to it.
Zh: “感谢你的帮助。盖理和我要开始拆营地了。如果你还有什么问题，现在赶紧问吧！等你到那里的时候，我们估计已经走了。”

En: FINALLY! Someone's talking sense!
Zh: “终于有人说了句讲理的话！”

En: Finally, you're being sensible! I'll start packing right now.
Zh: “你总算是明白事理了！我马上开始收拾。”

En: He's not comfortable with the possibility that you'll claim the find -- but he's lying about this even to himself.
Zh: 他对你宣告发现竹节虫的可能有些不太舒服——不过即使在这一点上，他也在对自己撒谎。

En: That's *highly* unlikely, officer. But, in the event you do... He takes out a small white spray can. "I'll spray you with a pheromone mixture I developed."
Zh: “那就*更*不可能了，警官。不过，如果遇到了……”他拿出一小瓶白色的喷雾壶。“我就给你喷一点自己开发的费洛蒙混合物。”

En: Yeah. That was me. I broke the water lock with my motor carriage. But it's fixed now. You can go back.
Zh: “没错。是我。我用汽車弄坏了水闸。不过现在已经修好了。你可以回去啦。”

En: You broke the water lock with a motor carriage? But there was a billboard in the canal... He looks south. "Not a vehicle. It said 'Samaran Butter.'"
Zh: “你用汽車弄坏了水闸？但是运河上有块广告牌……”他看向南方。“不是車。上面写着‘萨玛拉黄油’。”

En: No, see, I jumped *over* the canal in my motor carriage, *tearing* through the Samaran Butter Sign, which fell *into* the canal, blocking the gates. I used the pawnshop as a launch ramp.
Zh: “不是，是这样的，我开車*飞过*运河，*撞坏*了萨马拉黄油的标志，它*掉进*运河里，堵住了大门。我把当铺当做跳台了。”

En: I guess you're right. It was really the sign's fault. Anyway, you can go back now.
Zh: “我猜你是对的。确实是广告牌的错。不管怎么说，现在你可以回去了。”

En: Why? He spreads his arms in disbelief.
Zh: “为什么？”他怀疑地张开双臂。

En: Because I needed to drive into the ocean.
Zh: “因为我需要开到海里去。”

En: Because I'm insane.
Zh: “因为我疯了。”

En: It was an *experiment* to gauge the Coupris 40's aerial performance. I'm something of a gentleman scientist.
Zh: “那是一个*实验*，为了测试库普瑞斯40的航空性能。我也算是一个有绅士风度的科学家。”

En: I'm sorry. I don't know.
Zh: “很抱歉。我不知道。”

En: Insane. Yes. Okay. Good. He quickly comes to terms with it. "But you said the water lock is fixed now, so we can go back..."
Zh: “疯子。是的，好吧，很好。”他很快就接受了。“但是你说水闸现在修好了，我们可以回去……”

En: Sure. He quickly comes to terms with this. "But you said the water lock is fixed now, so we can go back..."
Zh: “当然。”他很快就接受了。“但是你说水闸现在修好了，我们可以回去……”

En: Scientist. Yes. Okay. He quickly comes to terms with it. "But you said the water lock is fixed now, so we can go back..."
Zh: “科学家。是的。好吧。”他很快就接受了。“但是你说水闸现在修好了，我们可以回去……”

En: DID HE SAY WE CAN GO BACK NOW?
Zh: “他说我们现在可以回去了？”

En: I just have to do one more round, see if the phasmid has taken the bait... Then we're going. He refastens a bit of netting that has come loose in the wind.
Zh: “我只用再逛一圈，看看有没有竹节虫上钩……然后我们就走。”他把被风吹散的网重新固定了一下。

En: This is a man possessed, always on the brink of some breakthrough. He won't leave if there's a sliver of hope *the Great Find* might happen today.
Zh: 这是个着了魔的男人，总是处在突破的边缘。如果今天还有出现*伟大发现*的可能，他是不会离开的。

En: YES, GARY! WE CAN GO SOON! He turns to you. "If you see Lena, tell her I won't be long."
Zh: “是的，盖理！我们很快就能走了！”他转向你。“如果你看见莉娜，告诉她我很快回来。”

En: No, no, it was all me. I caused your delay, and I'm sorry. But you can return now.
Zh: “不，不，都是我一个人的错。是我让你耽搁的，我很抱歉。不过现在你可以回去了。”

En: Good to see you again, officer. To what do I owe the pleasure?
Zh: “很高兴又见到你了，警官。我该怎么报答你呢？”

En: Hello! Lena and I were just discussing the design of the new trap.
Zh: “你好啊！莉娜和我正在讨论新陷阱的设计。”

En: Hello, officer! I think I almost have it! A new trap design, that is! I know you're sceptical, but I have a good feeling about this.
Zh: “你好啊，警官！我差不多就快想出来了！新陷阱的设计！我知道你很怀疑，但我自己感觉很好。”

En: Oh, hello, officer. He covers his mouth, struggling to suppress a coughing fit.
Zh: “哦，你好啊，警官。”他捂住嘴巴，努力压住咳嗽的冲动。

En: Sir, your wife is waiting for you.
Zh: “先生，你的妻子在等你。”
