En: YES, GARY! WE CAN GO BACK SOON! He turns to you. "If you see Lena, tell her I won't be long now."
Zh: “没错，盖理！我们很快就能回去了！”他转向你。“如果你看见莉娜，告诉她我很快回去。”

En: And we're not really the wildlife protection services.
Zh: “而且我们真不是野生动物保护协会的。”

En: The way he said the word *phasmid* made it sound full of mystery -- this is a very special word for him indeed.
Zh: 他说出*竹节虫*这个词的方式，让它听起来充满神秘感——对于他来说，这确实是一个非常特殊的词汇。

En: You're not here to hide a dead body, are you? I've been seeing a lot of dead bodies.
Zh: “你不是来这里藏尸的，对吧？我最近看见不少死尸。”

En: You're not here to hide a dead body, are you? I've been seeing a lot of dead bodies.
Zh: “你不是来这里藏尸的，对吧？我最近看见不少死尸。”

En: We'll be on our way soon. He gets up. "Thank you again for lending a hand."
Zh: “我们马上就走。”他站起身来。“再次感谢你的帮助。”

En: It's great to see you again, officer! He grins, bowing awkwardly. "My wife can't wait to thank you. Go on, talk to her."
Zh: “再见到你真是太高兴了，警官！”他咧嘴一笑，笨拙地鞠了一躬。“我的妻子等不及要谢你了。去吧，跟她聊聊。”

En: Ha ha, nothing like the gratitude of a good woman. Now, then, what can I do for you? He gives you a gruff pat on the shoulder.
Zh: “哈哈，没有什么比一个好女人的感激之情更好的了。现在，有什么我能帮你的吗？”他粗鲁地拍了拍你的肩膀。

En: I couldn't possibly shower thanks on you as enthusiastically as my wife has, he says with a friendly nod. "But I am grateful for your assistance, officer."
Zh: “我不可能像妻子那样热情地向你道谢，”他友好地点头说到，“但我很感激你的帮助，警官。”

En: There, there, dear. She gently touches her husband's arm. "Don't talk too much. And have some more tea."
Zh: “来了，亲爱的。”她轻轻地抚摸了一下丈夫的手臂。“别说太多话。多喝点茶吧。”

En: You broke the water lock with a motor carriage? But there was a billboard in the canal... He looks south. "Not a vehicle. It said 'Samaran Butter.'"
Zh: “你用汽車弄坏了水闸？但是运河上有块广告牌……”他看向南方。“不是車。上面写着‘萨马拉黄油’。”

En: No, see, I jumped *over* the canal in my motor carriage, *tearing* through the Samaran Butter Sign, which fell *into* the canal, blocking the gates, as I landed on the other side. I used the pawnshop as a launch ramp.
Zh: “不是，是这样的，我开車*飞过*运河，*撞坏*了萨马拉黄油的标志，它*掉进*运河里，堵住了大门。我把当铺当做跳台了。”

En: I guess you're right. It was really the sign's fault. Anyway, you can go back now.
Zh: “我猜你是对的。确实是标志的错。不管怎么说，现在你可以回去了。”

En: Why? He spreads his arms in disbelief.
Zh: “为什么？”他怀疑地张开双臂。

En: I'm afraid I may be insane.
Zh: “恐怕我可能是疯了。”

En: It was an *experiment* to gauge the Coupris 44's aerial performance. I'm something of a gentleman scientist.
Zh: “那是一个*实验*，为了测试库普瑞斯40的航空性能。我也算是一个有绅士风度的科学家。”

En: I'm sorry. I don't know.
Zh: “很抱歉。我不知道。”

En: Insane. Yes. Okay. Good. He quickly comes to terms with it. "But you said the water lock is fixed now, so we can go back..."
Zh: “疯子。是的，好吧，很好。”他很快就接受了。“但是你说水闸现在修好了，我们可以回去……”

En: No, no, it was all me. I caused your delay, and I'm sorry. But you can go back now.
Zh: “不，不，都是我一个人的错。是我让你耽搁的，我很抱歉。不过现在你可以回去了。”

En: Scientist. Yes. Okay. He quickly comes to terms with it. "But you said the water lock is fixed now, so we can go back..."
Zh: “科学家。是的。好吧。”他很快就接受了。“但是你说水闸现在修好了，我们可以回去……”

En: DID HE SAY WE CAN GO BACK NOW?
Zh: “他说我们现在可以回去了？”

En: Sure. He quickly comes to terms with this. "But you said the water lock is fixed now, so we can go back..."
Zh: “当然。”他很快就接受了。“但是你说水闸现在修好了，我们可以回去……”

En: Because I needed to get to the ocean.
Zh: “因为我要到海里去。”

En: YES, GARY! WE CAN GO SOON! He turns to you.
Zh: “是的，盖理！我们很快就能走了！”他转向你。

En: YES, GARY! WE CAN GO BACK SOON! He turns to you.
Zh: “是的，盖理！我们很快就能走了！”他转向你。

En: MAYBE THE WATER LOCK IS FIXED!
Zh: “也许水闸已经修好了！”

En: We were supposed to go back to Martinaise a couple of days ago, but that damned water lock is broken. He gives a dismissive wave.
Zh: 他不屑地挥挥手。“我们几天前就该回到马丁内斯的，但是那个该死的水闸坏掉了。”

En: Oh good. We should really be getting back. Gary could use a hot shower and a warm bed...
Zh: “很好啊。我们真的应该回去了。盖理需要洗个热水澡，还有一张温暖的床……”

En: The water lock's been fixed. It was fine when I crossed it. (Withold the whole story.)
Zh: “水闸已经修好了。我走过来的时候就是好的。”（隐瞒完整的故事。）

En: The water lock's been fixed. It was fine when I crossed it.
Zh: “水闸已经修好了。我走过来的时候就是好的。”

En: Yeah. That was me. I broke the water lock with my motor carriage. But it's fixed now. You can go back.
Zh: “没错。是我。我用汽車弄坏了水闸。不过现在已经修好了。你可以回去啦。”

En: He's the kind of robust man who feels most at home outdoors, doing strenuous work in places where most people wouldn't venture.
Zh: 他是那种很粗旷的男人，在户外感觉到最自在，喜欢去大部分人不敢去的地方，做些费力的工作。

En: Either way, have a safe trip back! [Leave.]
Zh: “不管怎么说，祝你旅途平安！”[离开。]

En: Hello hello! He gets up. "What can I do for you, officer?"
Zh: “哎呀，你好啊！”他站起身来。“有什么能帮你的吗，警官？”

En: If you see Lena, tell her I won't be long, he says, getting up. "I'm so close to being done here..."
Zh: “如果你见着莉娜，告诉她我很快回去，”他一边说，一边站起身。“我差一点就要弄完了……”

En: You must be Lena's husband, Morell. That's why you're here.
Zh: “你一定是莉娜的丈夫，莫雷尔。所以你会待在这里。”

En: We were supposed to go back to Martinaise a couple of days ago, but that damned water lock is broken. He gives a dismissive wave.
Zh: 他不屑地挥挥手。“我们几天前就该回到马丁内斯的，但是那个该死的水闸坏掉了。”

En: Yes, yes, I am. He looks at you with mild surprise, then quickly recovers --
Zh: “是的，没错，我是。”他有些吃惊的看着你，然后很快恢复了原样——

En: The water lock's been fixed. It was fine when I crossed it. (Withold the whole story.)
Zh: “水闸已经修好了。我走过来的时候就是好的。”（隐瞒完整的故事。）

En: The water lock's been fixed. It was fine when I crossed it.
Zh: “水闸已经修好了。我走过来的时候就是好的。”

En: This is a slippery one -- he may respect the law, but mostly wants to pass under the radar. You won't get a chance to talk after you walk out of here.
Zh: 这可够狡猾的——他也许是尊重法律，但主要还是想低调行事。在你走出这里之后，就很难找到机会跟他说话了。

En: He hasn't been particularly forthcoming before. He may well still be hiding something. After he's left it's too late.
Zh: 他以前并没有特别坦诚过。说不定还隐瞒着什么事情。等他离开之后，一切就太迟了。

En: I think a little hooligan called Cuno may have stolen the locusts.
Zh: “我觉得是一个叫坤诺的小混混偷走了蝗虫。”

En: I may have met a kid that's the locust stealing type.
Zh: “我见过一个小孩，他可能是会偷走蝗虫的那种类型。”

En: I have a feeling some local kids may have stolen the locusts.
Zh: “我有种感觉，可能是本地小孩偷走了蝗虫。”

En: I'll talk to the little gremlin and see if anything comes up.
Zh: “我会跟那些小鬼聊聊，看看能不能打听到什么。”

En: I'll look around and see if the kids in Martinaise know anything.
Zh: “我会去马丁内斯转转，看看有没有小孩知道些什么。”

En: Well, I see you've got all the help you need. The man turns to his companions. "I'll see you tonight at my place -- let's play 'Suzerainty' -- but no more field trips for me."
Zh: 男人转向他的同伴。“呃，我想你已经得到所有想要的帮助了。今晚在我家见——我们一起玩‘大领主’——不过我再也不要搞什么实地考察了。”

En: We were supposed to go back to Martinaise a couple of days ago, but that damned water lock is broken. He gives a dismissive wave.
Zh: 他不屑地挥挥手。“我们几天前就该回到马丁内斯的，但是那个该死的水闸坏掉了。”

En: I'm looking for a suspect. Have you seen a woman with red hair who seemed to be on the run?
Zh: “我在找一个嫌疑人。你有没有见过一个红头发的女人，好像是在逃跑？”

En: I'm looking for a suspect. Have you seen anyone who appeared to be on the run?
Zh: “我在找一个嫌疑人。你有没有像是在逃跑的人？”

En: It's made of musk and research chemicals. The pheromones *should* attract the insect to you, or at least prevent it from bolting at the sight of you. It's quite potent, will last you about a week.
Zh: “它是麝香和研究化学物质制成的。这种费洛蒙*应该*可以把昆虫吸引到你身上，或者至少可以防止它们一见到你就逃跑。它的药效挺强的，至少可以持续一星期。”

En: This is the smell of dying reeds, of longing crumbling into the water.
Zh: 这是枯萎芦苇的味道，是渴望坠入水中的味道。

En: How big is this phasmid?
Zh: “这个竹节虫有多大？”

En: I'm expecting it to be quite giant. One known species of phasmid, called the *megaphasmodea zhouensis*, is about the size of a grown man's forearm. So... He leaves the conclusion up to you.
Zh: “我估计会非常大。一种名为*巨型竹节虫*的已知竹节虫，尺寸跟成年男子的小臂差不多。所以……”他把结论留给了你。

En: It's not so different from the putrefaction of the hanged man, actually.
Zh: 其实，这和吊人身上的腐败物没什么不同。

En: Did you hear that -- pheromones! You were right about the teenagers...
Zh: 你听见了吗——费洛蒙！你对青少年的看法是对的……

En: So it really *was* the phasmid that made the teenagers make out...
Zh: “所以说，其实*是*竹节虫，让这些年轻人亲热的……”

En: Oh, I *doubt* there's any connection there. Anyway, these pheromones will attract the insect to you, or at least prevent it from bolting at the sight of you. It's quite potent, should last you about a week.
Zh: “哦，我*怀疑*两者并没有什么联系。不管怎么说，这些费洛蒙会把昆虫吸引到你身上，或者至少可以防止它们一见到你就逃跑。它的药效挺强的，至少可以持续一星期。”

En: The lieutenant wrinkles his nose. "I hope you're not buying this. He dispenses it without letting you touch the canister, so it would be precious, like holy water..."
Zh: 警督皱了皱鼻子。“希望你不要相信这些。他分给你的时候都没让你碰那个罐子，所以也许挺珍贵的，像圣水一样……”

En: For all his passion, this man is diligent and patient. You could learn things from him.
Zh: 尽管充满激情，这个男人还是勤勉又有耐心。你可以从他身上学到很多东西。

En: His hands are large and weather-worn, but also used to delicate, precise work.
Zh: 他的手很大，而且饱经风霜，不过也习惯了精妙、细致的工作。

En: How about rekindling that camp fire so we can dry off?
Zh: “不如重新把篝火点燃，这样我们就能把自己烘干？”

En: Gary, what are you doing there?
Zh: “盖理，你在做什么呢？”

En: Don't you remember that method I showed you back in... never mind.
Zh: “难道你不记得我教给你的方法了吗……没事了。”

En: A lavender shadow?
Zh: “一个淡紫色的影子？”

En: Yes. I know it sounds like we were snacking on funny mushrooms -- but I did see motion and colour. Mine is not a *confirmed* sighting of a cryptid, however. Much goes into *verifying* these things, and my account comes short of a few standards.
Zh: “是的。我知道，听起来很像是我们吃了什么古怪的蘑菇——但是我看见它的动作和颜色了。不过，这次看见神秘动物的经历并没有经过*确认*。*验证*这些东西需要花费很多精力，而我的账户没有达一些标准。”

En: He makes it a real point here -- to sound falsifiable.
Zh: 他提出了一个真正的观点——这样听起来是可以被验证的。

En: Furthermore, he raises his finger. "I am not saying it was a *confirmed* sighting. I am painfully aware of what goes into verifying such things. There is a serious possibility that I saw a squirrel, or a trick of the light. I am my own harshest critic."
Zh: “而且，”他竖起手指。“我并没有说这是*确认的*目击行为。我非常清楚验证这些事情需要花费多少精力。我很可能是看见了一只松鼠，或者是光线的幻影。我是对自己最苛刻的批评家。”

En: And Lena's sighting of the Phasmid. Is that...
Zh: “那莉娜看见竹节虫的事。也是……”

En: Let's talk about a different cryptid.
Zh: “我们聊聊不同的神秘动物吧。”

En: Confirmed, he replies quickly. "It's 100% verified and meets all the standards of an authentic cryptid sighting."
Zh: “确认过的，”他飞快地回应到。“那件事经过了100%验证，满足了真正目击到神秘动物的所有标准。”

En: The woman nods, thoughtfully, while her hands smooth over the plaid covering her knees.
Zh: 老妇人若有所思的点点头，用手抚平了膝盖上铺着的格子花呢。

En: There is *some* uncertainty in that motion, no? Smoothing the fabric like that, as if looking for some kind of confirmation...
Zh: 这个动作里好像包含着*某些*不确定性，是吗？像那样抚平面料，就好像在寻求某种确认……

En: *...we would have to discuss?* he wants to say, but decides against it.
Zh: *……我们有什么好讨论的？*他本想这么说，但还是决定不这么做。

En: You need to ask him about specific cryptids -- cryptids you've heard about from Lena, or his friend Gary. He won't *just* talk.
Zh: 你需要向他打听一些特定的神秘动物——-你从莉娜，或是他的朋友盖理那里听到的神秘动物。他不会*就这样*轻易开口的。

En: *...we would have to discuss?* he wants to say, but decides against it since you've offered to help.
Zh: *……我们有什么好讨论的？*他本想这么说，不过既然你提出要帮忙，他还是决定不这么做了。

En: You need to ask him about specific cryptids -- cryptids you've heard about from Lena, or his friend Gary. He won't *just* talk.
Zh: 你需要向他打听一些特定的神秘动物——-你从莉娜，或是他的朋友盖理那里听到的神秘动物。他不会*就这么*轻易说话的。

En: A warm wave passes him. Of course the *kind* green ape is her favourite, he thinks.
Zh: 一阵暖流从他身边经过。当然了，*善良*绿人猿是她的最爱，他想到。

En: WHAT? SOUTH SAFRE?! THEY'RE JUST ON A DIFFERENT RUNG OF THE LADDER, MORELL! I HAD NO *PROBLEM* WITH THEM!
Zh: “什么？南萨弗里？！它们只是同一条进化道路上的不同阶段罢了，莫雷尔！我对它们完全没有任何*意见*！”

En: REALLY? You kept complaining how dirty everything is, but we digress...
Zh: “真的吗？你总是抱怨一切都很脏，好吧，我们离题了……”

En: THEY'RE A *NASCENT CULTURE*. I just didn't feel comfortable, and... Let's change the topic, okay? Talk about your critters or whatever... He mumbles something.
Zh: “它们是*新兴文化*。我只是感觉不太舒服，而且……我们还是换个话题，好吗？聊聊你的小动物，或者其他什么的……”他的嘴里嘟囔着。

En: Yes. Back to cryptozoology. You don't want to sow disagreement between friends.
Zh: 是的。回到神秘动物学上。你可不想挑起朋友之间的不和。

En: He does not want to make it feel like you knowing it is some big deal.
Zh: 他不想让你觉得这是一件大事。

En: The... dread moose?
Zh: “恐怖……驼鹿？”

En: Get outta here!
Zh: “少来，死开啦！”

En: No. I will not *get* out of anywhere. He lashes immediately. "The dread moose is a widely reported cryptid from Arda. It subsists entirely on flesh, and has even been known to dig up fresh graves in search of sustenance."
Zh: “不。我不会*死开*的。”他立马就回嘴了。“恐怖驼鹿是阿尔达广泛报道的一种神秘动物。它完全依靠肉食为生，甚至还会挖掘新坟来寻找食物。”

En: I am 100% behind the dread moose. I *utterly* believe it exists.
Zh: “我100%撑恐怖驼鹿。我*绝对*相信它的存在。”

En: Of course you do.
Zh: “你当然信了。”

En: The bodies found in the forest are just one piece of physical evidence. There's more -- sightings in Vaasa, reaching back *four centuries*. But, of course, nothing satiates the scepticism of...
Zh: “森林里发现的尸体只是物证的一部分。还有更多——瓦萨城有不少目击报告，一直可以追溯到*4世纪*。不过当然了，没什么能满足那些怀疑论……”

En: A *detective*. He finishes the sentence for him, then his tone turns surprising mild. "Pardon me, I did not wish to seek conflict. It's simply my training to question things."
Zh: “一名*警探*的怀疑论。”警督替他完成了句子，之后他的口吻变得出奇的温和。“请原谅，我不想引起什么冲突。只是我接受的训练就是要问问题。”

En: Understood, lieutenant. The man tips his hat slightly and then looks to you.
Zh: “我能理解，警督。”男人轻轻抬了抬帽子，然后把目光转向你。

En: You're right to. He gives you an approving tip of his hat. "The bodies found in the forest are just one piece of physical evidence. We also have sightings in Vaasa, reaching back *four centuries*."
Zh: “你是对的。”他抬起帽子，对你的话表示赞同。“森林里发现的尸体只是物证的一部分。瓦萨城也有过目击报告，一直可以追溯到*4世纪*。”

En: In fact, we may be close to capturing a live specimen. Modern genetic methods may prove *crucial* in differentiating the dread variant from the herbivorous one. Anyway... He gestures for you to continue.
Zh: “事实上，我们也许就快抓到一个活的物种了。在区分食肉和食草变体时，现代遗传学方法也许是*至关重要的*。总之……”他向你比了个手势，接着继续说了下去。

En: Another one? he seems angry for a second, but then settles down.
Zh: “还来？”有那么一两秒，他好像很生气，不过很快平静下来。

En: Anyway... He settles down. "There is *more* than enough evidence to justify a thorough *search* for the dread moose. Let's close the subject, before it turns into an argument."
Zh: “总之……”他平静下来。“需要彻底*搜查*恐怖驼鹿这一点，是有*足够多*的证据来支撑的。这个话题就到此为止吧，再这样下去就要变成辩论会了。

En: Here is a man who has had more than his fair share of heated arguments -- and would, surprising as it may seem, prefer not to have any more.
Zh: 这是一个在激烈争论中经常占据上风的男人——不过，可能让人觉得惊讶的是：他反而觉得还是不要再继续下去的好。

En: Me -- I'm not a people person, unless you haven't noticed. And I don't make a good lecturer. My strength lies in field work and *persistence*. He brushes an errant strand of hair from his eye.
Zh: “我——我不是一个擅长跟人打交道的人，除非你没发现这一点。而且我也不是个好演说家。我的长处是野外考察和*坚持*。”他用手撇开那缕搭在眼睛上的头发。

En: This is a gruff man who's been ridiculed too many times to feel comfortable talking about what's dearest to his heart. It's in his shoulders, his face, his... everything.
Zh: 这是一个被奚落过太多次的坏脾气男人，谈起心中最宝贵东西的时候，反而会感觉到不太自在。他的肩膀，脸上，他的……一切都在传递这种信息。

En: I fear this mindless barbarism may have wiped out the elusive creature entirely. Sightings of *towering luminosities* have grown rare recently. While they once used to be constant...
Zh: “我担心这种鲁莽又野蛮的行为会完全抹杀掉这种难以捉摸的生物。最近看见*高耸入云的光辉*的报告越来越少。以前它们挺常见的……”

En: Yes. Sightings of *mirages* are constant. A mirage is a constant phenomenon that people have no time to *report* when a war is going on.
Zh: 没错。看见*海市蜃楼*是挺常见的。它本身就是一种寻常的光学现象，而且在战争年代，谁会有时间去*报告*这种事情呢。

En: It remains unclear what this has got to do with *you seeing it*, as he was inquiring before. He was just being defensive.
Zh: 就像他之前问的，*你看见过*跟这件事到底有什么关系，最后还是不清不楚。这只是他在自我辩护。

En: That's what I thought. You haven't *actually* seen it. In fact, I can't imagine how you could either -- the Samarskilt desert region has been embroiled in a civil war for the last eight years.
Zh: “我也是这么想的。你其实并没有*亲眼*见过。而且我也无法想象你怎么能看到——萨马斯特沙漠区域过去八年一直处于内战之中。”

En: Of course you haven't. He seems satisfied.
Zh: “你当然没有了。”他好像很满意。

En: A bit of jealousy there. He'd want that glory, truth be told. Even if he'd have to inject *himself* with bacteria to get it.
Zh: 你感觉到一丝妒忌。老实说，他想要那份荣耀。就算是要给*自己*注射细菌，也在所不惜。

En: Better not to go into the Nnong Okk.
Zh: “最好别深究努奥克克了。”

En: Yes. Probably. Another cough. His face grows reddish from it.
Zh: “是啊，也许吧。”又是一声咳嗽，让他的脸变得有些发红。

En: When it's not hunting its prey in this manner, the Okk hangs from tree branches, like a bat, waiting to dive on hapless prey below, on the jungle floor.
Zh: “当它不用这种方式狩猎的时候，努奥克克就会像蝙蝠一样挂在树枝上，等待冲向丛林下方倒霉的猎物。”

En: When did she disappear?
Zh: “她什么时候失踪的？”

En: Oh, decades ago. In the Thirties. I didn't know her personally, of course. A chasm of academic pretension still stood before us, even though she had unusual courage for someone from the other side.
Zh: “啊，十几年前了吧，30年代的事。当然了，我并不认识她本人。我们面前仍然有一道学术主张的鸿沟，即使是在对面的某些人看来，她也拥有非同寻常的勇气。”

En: By all means... He coughs and wipes his mouth.
Zh: “请务必如此……”他咳嗽几声，然后擦了擦嘴唇。

En: Oh, the woman chimes in. "Any time -- just ask. I know those critters in and out. Not quite at the level Morell does, but still..."
Zh: “哦，”老妇人插了进来。“随时可以——只要你开口。我知道那些小动物的来龙去脉。虽然水平不如莫雷尔，不过……”

En: Let's talk about a different cryptid.
Zh: “我们聊聊别的神秘动物吧。”

En: Honestly, I doubt you'd find any of it comprehensible.
Zh: “老实说，我怀疑你根本理解不了。”

En: Okay... He eyes you sceptically. "I already did my best to inform you. I don't really know what else I could discuss with a layman..."
Zh: “好吧……”他疑惑地看着你。“我已经尽力跟你解释了。我真的不知道还能跟一个门外汉讨论些什么……”

En: Layman? You always have to prove yourself with this man. Which means -- *you* have to provide the cryptids. *Maybe* he'll answer.
Zh: 门外汉？你总是要在这个男人面前证明自己。这就意味着——*你*必须找到神秘动物。如此一来，他*也许*会回应。

En: Willow people? Not at *all*.
Zh: 柳树人？根本没*前途*。

En: All right. He remains guarded.
Zh: “好吧。”他还是很谨慎。

En: Be friendly, dear, the woman says. "The detective really likes these critters, we've talked about them in great *detail*."
Zh: “友好点，亲爱的，”老妇人说到。“警探先生是真心喜欢这些小动物，我们已经非常*详细地*讨论过了。”

En: Now the man feels embarrassed. Time to move on perhaps?
Zh: 现在男人觉得有些尴尬了。也许是时候说再见了？

En: Your friend Gary told me about the burning rhino.
Zh: “你的朋友盖理告诉我燃烧犀牛的事。”

En: Did he? Well -- that one's a hoax. Some Seraise rice farmers set fire to rhinoceros cadavers and used them scare tourists.
Zh: “他有吗？呃——那是恶作剧。有些萨弗里的米农点燃了犀牛尸体，想要吓唬游客的。”

En: Did he? He lowers his voice. "That one's a hoax. Some Seraise rice farmers set fire to rhinoceros cadavers and used them scare tourists."
Zh: “他有吗？”他压低声音。“那是恶作剧。有群萨弗里的米农点燃了犀牛尸体，想要吓唬游客的。”

En: Gary obliviously stares at the stage -- safe in the knowledge the burning rhinoceros exists.
Zh: 盖理显然是在凝视着舞台——在他的意识里，燃烧犀牛还安然无恙的存在着。

En: Gary obliviously stares at the stage -- safe in the knowledge the burning rhinoceros exists.
Zh: 盖理显然是在凝视着舞台——在他的意识里，燃烧犀牛还安然无恙的存在着。

En: Have you told Gary this?
Zh: “你跟盖理说过这件事吗？”

En: What if the other cryptids are hoaxes too?
Zh: “如果其他神秘动物也是恶作剧呢？”

En: I knew it can't be real. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我就知道不可能是真的。”（总结。）

En: Many times. It always turns into an argument. I don't want to repeat it. The rhino holds a special place in his heart -- let it. Myths are part of my field.
Zh: “很多次了。最终都会变成一场辩论。我不想再重复这个话题。犀牛在他心里占据了很特殊的位置——那就这样吧。本来神话也属于我的研究领域。”

En: It's a *gut feeling*. He knows it exists, fuck the so-called *specialists*.
Zh: 这是一种*直觉*。他知道它是存在的，让那些所谓的*专家*见鬼去吧。

En: Many of them probably are. Statistically speaking, about 20% of all cryptids are verified hoaxes. Uncovering falsehoods deliberately fabricated to fool the public is just as much my calling as finding new species.
Zh: “很多都可能是的。从统计数据上看，大约有20%的神秘动物已经被证实是恶作剧。揭露为了欺骗公众而精心编造的谎言，和发现新物种一样，都是我的使命。”

En: If perhaps *slightly* less enjoyable.
Zh: 或者会*稍微*没那么愉快。

En: What if you're wrong? What if it's real? (Conclude.)
Zh: “如果你错了呢？如果它是真的呢？”（总结。）

En: I doubt that. Once confirmed a hoax, so-called sightings of the rhino stopped. A cough. "Was there anything else, or..."
Zh: “我深表怀疑。一旦被证实是恶作剧，所谓的犀牛目击情报就会终止。”他咳嗽一声。“还有其他事吗，或者……”

En: Did you? He seems incredulous. "It is almost as difficult to confirm a hoax as it is to confirm a sighting..." A cough.
Zh: “是吗？”他看起来有些怀疑。“证实是恶作剧，基本跟确认目击证明一样困难……”他咳嗽了一声。

En: See Kim, one of them is a hoax.
Zh: “看见了吗，金，其中有一个是恶作剧。”

En: Which one? He hasn't been paying attention.
Zh: “哪一个？”他好像根本没注意。

En: The burning rhinoceros.
Zh: “燃烧犀牛。”

En: Mhm. The *burning rhino* is where they draw the line. He's unimpressed.
Zh: “嗯。*燃烧犀牛*是他们的底线。”他毫无兴趣。

En: He's a good and loyal man despite his ramblings, the elderly cryptozoologist thinks to himself.
Zh: 虽然经常说些漫无边际的话，但他是个善良而又忠诚的好人，年迈的神秘动物学家这样想到。

En: Their relationship transcends Gary's politics. It must -- to work. Plus, a guy like Morell knows a thing or two about being on the fringes himself, though his fringes are... different.
Zh: 他们的关系超越了盖理的政治观念。它必须——这样继续下去。再说了，像莫雷尔这种人，基本搞不清楚自己到底有多边缘，尽管他的边缘……有些不太一样。

En: He eyes you sceptically. "All right -- what cryptids, precisely? I usually discuss these things with *specialists*, so I don't know what..."
Zh: 他怀疑地看着你。“好吧——准确的说，是什么神秘动物？我经常跟*专家*讨论这些事情，所以我不知道……”

En: I know about the most dangerous cryptid -- the Gnome of Geroma.
Zh: “我知道最危险的神秘动物——赫罗玛侏儒。”

En: A moose that looks like any other moose... What's going on here? He's kidding right?
Zh: 一头驼鹿，看起来跟其他驼鹿一模一样……这是怎么回事？他是在开玩笑吧？

En: I know the biggest cryptid -- the Giant of Koko Nur.
Zh: “我知道最大的神秘动物——科科努尔巨人。”

En: I know about *Cryobacter katlensis*.
Zh: “我知道*冷凝细菌卡特兰西斯*。”

En: Tell me about willow people again.
Zh: “再跟我说说柳树人。”

En: Which cryptid did you *almost* catch? You said before, that you almost caught one.
Zh: “你*差点*抓住的神秘动物是什么？你之前说过的，差点就抓到的那个。”

En: I know all about the kind green ape! Look, I even have a kind green ape pen! (Take out the pen)
Zh: “我知道善良绿人猿！看，我甚至还有一只善良绿人猿的笔！”（掏出笔）

En: I know all about the kind green ape!
Zh: “我知道善良绿人猿的一切！”

En: *Formerly* the most dangerous, yes... But do you know the most dangerous *living* cryptid?
Zh: “*以前*是最危险的，没错……但是你知道*现存*的最危险的神秘动物是什么吗？”

En: Hold on... Does it also attack people?
Zh: “等等……它也会攻击人类吗？”

En: Living? No.
Zh: “现存的？不知道。”

En: A lavender shadow... He smirks.
Zh: “一个淡紫色的影子……”他幸灾乐祸的笑了。

En: I can try to guess.
Zh: “我可以试着猜一下。”

En: That must be the evil apes dukin' it out on the giant ball.
Zh: “一定是在巨大的球体上一决雌雄的邪恶人猿。”

En: The what?
Zh: “什么？”

En: The evil apes. I suspect I may be one myself.
Zh: “邪恶人猿。我怀疑自己也是其中之一。”

En: We are descended from evil apes and to apes we shall return.
Zh: “我们是邪恶人猿的后代，最终也会回归为人猿。”

En: Oh, you're just talking about *humans*. Well, yes, we are quite dangerous, but we're hardly cryptids. He corrects his hat and says -- as if it's the most sensible thing in the world...
Zh: “哦，你说的是*人类*。呃，没错，我们是很危险，但我们算不上什么神秘动物。”他整了整自己的帽子，然后回答到——就好像这是世界上最合乎情理的事……

En: Just like an ordinary Ardan moose.
Zh: “就像一只普通的亚尔当驼鹿。”

En: That's what I thought. You haven't *actually* seen it. And I can't imagine how you could either -- the Samarskilt desert region has been embroiled in a civil war for the last eight years.
Zh: “我也是这么想的。你其实并没有*亲眼*见过。而且我也无法想象你怎么能看到——萨马斯特沙漠区域过去八年一直处于内战之中。”

En: The most dangerous cryptid is a carnivorous ruminant, known colloquially as the *dread moose*.
Zh: “最危险的神秘动物是一种肉食反刍动物，通俗地说，就是*恐怖驼鹿*。”

En: But that's not all! Some of the slaughterhouse apprentices went hiking by a nearby creek and saw a moose nibbling on an unidentified carcass!
Zh: “但这还不是全部！一群屠宰场学徒去附近的一条小溪边远足，然后看见一只驼鹿正在啃咬一具难以辨认的尸骸！”

En: That's impressive, I guess. He does not seem that impressed. "But have you *seen* it with your own eyes?"
Zh: “我猜一定给人留下了很深刻的印象吧。”他看起来并没有被打动。“但是你自己亲眼*见过*吗？”

En: Oh, everyone knows about that one, thanks to professor Mijanou being the talk of the town for a time... He coughs in his fist.
Zh: “哦，所有人都知道这个，多亏了米伽欧教授，这一度成为了全城热议的话题……”他把手拳在嘴边，咳嗽了起来。

En: I see you've been talking about cryptids with Lena, he smiles. "The kind green ape is one of her favourites."
Zh: “我猜你一定是跟莉娜讨论过神秘动物了。”他笑了。“善良绿人猿是她的最爱之一。”

En: I'm guessing you heard about it from Lena, he smiles. "The kind green ape is one of her favourites."
Zh: “我猜你是从莉娜那里听说的吧，”他笑了。“善良绿人猿是她的最爱之一。”

En: I haven't had a chance to travel to *Koko Nur*, no. And I likely never will. The Samarskilt desert region has been embroiled in a small civil war for the last eight years.
Zh: “我还没找到机会去*科科努尔*。可能永远也去不了。萨马斯特沙漠区域过去八年一直处于内战之中。”

En: We travelled to South Safre to look for it once. Gary and I got stuck in a rainstorm, though, and had to spend most of our time there in a little village.
Zh: “我们曾经去过一次南萨弗里寻找它。不过盖理和我被一场大暴雨困住了，大部分时间都只能待在一个小村庄里。”

En: The search was fabulously unsuccessful -- but the people were very nice. I'm glad they didn't understand what Gary was saying about them...
Zh: “那次搜寻真是失败地让人难以置信——不过那里的人很好。还好他们不明白盖理到底说了些什么……”

En: I was lying. I haven't actually seen it. I just wanted to impress you.
Zh: “我在撒谎。我其实没有亲眼见过。我只是想打动你。”

En: They're very, very thin, almost flat, in fact, and can camouflage themselves easily, wrapping themselves around trees and blending in with the tree bark. In that way, they're not too dissimilar from the phasmid we are looking for here.
Zh: “它们非常、非常的薄，事实上几乎是平的，而且可以轻易地伪装自己，把自己包裹在树叶之中，跟树干融入在一起。这样看来，它们跟我们正在寻找的竹节虫并没有太大的区别。”

En: Wait, so I may have seen these willow people without knowing it?
Zh: “等等，所以我可能见过柳树人，但是自己却不知道？”

En: How did you almost catch a willow person?
Zh: “你是怎么差一点抓到柳树人的？”

En: You probably have!
Zh: “确实有这个可能！”

En: I'm having trouble remembering...
Zh: “我的记忆有点问题……”

En: Remarkable! When?
Zh: “真是太神奇了！什么时候？”

En: (Truth.) "No."
Zh: （真话。）“没有。”

En: A willow person. It's a long story, one non-specialists would find rather dull.
Zh: “一个柳树人。这是个很长的故事，如果不是专业人士的话，肯定会觉得挺枯燥的。”

En: (Lie.) "Yes."
Zh: （撒谎。）“见过。”

En: What are willow people?
Zh: “柳树人是什么？”

En: Have *you* seen it?
Zh: “那*你*见过吗？”

En: They're not people, really -- some argue that they're not even animals, as they seem to have evolved directly from trees. He says it in a self-explanatory, every day manner.
Zh: “它们并不是真正的人——有些人认为它们甚至都算不上是动物，因为它们似乎是从树木直接进化而来的。”他以一种稀松平常、不需要加以说明的方式陈述着。

En: This isn't something unique. Various species of deer have been known to scavenge when plant food is scarce.
Zh: 这没有什么好稀罕的。很多人已经知道，当植物性食物匮乏的时候，不同种类的鹿之间也会彼此蚕食。

En: Mijanou liked *extreme* animals, you see. One of the few figures of the academic establishment I respect. Really a shame she disappeared.
Zh: “你也看见了，米伽欧喜欢*极端*的动物。学术机构里我尊重的人不多，她就是其中之一。她的失踪真的很可惜。”

En: Enough tales then. Let's change the subject.
Zh: “传说已经说的够多了。我们换个话题吧。”

En: A flightless *cursor owl* found in the Semenine isles. Its long legs permit the Nnong Okk to run faster than any other avian, perhaps any other *animal*, who knows?
Zh: “在塞美尼群岛发现的一种不会飞的‘光标猫头鹰’。那双长腿能让努奥克克跑得比任何鸟类、也许是任何其他*动物*都快，谁知道呢?”

En: Believe me, you won't *guess* it. You either know, or you don't. He corrects his hat and says -- as if it's the most sensible thing in the world...
Zh: “相信我，你*猜*不到的。你要么知道，要么不知道。”他整了整自己的帽子，然后回答到——就好像这是世界上最合乎情理的事……

En: The Nnong Okk?
Zh: “努奥克克？”

En: Gary and I painted an entire grove's worth of trees in slow-drying paint. It was a bright lavender colour. I was hoping one of the willow people would get paint on it and not be able to camouflage itself.
Zh: “盖理和我用慢干油漆涂满了一整片树林的每一棵树。那是一种明亮的淡紫色。我希望能有一个柳树人沾上油漆，这样它就不能伪装自己了。”

En: After waiting in hiding for hours, I saw a figure slip from one of the trees, a lavender shadow dashing through the grove...
Zh: “隐蔽几小时之后，我看见一个影子从其中一颗树上滑下来，一个淡紫色的影子飞快地穿过树林……”

En: Although -- probably because her life ended as a result of her work in Katla -- no one remembers her contributions to the search for the Nnong Okk.
Zh: “虽然——可能是因为卡特拉的工作让她的生活画上了句号——没人记得她对努奥克克研究做出的贡献。”

En: Those people were on a different rung of the ladder of civilization, that's all.
Zh: “那些人处于文明阶梯的不同阶段，仅此而已。”

En: You kept complaining about how dirty they were.
Zh: “你一直在抱怨他们到底有多脏。”

En: And I realize that their hygiene habits were completely appropriate for a nascent culture... I just didn't feel comfortable. Let's change the topic.
Zh: “然后我意识到，他们的卫生习惯完全符合一种新兴的文化……我感觉有点不太舒服。我们换个话题吧。”

En: I know you think we were snacking on funny mushrooms. It's easier to mock someone than to admit that the world might be more interesting than you've imagined.
Zh: “我知道你会觉得我们是吃了些奇奇怪怪的蘑菇。承认世界远比你想象中的有趣很难，嘲笑别人显然要容易得多。”

En: And that's not all! Some of the slaughterhouse apprentices went hiking by a nearby creek and saw a moose nibbling on an unidentified carcass!
Zh: “但这还不是全部！一群屠宰场学徒去附近的一条小溪边远足，然后看见一只驼鹿正在啃咬一具难以辨认的尸骸！”

En: Okay. What does the dread moose look like?
Zh: “好吧。那这只恐怖驼鹿长什么样？”

En: Yes. The dread moose subsists entirely on flesh. It has even been known to dig up fresh graves in search of sustenance.
Zh: “没错。恐怖驼鹿完全是食肉为生的。已经证实它甚至会挖掘新坟来寻找食物。”

En: Human remains *have* been found deep in the forest, torn apart, then trampled into the mulch by large hooves. Infer from *that* what you will.
Zh: “曾经在森林深处*发现过*人类残骸——被撕裂，然后被巨大的蹄子踩进了地膜。从*那件事*，你能推断出什么。”

En: I chased it with a net -- not very elegant, but you can't be elegant in the field -- and, well, it was faster than me!
Zh: “我拿着一张网追赶它——不是很优雅，但是在现场也谈不上什么优雅——然后，好吧，它速度比我快！”

En: And then?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: Then how can you tell if it's the ordinary or the dread kind?
Zh: “那你该怎么分辨它到底是普通驼鹿，还是恐怖驼鹿呢？”

En: You can't! That's what makes it so dreadful... and hard to identify.
Zh: “不能！这就是它如此恐怖的原因所在……而且很难辨认。”

En: No offence, officer, but I'm not much of a *pedagogue* -- I don't know what I would've done if Lena hadn't persuaded me to go back to field research. You should ask her, if you want interesting stories.
Zh: “无意冒犯，警官，但是我也不是个好*老师*——如果莉娜没有说服我回归实地调研，我也不知道自己会做什么。如果你想听什么趣味故事的话，最好去找她。”

En: Just tell me about a cool cryptid. Any cryptid.
Zh: “那就跟我讲个酷炫的神秘动物。随便哪个都可以。”

En: I'm not completely sure about this *dread moose*...
Zh: “我对这个*恐怖驼鹿*不是很肯定……”

En: The bodies found in the forest are just one piece of physical evidence. There's more...
Zh: “森林里发现的尸体只是物证的一部分。还有更多……”

En: What's the other evidence?
Zh: “还有什么别的证据？”

En: I think I've heard enough about the dread moose. Let's pick another one.
Zh: “我觉得我已经听够恐怖驼鹿的故事了。换一个吧。”

En: Are there any reliable eyewitness accounts of a moose killing other animals for food? (Conclude.)
Zh: “还有其他可靠的目击者能证实驼鹿杀死其他动物当做食物吗？”（总结。）

En: The recent surge in the moose population. As hidden carnivores, the dread moose are effectively removing competition for both themselves and their evolutionary cousins.
Zh: “最近驼鹿数量飙升。作为一种隐蔽的肉食动物，恐怖驼鹿正在有效地消除同类以及它们的进化表亲之间的竞争。”

En: One slaughterhouse at the outskirts of the woods in Vaasa reported that its staff kept seeing moose in the distance.
Zh: “瓦萨城森林郊外有一个屠宰场，他们报告说自己的员工经常在远处看见驼鹿。”

En: The moose would just stare at the building, as though they were waiting for something. Its eyes bloodshot and full of *cruelty*.
Zh: “这只驼鹿会盯着建筑物看，就好像在等待着什么。它的眼睛布满血丝，而且溢满*残酷*。”

En: It makes sense that such a majestic animal with natural weapons -- antlers -- would come to rule the forest. The only strange thing is that it took so long.
Zh: “像这种拥有天然武器——鹿角——的雄壮动物，征服整片森林是合情合理的。唯一奇怪的事情是，居然用了这么长的时间。”

En: But why would it need to hide its carnivorous nature?
Zh: “但它为什么需要隐藏自己的食肉天性呢？”

En: That does sound suspicious.
Zh: “确实听起来非常可疑。”

En: I'm not sure I'm buying it.
Zh: “我不确定自己到底信不信。”

En: The carnivorous moose are a very young species, the result of a genetic mutation that fared well in the process of natural selection.
Zh: “食肉驼鹿是一种非常年轻的物种，是在自然选择过程中表现良好的基因突变的结果。”

En: Moose are already being hunted for sport. Can you imagine what would happen if they came to be viewed as predators?
Zh: “狩猎驼鹿已经被当成了一项运动。如果它们被视为捕食者，你能想象会发生什么吗？”

En: What exactly is the relationship between carnivorous moose and regular moose?
Zh: “食肉驼鹿和普通驼鹿之间到底是什么关系？”

En: He sees in the dread moose something perversely beautiful and just.
Zh: 他在恐怖驼鹿身上看到了某种异常美丽又公正的东西。

En: The man sees you approaching and turns away...
Zh: 那个男人看到你走近后，转过身去……

En: Officers...
Zh: “警官们……”

En: Officer...
Mr. Villedrouin... The lieutenant nods.
Zh: “维尔德鲁安先生……”警督点点头。

En: Mr. Villedrouin...
Zh: “维尔德鲁安先生……”

En: I've been looking all over for you.
Zh: “我正到处找你呢。”

En: Look what the tide dragged in.
Zh: “这可真是稀客啊。”

En: I must admit. I did not take you for a sightseer. What do you want now?
Zh: “说实话，我可没把你当观光客。你想要什么？”

En: Is that so? Have you come to *enforce* an arrest warrant?
Zh: “是吗？你是来*执行*逮捕令的吗？”

En: Is that an RCM *term of art* for the *wrongfully accused*?
Zh: “这就是RCM对*错误指控*的专门术语吗？”

En: Listen, about what happened earlier...
Zh: “是这样，关于之前的事情……”

En: How do you expect to assign la responsabilité if you won't apologise for your own mistakes?
Zh: 如果你不为自己的错误道歉，还怎么能分配职责呢？

En: Sorry, bad joke. I wanted to talk about what happened earlier.
Zh: “抱歉，开了个不好的玩笑。我想谈谈之前的事情。”

En: Let's get right to it. I have something to say.
Zh: “我就开门见山了。我有事要说。”

En: Yes? He turns to face you.
Zh: “嗯？”他向你转过身。

En: And what is that, officer? He turns to face you.
Zh: “怎么了，警官？”他向你转过身。

En: I'm listening. He turns to face you.
Zh: “我在听。”他向你转过身。

En: It seems we had a little 'misunderstanding.'
Zh: “看来我们有一点小小的‘误会’啊。”

En: I would like to sincerely apologise for my behaviour. It was completely inappropriate, and I accept full responsibility.
Zh: “我真诚地为自己的所作所为道歉。这样很不合适，我愿意承担所有责任。”

En: I'm still pretty sure you were in the wrong, but I'm willing to forgive you.
Zh: “我还是觉得错的肯定是你，但我愿意原谅你。”

En: Unbelievable. I have never seen the nerve before...
Zh: “难以置信。我还没见过脸皮这么厚的人……”

En: Especially from a common officer of the Revachol Citizens Militia.
Zh: “尤其还是从一个瑞瓦肖公民武装的普通警官身上。”

En: Does this behaviour represent the RCM's *official stance* toward its Coalition partners? He turns to the lieutenant.
Zh: “这种行为代表了RCM对联盟伙伴的*官方立场*吗？”他向警督转过身。

En: That is certainly a *euphemistic* way to describe what I recall...
Zh: “以我记得的事情来说，这可真是相当*委婉*的说法啊……”

En: The man considers your words a moment, then nods slowly...
Zh: 那个人思考着你的话语，然后慢慢点了点头……

En: I accept your apology, officer, out of my *profound* respect for the RCM, and the importance of our continuing partnership.
Zh: “我接受你的道歉，警官，这是源于我对RCM的*充分*尊重，以及为了今后继续合作。”

En: Now, what brings you down to the coast?
Zh: “好了，你到海岸来干什么？”

En: And now, this conversation is over. Good day, *officer*.
Zh: “行吧，那没什么可说的了。日安，*警官*。”

En: Perhaps he does, perhaps he does not. In any case, this conversation is over. Good day, officers.
Zh: “也许他是，也许不是。总之没什么可说的了。日安，警官们。”

En: With that, he turns his back to you once more.
Zh: 说完，他再一次朝你背过身。

En: You aren't going to get out of this with evasion.
Zh: 靠借口是躲不掉的。

En: Do you hear that contempt in his voice? He's always considered himself *above* you.
Zh: 你听出他话里的不屑了吗？他总觉得自己地位比你*更高*。

En: Do you hear that contempt in his voice? He's always considered himself *above* you.
Zh: 你听出他话里的不屑了吗？他总觉得自己地位比你*更高*。

En: I must apologise on my partner's behalf, again. Please believe me when I say that he does not speak for the RCM in *any* capacity.
Zh: “请容我再次为搭档的行为道歉。请相信我，他*完全*没有资格代表RCM。”

En: The lieutenant stares at the man's back, then steps away. He makes sure no one else can hear him as he turns to face you...
Zh: 警督盯着他的后背，然后走开了。确保没人能听见后，他朝你转过身……

En: I don't understand why you have it in for this man. He's never been anything but gracious and helpful to our cause. Why do you feel the need to insult him?
Zh: “我不明白你为什么和他过不去。他对我们的工作既热情又很有帮助。你怎么就非要说他两句坏话？”

En: I'm sorry, Kim. I just can't help it.
Zh: “抱歉，金。我就是忍不住。”

En: It feels like he holds us in contempt, like he thinks he's better than we are.
Zh: “感觉他好像看不起我们，觉得自己更有本事。”

En: I just don't like his face, okay?
Zh: “我就是看他的脸不爽，行吗？”

En: Good god, couldn't you *try* at least?
Zh: “我的天，你就不能稍微*试着*忍一忍吗？”

En: Of course he does, he's from the Moralintern. But that doesn't mean *we* don't have standards to maintain...
Zh: “他当然会这样，毕竟他可是国际道德伦理委员会的。可这不代表*我们*没有自己的原则啊……”

En: Good god, couldn't you *pretend* at least?
Zh: “我的天，你就不能稍微*装*一下吗？”

En: I'm not even sure why I'm bothering. This is *your* side task, after all...
Zh: “我都不知道自己为什么操这个心。说到底，这只是*你的*支线任务啊……”

En: Now, let's go. You've done enough damage here.
Zh: “好了，我们走吧。你在这惹够多乱子了。”

En: Ah, super, here you are again, officer. How can I help?
Zh: “啊，真不错，你又来了，警官。有什么需要帮忙的吗？”

En: As you approach, the man turns and greets you with a polite wave.
Zh: 那个男人看你走近后，转过身礼貌地向你挥挥手。

En: Ah, super, it's the officer! I was not expecting to run into you again, but things have a funny way of turning out, no?
Zh: “啊，真不错，警官来了！没想到还会再见到你，但事情总是出人意料嘛，对不对？”

En: What brings you down to the scenic Martinaise coast?
Zh: “什么风把你吹到美丽的马丁内斯海岸了？”

En: He appears completely at ease, like a common holidaymaker.
Zh: 他看起来悠闲自在，就像普通的度假者一样。

En: I need your help forming a committee. I'm trying to assign *la responsabilité*.
Zh: “我需要你帮我成立一个委员会。我正打算分配*职责*。”

En: Thank you for your time. [Leave.]
Zh: “感谢你的宝贵时间。”[离开。]

En: Actually, I was wondering what *you're* doing out here.
Zh: “其实，我是想知道*你*在这里做什么。”

En: My friend, if it's *la responsabilité* you're after, I have good news for you: There's no need to form such a committee, because it already exists!
Zh: “朋友，如果你想找我是为了什么*职责*，那我有个好消息：没必要成立什么委员会，因为已经有过了！”

En: God, of course it does. These Moralintern types...
Zh: 嗨，果然是这样。这些国际道德伦理委员会的家伙……

En: The Comité de Responsabilité de Revachol. It acts as a sort-of clearinghouse for Coalition activities. To put it simply, they are the ultimate arbiters of la responsabilité in this part of the world.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖职责委员会，就跟联盟里的清算中心差不多作用。简单来说，他们就是这片地盘上最终决定职责的人……”

En: Yes, this is just the sort of reasonable authority you're looking for!
Zh: 没错，这就是你要找的那种理性权威！

En: I would offer to connect you with the committee myself, but alas, I am not actually *in* Martinaise.
Zh: “我是很想亲自把你介绍给委员会，但很可惜，我不*在*马丁内斯。”

En: What? Where is he, then?
Zh: 什么意思？那他在哪？

En: You *knew* there was something strange about this one! He's an astral projection, a bureaucratic phantasm!
Zh: 你*就知道*这人有点奇怪！他是灵魂的投影，是官僚的幻象！

En: He's speaking figuratively. He means he's not in Martinaise in his *official* capacity.
Zh: 他是在打比方。他的意思是自己不是以*官方*身份待在马丁内斯的。

En: What are you talking about? I'm looking at you right now!
Zh: “你在说什么呢？你这不是就在我眼前吗！”

En: (Attempt to stick your hand through his astral form.)
Zh: （试图用手穿过他的幻象。）

En: I got it. You're only here on *personal* business.
Zh: “我明白了。你是因为*私人*事务来这的。”

En: So... where *are* you, then?
Zh: “所以……你*在*哪？”

En: Of course, we are speaking informally, but *officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “当然了，我们现在是非正式谈话，但从*官方*角度来说，我还在德尔塔，筹备着即将召开的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: Précisément. *Officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “正是。从*官方*角度来说，我还在德尔塔，筹备着即将召开的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: Why, *officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives. I'm only in Martinaise in an unofficial capacity...
Zh: “怎么，从*官方*角度来说，我还在德尔塔，筹备着即将召开的燃油衍生品会议。我不是以官方身份待在马丁内斯的……”

En: Mais que faites-vous, officer? Your hand bumps up against the man's solid if somewhat fleshy chest.
Zh: “你怎么回事，警官？”你的手碰到了他坚实肥厚的胸部。

En: That's why it would be extremely irregular, or potentially even *inappropriate* for me to intercede with the Committee concerning a district I'm not officially in.
Zh: “所以从官方角度来说，我并不在这里，如果我向这里的地区委员会说好话，那就很不合规，甚至可以说很*不妥当*。”

En: Yes, I was wondering the same thing. What are you doing?
Zh: “是啊，我也奇怪。你怎么回事？”

En: He said he wasn't really here. I had to make sure he wasn't an astral projection.
Zh: “他说他不在这里，所以我得确认下他不是幻象。”

En: You said you weren't really here. I had to make sure you weren't an astral projection.
Zh: “你说你不在这里，所以我得确认下你不是幻象。”

En: Nothing, just lost my balance a moment.
Zh: “没事，只不过稍微失了下智。”

En: No, no, don't be silly. We're simply speaking in an informal capacity. *Officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “不不，可别犯傻了。我们只不过说话通俗了一点。从官方角度来说，我还在德尔塔，筹备着即将召开的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: Perhaps your blood sugar is a bit low? As I was saying, *officially* speaking I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “你是不是有点低血糖？我刚才的意思是，从官方角度来说，我还在德尔塔，筹备着即将召开的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: But this is an urgent matter. I *need* to get in touch with the Committee.
Zh: “但这是紧急事务。我*必须*和委员会取得联系。”

En: How am I supposed to contact the Committee then?
Zh: “那我要怎么联系委员会？”

En: Are you saying I should just give up?
Zh: “那你是觉得我应该放弃了？”

En: Yes, you've made your *persistence* quite clear. Under normal circumstances I would have to insist you go through the regular channels.
Zh: “是的，你的*来意*已经相当清楚了。通常情况下来说，我会建议你走正常渠道……”

En: The man frowns. "Well, under ordinary circumstances, the Committee contacts *you*."
Zh: 他皱起眉头。“这个，通常情况下来说，是委员会联系*你*……”

En: I don't mean to discourage you, of course, just to say that it won't be *easy* to reach the Committee.
Zh: “当然了，不是我想给你泼冷水，但想联系委员会真的不太*容易*……”

En: No, no. You can't let them give you the runaround.
Zh: 不不，可不能被他们给搪塞过去了。

En: In other words, the Committee only knows what it *wants* to know.
Zh: 换句话说，委员会只知道它*想*知道的事。

En: Not exactly a model of accessible and accountable governance...
Zh: 看来并不完全是一个公开透明的政府啊……

En: But if you have information of a vital interest, they might be willing to entertain an exception. In which case, I would advise you to contact them via Coalition Warship Archer.
Zh: “不过，如果你有事关重大利益的情报，或许他们也会网开一面。那样的话，我建议你通过联盟国战舰弓箭手号与他们联系。”

En: Wait, what's Coalition Warship Archer?
Zh: “等等，联盟战舰弓箭手号是什么？”

En: Why do I need to go through Archer to speak to the Committee?
Zh: “为什么我要通过弓箭手号和委员会联系？”

En: So how does one go about contacting a Coalition warship? (Proceed.)
Zh: “所以联盟国战舰又要怎么联系？”（继续。）

En: Officer? You mean to say you haven't noticed the giant Coalition aerostatic peacefully patrolling the skies over Revachol?
Zh: “警官？你是说从没注意过在瑞瓦肖上空平静巡逻的巨大联盟静风舰吗？”

En: You follow the man's gaze out toward the west. There, hanging some distance above the horison, you can just make out a strange silhouette...
Zh: 你顺着他的目光向西看去。勉强能看见地平线上方高悬着一个奇怪的轮廓……

En: You follow the man's gaze out toward the west. There, amidst the low-hanging stars, you can just make out signs of motion...
Zh: 你顺着他的目光向西看去。勉强能看见低垂的星空中有什么东西正在运动……

En: It looks, to your eye, like some kind of long, metallic fish, suspended beneath a number of ceiling fans...
Zh: 在你看来，那东西就像是一条悬挂在吊扇下面的金属鱼……

En: You look around, but you don't see anything unusual. Then again, your eyes aren't the sharpest...
Zh: 你环顾四周，但并没有见到什么不寻常的东西。不过话说回来，你的眼睛也不算特别好……

En: It's a set of position lights moving in unison, like a restless constellation...
Zh: 一组灯光以固定的形状移动着，仿佛一个躁动的星座……

En: You can't see anything. But then, it's dark, and your eyes aren't the sharpest...
Zh: 你什么也没看到。不过话说回来，天这么黑，你的眼睛也不算特别好……

En: Not just any fish, it moves like some ancient bottom-feeder gliding over the ocean floor in search of prey...
Zh: 并不像一般的鱼，而像是某种在海床上滑行的远古底层动物一样移动，寻找着猎物。

En: Don't let the paintjob fool you, it's a goddamn killing machine, and there's nothing you can do to stop it!
Zh: 别被外表给骗了，这他妈可是个杀人机器，而你完全没办法阻止它！

En: It's about four-and-a-half kilometres from your position, you estimate.
Zh: 你估计那东西距离你大概有4.5公里这样。

En: No, it's more like a creature from the abyssopelagic zone, a great dark bioluminescent fish...
Zh: 不，它更像是来自深海的生物，一条发着幽光的巨大黑鱼……

En: Don't let the shiny lights fool you, it's a goddamn killing machine, and there's nothing you can do to stop it!
Zh: 别被善良的灯光给骗了，这他妈可是个杀人机器，而你完全没办法阻止它！

En: Oh, right. The *Archer*. I thought you said... something else.
Zh: “哦，是啊。*弓箭手号*。我还以为你说的是……别的东西。”

En: I don't think I've ever noticed it before.
Zh: “我以前还从没注意过。”

En: Ah, well, I'm glad to hear that at least. It would really be *quite unusual* for you to have never noticed it before...
Zh: “啊，好吧，至少我还是挺高兴听到这消息的。你要是之前没注意到，那才*真是奇怪*……”

En: Interesting. In a sense, that's the very idea. The Coalition doesn't want the people of Revachol to feel that they are under a *military* occupation. That is why the Archer is always careful to maintain a constant, discreet distance.
Zh: “有意思。从某种程度上说，要的就是这个效果。联盟不希望瑞瓦肖的人民感觉自己生活在*军事*统治下。所以弓箭手号才会一直注意保持谨慎的距离……”

En: It's amazing. An inspiring symbol.
Zh: “很厉害。非常激励人心的象征。”

En: It looks like a fish.
Zh: “看起来像条鱼。”

En: It looks like a killing machine.
Zh: “看起来像台杀人机器。”

En: It looks like it's about four-and-a-half kilometres away.
Zh: “感觉差不多离这儿有4.5公里远。”

En: Huh. Can't believe I never noticed it before.
Zh: “哈。我之前居然从来没注意过。”

En: Isn't it? A triumph of both technical ingenuity and international cooperation. I, for one, feel much safer knowing it's up there, watching all of us.
Zh: “对吧？这是技术创新与国际合作的成果。就我自己来说，知道它在上空看着我们，会让我感觉安全多了。”

En: Fascinating. I always thought it was more like a dragonfly. But isn't it interesting how humanity always finds inspiration in nature?
Zh: “真不错。我一直感觉更像是一只蜻蜓。不过，人类总是能从大自然中找到灵感，很有意思对吧？”

En: Well, of course it *is* a heavily armed and armoured warship with a full complement. But I assure you its purpose in Revachol is to *keep* the peace, not disturb it.
Zh: “嗯，它*确实*是艘全副武装、船坚炮利的战舰。但我向你保证，它在瑞瓦肖的目的是为了*维护*和平，而不是打破和平……”

En: That is part of the idea, of course. The Coalition doesn't want the people of Revachol to feel that they are under a *military* occupation. That is why the Archer is always careful to maintain a constant, discreet distance.
Zh: “当然了，这也是一部分目的。联盟不希望瑞瓦肖的人民感觉自己生活在*军事*统治下。所以弓箭手号才会一直注意保持谨慎的距离……”

En: You see, in addition to being an airborne artillery platform, Coalition Warship Archer is also the linchpin of the Coalition's surveillance and communications infrastructure in Revachol.
Zh: “是这样，除了充当空中火力平台以外，联盟国战舰弓箭手号也是联盟对瑞瓦肖进行监视以及通信基建的关键一环。”

En: And listening! It has the most objective vantage point in the entire city. Not to mention a vast array of radio, photographic, and meteorological monitoring instruments.
Zh: “而且听好了！它能从最有利的角度俯瞰整座城市。除此之外，还搭载了大量无线电、摄像与气象监测设备……”

En: I find it a great comfort to know there are benevolent powers watching over all, in strict accordance with the Wayfarer Act and the Bristelmüne Conventions.
Zh: “知道友善的力量在照看着一切，并且严格遵守着旅人法案与布里斯特尔姆公约，让我倍感欣慰。”

En: Hold on, it's *watching* us, too?
Zh: “等等，也就是说它也*正在监视*我们？”

En: Hmmm, this is quite the problem, very tricky. Of course, the Archer has orders to fire on any unidentified aerostatics that might approach it, so it might be safer to get in touch *from the ground*...
Zh: “嗯，这也是个问题，很麻烦的问题。当然了，弓箭手号有权向任何接近它的不明飞行物开火，所以最好还是*从地面*联系比较安全……”

En: But in that case, you would require a radio transmitter capable of broadcasting on Coalition frequencies. And that kind of technology naturally isn't typically available for non-Coalition use. Yes, it *is* a bit of a conundrum.
Zh: “但这样的话，你需要一台能在联盟频率上传输信号的无线电发射器。而这种技术通常来说不会用于非联盟的用途……没错，*确实*是个难题……”

En: I suppose there might be some way to *circuit-bend* your way onto those frequencies, but you'd have to be one of those *techno-tinkerers* to do it.
Zh: “我觉得可能有某种办法通过*电路创意改装*进入那些频率，但你得找个*技术工匠*才能把这活儿干了。”

En: Do you know any of these tinkerers?
Zh: “你认识那些工匠吗？”

En: Is that... legal?
Zh: “这……合法吗？”

En: So what am I supposed to do?
Zh: “所以我该做什么？”

En: Unfortunately not, no, I don't have the pleasure. I am merely a representative of the Coalition Government, not very well-versed in technology. But you...
Zh: “不好意思，不认识，我没那个荣幸。我只不过是联盟政府的代表，对技术方面不太精通。不过你嘛……”

En: Unauthorised circuit-bending is a very serious matter with grave security implications, but as of the moment there is no specific law *forbidding* it, in Revachol at least...
Zh: “未经授权的电路改装是十分严重的事情，可能造成重大安全隐患，但至少在瑞瓦肖，面前还没有明确的法律*禁止*这种行为……”

En: That, my friend, is your responsabilité. I cannot take it from you, nor can you delegate it to someone else...
Zh: “朋友，那就是你的指责了。我不能替你分担，你也不能推给别人……”

En: Always my pleasure to be of assistance to the RCM. Godspeed, and if we don't meet again, bonne chance!
Zh: “我随时乐意帮助RCM。祝你成功，如果以后没机会再见的话，那就祝你好运！”

En: Already leaving? He shakes his head merrily. "À tout à l'heure, officer!"
Zh: “这就要走了？”他愉悦地摇摇头。“回见，警官！”

En: Mm, well that's très simple. I was visiting the fishing village just north of here. They have applied for a series of micro-loans to revitalise the old market, and well, I wished to see the situation *first-hand*...
Zh: “哦，这很简单，我当时去北边的渔村看了看，他们申请了一系列小额贷款，好振兴旧市场。我真希望能*亲眼*见证那一天……”

En: And then, well, I had some extra time on my hands, so I decided to stroll down here. It's quite peaceful, isn't it?
Zh: “所以我有了一些空余时间，于是决定来这里散散步。这里挺平静的，对吧？”

En: (Look around.) "Yeah, it's lovely."
Zh: （环顾四周。）“没错，很棒。”

En: It's not quite my scene.
Zh: “不太是我喜欢的类型。”

En: You do know there was a mass execution right over there?
Zh: “你知道那里曾经有过一场大屠杀吧？”

En: Did you see all the bullet holes in that wall over there?
Zh: “你看到那边墙上的弹孔了吗？”

En: The man takes a deep breath. "Yes, there's nothing like a stroll along the coast to lift one's spirits."
Zh: 他深吸一口气。“是啊，没什么比在海边散散步更能让人打起精神了。”

En: Well, you know what they say, 'In matters of taste, there can be no dispute'! For my part, I find it quite invigorating.
Zh: “嗯，俗话说得好，‘品位无高下’！至少我是觉得挺能振奋精神的。”

En: Actually, there was something *else* I wanted to ask you about.
Zh: “其实，我*还有*件事想问问你。”

En: Ah, bon? I'm all ears, officer.
Zh: “啊，怎么了？我洗耳恭听，警官。”

En: Really? The man frowns. "*Here*? How can you be sure?"
Zh: “真的？”那人皱起眉头。“*这里*？你确定吗？”

En: It's sort of a gift I have. I can reconstruct things that happened in the past. High-end detective stuff.
Zh: “算是我的某种天赋吧。我可以重现过去发生的事情。高级警探的本事。”

En: For one, that wall over there is *covered* in bullet holes.
Zh: “比方说，这里的墙上*布满*了弹孔。”

En: Ah, a very useful gift for a police detective, I'm sure. Fascinating, *how much history* is contained right here, if only one knows where to look for it...
Zh: “啊，对警探来说，这天赋可真是方便啊。太厉害了，只要去那里看看，就知道那地方包含了*多少历史*……”

En: Ah, is that what those pock marks are? I might've guessed. Fascinating, *how much history* is contained right here, if only one knows where to look for it...
Zh: “啊，原来这些斑点是这么来的？我大概也猜到了。太厉害了，只要去那里看看，就知道那地方包含了*多少历史*……”

En: Are you just going to let him get away with these generalities and euphemisms?
Zh: 你打算让他就这样轻描淡写地一笔带过吗？

En: I'm sure the communards who were killed here take a different view.
Zh: 在这里被杀的公社社员肯定不这么觉得。

En: Ugly work, but at least someone had the balls to do what had to be done.
Zh: “肮脏的工作，但至少有人有勇气做这些该做的事情。”

En: Really, it's the property damage that most offends me.
Zh: “是啊，最让我生气的还是财产损失。”

En: Yes, the loss of human life was truly catastrophic... This is why the Coalition is so focused on preventing another war. Our peacekeeping mission is a testament to this...
Zh: “是啊，失去的生命实在是太可怕了……所以联盟才会致力于阻止战争再次发生。我们的维和任务就是最好的力证……”

En: Thankfully, the region is becoming more and more stable. I'm confident that atrocities like this belong in the past. The future is... he snaps his fingers, "*tranquilou bilou!*"
Zh: “好在这片地区已经越来越稳定了。我相信这样的暴行已经成为了历史。未来是……”他打了个响指。“*tranquilou bilou！*”

En: *Tranquilou bilou*? Let's not get into this, you can't argue with someone who uses words like 'tranquilou bilou'.
Zh: *tranquilou bilou*？还是别说这些了，你跟用“tranquilou bilou”这种词的人没什么可争的。

En: A rather *brutal* perspective on history. He frowns slightly. "I personally take the more nuanced view that it was regrettably harsh, but perhaps necessary to end the war..."
Zh: “这是历史*残忍*的一面。”他微微皱眉。“我个人的意见略有不同。虽然这话不太好听，但或许必须要结束战争……”

En: Now, now. Human life is *always* more important than private property...
Zh: “行了行了。生命*永远*比个人财产重要……”

En: Though, I do hope whoever was responsible at least compensated the building's owner appropriately.
Zh: “不过，我希望为此负责的人至少能给房主们一些补偿。”

En: You're right. It's so complex and fascinating I can't even *begin* to think critically about it.
Zh: “你说得对。这件事如此复杂、如此迷人，让我甚至没法*试着*从批判性的角度去思考。”

En: Well said. Let us just be grateful that *we* don't live in such fascinating times.
Zh: “说得对。庆幸*我们*生在如此迷人的时代吧。”

En: You feel like there's *something* you could say here, if only you really knew what was going on...
Zh: 你感觉自己可以说些*什么*，但首先得知道这是怎么一回事……

En: You feel like there's *something* you could say here, if only you really knew what was going on...
Zh: 你感觉自己可以说些*什么*，但首先得知道这是怎么一回事……

En: Ah, is that what those pock marks are? I might've guessed. Fascinating, *how much history* is contained right here, if only one knows where to look for it...
Zh: “啊，原来这些斑点是这么来的？我大概也猜到了。太厉害了，只要去那里看看，就知道那地方包含了*多少历史*……”

En: Hmmm, perhaps your young friends in the *tent* might be of assistance?
Zh: “嗯，也许你在*帐篷*里的年轻朋友们可以帮帮忙？”

En: This sounds like something that programmer you met in the church might help with...
Zh: 感觉你在教堂里遇见的那个程序员可以帮到忙？

En: Let me just say that I have *complete confidence* in the RCM. I'm sure you'll figure something out.
Zh: “我对RCM可是有着*充分的信任*，我相信你一定会有办法的。”

En: You *will* figure it out. Something tells you the person you're looking for is already on this coast...
Zh: 你*一定*会有办法的。你总感觉要找的这个人就在海岸……

En: Perhaps your new friends in the tent might be able to help you?
Zh: 或许你在帐篷里认识的新朋友可以帮到你？

En: Hmmm, sounds like something that programmer we met in the church might be able to help with...
Zh: “嗯，感觉你在教堂里遇见的那个程序员可以帮到忙……”

En: Oh, good: 'Techno-tinkerers.' Just what this *side task* was missing...
Zh: “啊，真好：‘技术工匠’。正好是漏掉的*日常杂事*……”

En: The awkward silence that follows is yours to fill.
Zh: 接下来的尴尬沉默正等着你去化解。

En: This isn't the end. There may yet be someone along this coast who can direct you where you need to go...
Zh: 还没结束。这片海岸或许还有人能为你指点迷津……

En: This isn't the end. There may yet be someone along this coast who can direct you where you need to go...
Zh: 还没结束。这片海岸或许还有人能为你指点迷津……

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: What's certain is that we must do everything to prevent such tragic events from ever happening again. The answers lie in *debates*, not in violence.
Zh: “毫无疑问的是，我们必须尽一切可能防止这种悲剧再次上演。答案是*讨论*出来的，而不是争斗出来的。”

En: The Bristelmüne Conventions are a series of international agreements governing the treatment of military personnel and civilians in theatres of war as well as occupied territories. In practice, they are more talked about than observed.
Zh: 布里斯特尔姆公约是有关在战争期间或是被占领期间军事人员与平民待遇的国际约定。谈论它的人很多，但实际遵守的没多少。

En: The sea air is so invigorating, don't you agree?
Zh: “大海真是生机勃勃啊，你说对吗？”

En: Lots of opportunities for revitalisation here.
Zh: “这里有许多复兴的机会。”

En: Ah, look! A seagull.
Zh: “啊，快看！是海鸥！”

En: This whole area is a strong candidate for a micro-loan.
Zh: “整片区域都是很好的小额贷款候选。”

En: First responsabilité, then stabilité, then prospérité!
Zh: “首先是职责，其次是稳定，然后是繁荣！”

En: Even high-ranking bureaucrats are entitled to a holiday.
Zh: “就算是高级官员，也是有权利休假的。”
